# Thread: Nyseides: not just another pretty constellation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyseides-not-just-another-pretty-constellation.7747/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nyseides — 2013-07-29T17:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyseides-not-just-another-pretty-constellation.7747/post-91100>

Hi guys. Its my pleasure to write with great passion about finding and trying to figure out how to do this "Jack Kruse Thing." Its not the beginning of my transformation, not by a long shot. I've been in the process of getting to optimal for years, I guess going back to getting Sober in AA 22 years ago. That was a good first step. I've made a lot of changes in my world since then. I've come a long way baby.

In school I studied Biochem and nutrition in university, and worked in dietetics, ugh, in hosptial. I also worked at the Faculty of Medicine at U of Toronto as a research assistant. Reading medical journals was my idea of a good time. But as a girl who had autoimmune problems that conventional medicine made sicker, I was always seeking solutions outside the box. I became a Registered Holistic Nutritionist in 98 in Toronto and joined the ranks of the Vegetarians and got fatter.

I've been low carb for a long time now, but suffer depression every time I get 6 months in. The last time that happened is when I discovered Jack Kruse who seemed to say I might not have that problem in the presence of enough Iodine, so I got tested for that, and viola, I am Iodine deficient and prolly bromine-toxic.

For months I dabbled in the Leptin Reset protocol but found it hard to stop binge eating carbs. I restarted for the umpteenth time about 10 weeks ago, and I nailed it. No binges. The trick was to eat lots of coconut oil in my coffee, and eat enough, actually eat 50 g, or more, of protein. It will kill your hunger. And eating that early will help your clock.

I've always thought of myself as a kid who had no internal clock. I mean, I had insomnia my whole life, was necessarily a "night owL" as are many survivors of child abuse. But even before that, I've read that babies adopt mama's clock, and as an infant reliquinshed for adoption by 15 year old living in a home for wayward girls, 1964, mom wasn't even allowed to touch me in the incubator as the story goes. In my old "Parent-Finders" group, before I met my birth mom, we used to say that babies who had no mom to go to, had to go right to God to survive. Its how I understand surviving that.

I have since discovered I do have an internal clock, works just fine, lets me fall asleep by 10 without meds lol, wakes me up before my alarm goes off at 530 every morning. I take my T3, go back to bed till 7 and get up, go for a walk to get sunshine in the retinas. Eat BAB within half hour. Even if I don't improve anything else, the ability to have a routine like this has improved my life beyond measure. Made me feel like I qualified for my membership card, human race, vs some subhuman night crawling thing.

I'm brand new to posting on the forums, but have been lurking for months. I look forward to helping you if I can be of service. My specialities include but aren't limited to research, advocacy, translating medical shit to the layman, motivating others, being a great floor model (i mean role model lol), and public speaking.

I just found a great job I want. Do you think they'd let me use Kruse protocols? Take a look. Be well.

http://www.thunderbay.ca/Assets/City+Government/Employment/docs/2013/Health+$!26+Wellness+Coach+(CMGR-12-13).pdf

### fitness@home — 2013-07-29T22:37:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyseides-not-just-another-pretty-constellation.7747/post-91130>

Nyseides, welcome to the world of journals. I look forward to reading yours!

### caroline — 2013-07-30T03:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyseides-not-just-another-pretty-constellation.7747/post-91140>

Thankyou for posting your story - this will be so helpful to so many people. Most of us didn't have a perfect childhood or have a perfect life and we can and do survive and - dare I say - thrive...

I hope that we hear lots more from you.....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-30T04:01:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyseides-not-just-another-pretty-constellation.7747/post-91146>

perfect is a myth......the road to optimal is paved with failures and learning from them

# Thread: Made Goal Weight - Set New Goal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/made-goal-weight-set-new-goal.7750/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-07-30T02:02:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/made-goal-weight-set-new-goal.7750/post-91138>

I set a goal to lose 100 lbs 12 years ago. After 10 years, I still had 85 to go.

Since starting the leptin rx in October 2011, I have lost an additional 90 lbs for a grand total of 105.

My experience is that I lose easily when the sun is high in the sky and not so much the rest of the year. I still have a good 6-8 weeks of good sun this year that hopefully will get me down another 15 lbs (200 lbs). If I can reach 199, that will be the lowest weight since I was 19, 45 years ago.

### caroline — 2013-07-30T03:31:28+0300

I seem to do much better in the summer time too.....my weight is much more stable and I sleep much better.....

# Thread: Recommendations on which labs to ask for?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Beverly — 2013-07-30T04:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91153>

Hi everybody,

I have an appt and would like to discuss my previous labs with my dr tomorrow. Lyme has already been suggested as has certain Celiac tests so I will ask for those.

If Dr K or anyone else could hack my labs and recommend any other labs to ask for based on the previous labs I posted last week, I would really appreciate it.

Thanks.

### nicld — 2013-08-03T04:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91405>

How did it go?

Sorry did not have time to reply sooner but here is what I did.

I had my full thyroid done including reverse T3, leptin, female hormones, DHEA, Vitamin d and B and a bunch of others. I would make a list of tests that you want done and hand it to your doctor, that's what I did. I did tell him that I am working on my leptin reset and wanted these tests done to see where I am at and I am in charge of my health. I have heard of so many people whose doctors won't do some tests. One has to take charge and say that it is their health and their money to spend on the tests.

### Beverly — 2013-08-03T23:19:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91450>

Hi,

Thanks for asking. I actually had to postpone it until Tuesday. Great advice, I will make a list of the tests I would like. The derm that ran all my recent labwork is one of those dr's that only does tests when I specifically ask for them. He thought my last round of labs were "normal" so I am pretty sure he won't be open to any additional testing.

So I am seeing a different dr at a clinic that thinks outside the box. He thinks I am having a sensitivity to gluten. Good for you for being your own advocate. I am putting my health as priority as well and I will let you know how my appt goes on Tuesday. I will be asking for lyme testing as well as discussing my high cholesterol results. Let me know how your blood work turns out.

### nicld — 2013-08-04T04:44:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91466>

Just be firm in asking for what you want. I am so glad that I read a bunch of stuff from Dr. Kruse so I had some knowledge of what specific tests that I wanted and more importantly why. Not sure why doctors are so hesitant on allowing patients to gather information about what is going on in their body. I got back a bunch of my results and shows that I am hormonally off. Now the fun of trying to find a doctor that can help while I keep doing the LR and CT.

Good Luck

Nicole

### caroline — 2013-08-04T08:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91473>

> [Beverly said:](/goto/post?id=91450)

> Hi,
>
> Thanks for asking. I actually had to postpone it until Tuesday. Great advice, I will make a list of the tests I would like. The derm that ran all my recent labwork is one of those dr's that only does tests when I specifically ask for them. He thought my last round of labs were "normal" so I am pretty sure he won't be open to any additional testing.
>

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-04T16:24:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91490>

THe following are the labs I always ask for

Berkley or other Lipid panel
CBC Test # 005009, CPT Code 85025
Comp metabolic Panel Test # 322000, CPT code 80053
Thyroid:
TSH, Test # 004259, CPT Code 84443
Free T4, Test # 001974, CPT code 84439
Free T3, Test # 010389, CTP Code 84481
RT3, Test # 070104, CPT 84482
Total T4 (Dzugan requested) Test # 001149 CPT Code 84436
Total T3 (Dzugan requested) Test # 002188, CPT code 84480
Progestrone, Test # 004312, CPT Code 84144
Estradiol, Sensitive, Test # 140244, CPT Code 82670
Testosterone Free Direct, & serum with Total Testosterone: Test # 140103, CPT Codes 84402, 84403
Prolactin Test # 004465, CPT code 84146
cortisol Test # 004051, CPT Code 82533
DHEA-S Test # 004020, CPT Code 82627
IGF-1 Test #010636, CPT Code 84305
RBC magnesium Test # 080283, CPT Code 83735
Vitamin D25 Hyrdoxy Test # 081950, CPT code 82306
Serotonin Test# 120204, CPT Code 84260 (requires frozen specimens)
Pregnenolone Test# 140707, CPT Code 84140 (requires Frozen Specimens)

### jib — 2013-08-04T18:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91496>

If I give this list to my doc will she know which lab to correspond the test# and CPT code to? Thanks.
What lab is this info for?

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-04T18:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/recommendations-on-which-labs-to-ask-for.7751/post-91497>

> [jib said:](/goto/post?id=91496)
>
> If I give this list to my doc will she know which lab to correspond the test# and CPT code to? Thanks.
> What lab is this info for?

your doc should. the Test # tells labcorp which test your getting... the CPT code tells them what to charge the ins company...

# Thread: Leptin receptors
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-receptors.7759/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Anna — 2013-07-31T05:12:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-receptors.7759/post-91232>

Hi
Was hoping @JackKruse might be able to answer my question, if not, hopefully someone who is knowledgable maybe able to help.

As we all know, we are not born with leptin receptors but rather, this is passed to us by our mother's breast milk.

My query is this - if we were not breastfed in our early days, how do we acquire leptin hormone receptors?

Thanks
Cheers
Anna

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-31T06:41:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-receptors.7759/post-91233>

not true........we are born with the receptor.......we get leptin to set the receptor from our mother's milk. It is the software that loads the hardware tow work based upon the field it finds itself in........to tell the child how energy is or is not present........to survive.

### Anna — 2013-07-31T09:28:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-receptors.7759/post-91237>

Oh thanks so much @JackKruse for replying - so the receptor that we are born with is in the cellulor level? and the leptin is the hormone that we get from the mother's milk that activates the receptor?
So if I have this correct, how does the receptor get set then if we are bottle fed and not breastfed?
Thanks for taking the time to help me understand this more.
Cheers
Anna

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-31T14:43:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-receptors.7759/post-91247>

it appears that the leptin molecule itself sets the receptor when it arrives in the brain from the mother's milk. The details of the molecular mechanism is not yet known.

### Anna — 2013-08-01T05:47:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-receptors.7759/post-91310>

Cheers, thanks @JackKruse for taking the time to respond - such an interesting discovery re leptin and I will continue to read and learn from any new research that becomes available.

# Thread: tDCS and my Existentialist State of Mind...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tdcs-and-my-existentialist-state-of-mind.7760/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 56
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-31T09:23:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tdcs-and-my-existentialist-state-of-mind.7760/post-91236>

Alright, firstly, I'd like to apologize for creating ANOTHER post. I hope the mods and frequent forum-goers here (and Dr. Kruse) don't believe that I'm just spamming the boards. I'm going to keep up with my latest thread I created after I dig a little deeper and potentially even try out a DIY supplement (probably starting with liposomal vitamin c as it seems rather easy). I just really needed to create this thread. I would love for some input from anyone with direct experience or knowledge on tDCS specifically, and hopefully Dr. Kruse can chime in.

So, awhile back a member posted on the Bulletproof Forum and created a large thread ([http://forum.bulletproofexec.com/index.php?/topic/4644-dear-davelostfalcos-biohacking-extravaganza/](http://forum.bulletproofexec.com/index.php?/topic/4644-dear-davelostfalcos-biohacking-extravaganza/)) and also put me in contact with a member of longecity.com (anti-aging type of community/forum). He showed me a tDCS device that is $40 rather than $400 (or more) which also has the correct specs and good reviews. I asked the member I was put in contact with at longecity and he had astounding results. He has Schizophrenia and has damaged his brain from drug use, and is twenty years old (roughly my age). He said the tDCS unit in 2 weeks has given him better results than ANY anti-depressants/psychotics. He also uses video games to 'quantify' his concentration that he has noted an increase in after tDCS sessions with this unit. The reason I really took his words to heart, is because his situation seems similar to mine. [http://paleohacks.com/questions/175...he-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ZhlbGvAH](http://paleohacks.com/questions/175553/last-question-on-the-topic-of-my-bizarre-symptoms#axzz2ZhlbGvAH) This is a post I made about my problems, and Dr. Kruse in fact commented and recommended I read EMF 2, which I just skimmed and largely delves into EMFs but someone told me he speaks about tDCS as well. The problems I have are entirely from substance use. This is a self-diagnosis, but that's all I need. I accidentally ingested what I believe to be either PCP or embalming fluid. Either way, they are both powerful anticholingerics which have devastating effects. Soon after I started experiencing all my symptoms. The days before and after I had also used large amounts of DXM and MDMA which largely act upon the serotonin receptors of our brain. I believe that I have been suffering from some forms of both HPPD (hallucinogenic post perceptual disorder) and the effects of anticholingeric deliriants which resulted in my anxiety and odd 'states of mind'. I've made great strides in my recovery from diet and stress management alone, but I couldn't pass up this opportunity. I always wanted an EmWave to take my meditation to the next level, but I don't have over $200 to drop on one, but I did have $40 from a paycheck for a tDCS unit.

In addition to this, I recently had more or less of an 'existential breakdown' recently. Oddly enough, in the book the 4HWW, there was a chapter on 'filling the void' after traveling and experiencing a lot of thrills. Before I read that chapter and the poem contained within, I came about a man named Jacques Fresco who got me thinking very abstractly about life and reality. For some reason I started thinking it was all meaningless and I was overwhelmed with anxiety. If life is meaningless, what is the point of living? The only answer I could come up with is Nafs - my fiance (Nafis also means 'precious gem' in Arabic, and she is more than a precious gem to me, I love her dearly). I eventually got over it, but it stuck with me since. See, I've always been a curious person, wondering about the nature of the universe and reality and the meaning of it all, even when I was young (like 8-10yrs old), and that has continued up until now (18 years old), especially since I've been introduced to so much knowledge. Some of my experiences with substances (psychedelics in particular) I would never take away - they were catalysts to an already open minded individual - it was like Neo being detached from the matrix, lol. But many others (including the event at the concert) I wish I could change. I suppose they made me who I'am, though.

Well, I don't know why I got into that long shpiel, but essentially, I would love Dr. Kruse opinion/recommendations on tDCS, as well as anyone knowledgeable in this area. I really purchased it originally to speed learn Polish with my fiance so we can eventually move to Poland and live with her family (which is quite an optimal place to live I believe - some beaches not too farm from the hilly grasslands of populated with small towns and family farms) - but now, I really want to use it or mood, motivation, and all around brain enhancement. I have no clinical diagnoses of any psychiatric/neurological disorder, but as I said before, I theorize I suffer from HPPD, anti-cholingeric effects of deliriants, and have a history of concussions and a back injury (in my c-1 or c-3 area, I can check). I believe tDCS would offer me a great opportunity to achieve this brain enhancement. Keep in mind, I do a lot of research. I have an evernote page with TONS of citation and presentations on tDCS, I just don't know anyone personally who has experience with this technology, and I want to do everything correctly. There are so many different 'montages' that stimulate or inhibit different brain areas, AND on top of that, we all have slightly different brain structuring, so it's hard to be very efficient with this (hence why there is HD tDCS, lol).

Here is the unit I purchased: [http://tdcs-kit.com/](http://tdcs-kit.com/) it apparently is set to the 2ma current used in many studies. The only sketchy part is I purchased the USB version which means I have less 'control' (not that I had much to begin with considering there is no breaker) over the voltage/current density than a battery operated unit - but I'm saving money. I planned on just plugging it into my laptop while on battery mode and using it for small amounts of time to see how I was affected. I'm not quite certain on what areas to stimulate right now, though, considering my current 'problems'. I want to eliminate all of my symptoms and what not of course, but I suppose with out highly precise imaging of my brain that is difficult.

Any information/insights besides, "don't screw with DIY tDCS, you'll kill yourself!!!",?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-07-31T15:37:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tdcs-and-my-existentialist-state-of-mind.7760/post-91259>

Direct DC current into the brain is really the focus of the July webinar and the next blog post really........myelin in the brain is the source of our DC current of regeneration. Myelin main purpose is for mainantence of the neurons we have........it is a form of energy......just as red light laser is. It can help re load a poor semiconductor. I think you are beginning to sense that you are severely lacking energy at a foundational level in you own brain. Become addicted to the things that nature does. It is the only way we can make it through the things we choose to do and remain healthy. Nature is the only ‘straight edge’ when you are the architect of your blueprint for life………We only command nature when we obey her rules. tDCS is a man made way to grab a bit of nature's Rx for health.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-07-31T23:29:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tdcs-and-my-existentialist-state-of-mind.7760/post-91284>

"Become addicted to the things that nature does," so what in your words would those be, then? Or is that for me to find out myself? Is tDCS a good choice for me right now? Because quite frankly I feel as though I really need something that can bring my brain to where it needs to be... I just purchased a bunch of books from Osho, an eastern Indian mystic who I learned of from the strength trainer Elliott Hulse and Paul Chek - I plan on doing a lot of his meditations, especially the 'dynamic meditations' preferably while grounding outside. I just feel as though tDCS could be more or less of a 'godsend' to me if I apply it to the correct areas.

The person who introduced me to this device also has experience with the Vetrolaser (low level laser therapy) and has found some more models that are roughly $40-50 rather than hundreds, just like that tdcs-kit. I'd be interested in that as well. I notice direct and instantaneous (as well as long lasting) effects from grounding when I take barefoot walks or do streches in the field. For instance, I recently went to Chicago with my mom to meet Elliott Hulse (a strength trainer/strongman competitor/philosopher dude that inspires me) but I literally got anxious and butterfly like feelings in my gut from the **buildings** ..... yes the buildings. I couldn't look up towards the sky or the top of the sky scrapers without feeling anxious because of how insignificant I felt. Once I took off my sandals and went barefoot and 'grounded' myself, I could actually look up to the top and was fine. I experienced the effects of grounding right there. The thing is there are so many apartments here with wifi, generators, telephone poles - I don't even think grounding has the effect it _should_ have. So that is why I want to pursue tDCS. I may even use tDCS to 'speed learning' electronics with a Radio Shack electronics tutorial kit and make my own HD tDCS device.

What do you think about my ideas and using tDCS for my 'problems' I posted about on PaleoHacks? I really need everything I can do to aid my brain/psyche. I've come so far, but yet have so far to go...

Between the Breathslim/Frolov Device and the Super Human Encoder, I'd save like $50 so if it is effective (breathing device) I may want to opt for that and save my money for some amber bulbs or just good food. How many times a day do you normally take your CP? Right now I'm trying for 3 - Morning CP, midday, and before bed.

[http://tdcs-kit.com/](http://tdcs-kit.com/) this is the tDCS kit I purchased. If I find something to use as a 'band' and hold the electrodes in place I plan on using it today, if not tomorrow morning (I will purchase something to use as bands if I have to). Thanks for the link ill be sure to check it out

Benefits are really hard for me to single out, as ive started dr.kruses protocols (leptin, adrenal, mito, gut, ct) some weeks after starting with buteyko, so its tricky to figure out what really did the trick (its probably the combination of everything!) but i went from the gutter (felt totally burned out including depression, anxiety, no energy, hormonal problems, brain fog, insomnia etc.) to pretty good overall (not optimal yet) in about 6 months

What i did notice from CP improvements right away (even before the protocols) were immidiate reduction of anxiety/social anxiety, addictive behaviour, mood swings, and insomnia, du to more CO2. My family and friends noticed those changes very quickly too

Concerning the frolov device, it was/is my main tool for breathwork, i was trying to do 2-4 20 minute sessions each day march - july. Still cant really get to grips with doing reduced breathing sessions, for me the frolov simply feels better.

From what ive read in the comments on the normalbreathing website, the rate of CP increase depends on many things. My own was pretty slow, i think it took me 1-1,5 months to get to 20+CP 24/7, while others seem to have succeeded in 1-2 weeks, so its definitly possible. Hope youll keep us updated on your progress!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-30T01:42:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tdcs-and-my-existentialist-state-of-mind.7760/post-94267>

I'm sorry, I never saw your reply Dr. Kruse. Do you think we can recreate this for 'better' water, or should our body just take care of that?

Okay, so, I haven't continued with tDCS as of yet. Only one session.... BUT, I have started stimulating my brain with Red LEDs. I used the protocol from this study in severe depression and anxiety: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2796659/#!po=6.25000](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2796659/#!po=6.25000). I stimulated my forehead for 1 minute in two different areas - F3/4 according to 10/20 EEG brain maps. I have to say, unless it's a coincidence, I have had less anxiety, better mood, more motivation and confidence as of the past two days. More emotional resilience as well. I only did this for 2 MINUTES! The study used 8 minutes of stimulation. As far as I know from my brief reading of some clinical reviews of the literature on transcranial LLLT, the primary mechanism of improvement from this therapy/bio-hack is hormesis. Hence why I started with only 2 minutes of stimulation and haven't done another session (almost a day and a half). Think of it as exercising - you won't go and do bicep curls to failure three days in a row, you must let the adaptation to occur.

A very kind gentleman over some bio-hacker boards literally sent me the LEDs, wall adapters, a book called Life's Ratchet, about order from chaos on the macroscopic and microscopic world, AND he sent me LEF BioPQQ, CoQ10 (ubiquinol) with Shilijat, and another small container of LEF PQQ. There are some studies about these substances ameliorating symptoms of Alzheimer's and other neurdegenerative disorders - Transcranial LLLT does the same. The combination of both should GREATLY enhance the health and energy production of my current mitochondria, cause mitochondrial biogenesis, enhance blood flow to my forehead so to speak, enhance oxygen uptake in the brain, cause dendrite growth, etc. I'm really excited about this.

So, a few questions that I'd LOVE answers to (or just simple input):
(1) What dose should I use from these supplements?

(2) Is there any longevity downsides to greatly increasing mitochondrial output and cellular respiration?

(3) I'm listening to Dave Asprey's free Bulletproof Lifestyle Creative Live webinar and Dr. Terry Wahls is on speaking about mitochondria in neurodegeneration, she thinks that enhancing mitochondria is key to reversing and preventing these problems, as well as enhancing cognitive performance. Make me wonder about the effects of my current protocol, known as TULIP (the ultimate laser protocol)

(4) College has once again started, and I'm beyond a full-time student now... I unfortunately have to take a small hiatus on reading the blogs here, but I plan on doing a weekly re-reading of Hormones 101, Dancing between purity and polarity, and CT-4-7. But, I had a question about Leptin and my brain. Strictly followed the LeptinRx for a few weeks, then became a bit loose on it. Fell off, did it strictly again, and have become loose with it again. I've been thinking about doing it VERY strictly for about 2-4 weeks to get my circadian rhythm back on track (currently waking up at about noon), but, then, considering I'm lean and athletic and eat clean, couldn't cold alone keep me leptin sensitive, and isn't intense exercise a leptin mimetic? I do body by science style lifting, tabata sprints, foundation training, and lots of walking. I created a DIY standing desk as well. It would be MUCH easier if I can fast in the morning rather than eating the BAB everyday because lot's of times the kitchen is filled up in my house or something always happens to get in the way.

(5) Iodine: Started switching from a cycical ketogenic paleo diet to more or less epi-paleo (don't eat enough oysters, raw seafood, or sea veggies, but lot's of fish, especially wild caught frozen salmon and shrimp). Since Iodine helped form our brain, and I have more or less a perceptual disorder, possibly from a lack of acetylcholne and negative psychological feedback loops, would iodine enhance my recovery? I would get this by taking/eating kelp and dulse and similar things, not Lugols.

Oh, by the way, I don't know if it is the \*slight\* increase in CT (10-12 minute cold showers a day + cold room and almost no clothes), or only drinking RO water, but the circles under my eyes are becoming less apparent and my skin is softer. Good signs, no?

Thanks for any input. I suppose I'll make this my "journal" as of now, and continue to update. Thanks again!

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-09-01T22:25:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tdcs-and-my-existentialist-state-of-mind.7760/post-94604>

**TULIP, or, The Ultimate Laser Protocol**

Alright, another quick update. I don't know if this is all placebo or coincidence, or if it is just a transient effect from LLLT that I'll have to do weekly to maintain, but I've been feeling pretty dang good the past 5 days (since my first session of putting a red LED to my forehead and starting TULIP). So far I have noticed a large reduction in anxiety and an increase in self-confidence and motivation, also an optimistic outlook on things as well. Not to mention my fiance and brother have both commented on my seemingly drastic change in aura and self-presentation - I'm buzzing with mental energy. Random memories have came back, I used the ROYGBIV saying (red, orange, yellow, etc.) to help describe the visual light spectrum to my fiance and how red light specifically has such drastic effects on physiology. Thoughts have been flowing so quickly and they just literally sort themselves out without any effort. It's like the pieces to a puzzle being put together by itself, only in my mind. I don't know if this has to do with the LLLT, or TULIP, or the combination.

For anyone interested in the application of transcranial photobiomodulation, I recommend reading this amazing review: [http://pdfcast.org/pdf/suck-it-trebek](http://pdfcast.org/pdf/suck-it-trebek) (you may have to download this and I don't know if that somehow violates anything on these boards - please I'm not spamming, fishing, trying to advertise, etc. thi pdf contains the most up to date literature review by the experts in the area of the transranial applications of red lasers and LED's). This is also an interesting study: [http://dspace.mit.edu/handle/1721.1/58558#files-area](http://dspace.mit.edu/handle/1721.1/58558#files-area).

I would love to understand how the field comes into play here. Since it affects literally everything, I wonder how red lasers/LEDs can affect the field, and I wonder if you are living in an alright field, if LLLT will aid in recovery or performance even more. This is a great guide set up by a fellow bio-hacker I've been in contact with: [http://www.longecity.org/forum/user/11887-lostfalco/](http://www.longecity.org/forum/user/11887-lostfalco/) he is the one who sent me the LED arrays as well as his TULIP stack and the two books be recommends. The books seem absolutely phenomenal and anyone reading Dr. Kruse' blogs must get them. They describe the interconnectedness of the macroscopic and microscopic world in great detail, as well as conservation of energy/energy transfers. It gives a good visual of what is happening with our mitochondria and how transcranial photobiomodulation affects them.

\*\*\*MAJOR POINT\*\*\* I firmly believe Dr. Kruse is onto something with all his work on 'the field' and magnetism and water. I'm not saying that people should just get a red laser and say screw grounding and avoiding artificial light, etc. But, if this is no coincidental few days of strong well-being, or placebo, then this is truly giving me a shot at getting my life in order. I seriously didn't know how I would finish this upcoming semester of school with my HPPD, hence why most of my courses are online (which I regret now), and there was no way I'd be able to attain another job. Now, I feel similar to how I did before I first experienced even minimal HPPD symptoms - only with a mass of knowledge behind me and quite possibly increasingly enhanced mitochondria.

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-09-02T23:44:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tdcs-and-my-existentialist-state-of-mind.7760/post-94703>

Some more information and an update:

I really, REALLY recommend the following two books: The Machines Of Life, by David S. Goodsell; it explains a lot of cellular/molecular biology in thorough but simple terms with lots of colored graphics. It's short and to the point. I've only read the first chapter, but will definitely finish the book this week - after, I plan on going back through some of the foundational blogs, especially regarding mitochondria. The second book is Life's Ratchet: How Molecular Machines Extract Order From Chaos, by Peter M. Hoffman. Another good book on cellular/molecular biology, but a bit more in depth and with a lot of citations. These books seem foundational to understanding some of the biological mechanisms and reactions going on in my current experiments with LLLT/TULIP.

Update:
3rd session with 48 red LED light to my forehead, directly before bed. Did it for only about 1.5 minutes at both regions. Felt normal, in fact slightly relaxed afterwards. Fell asleep quickly and woke up refreshed. Woke up, had some chocolate and tea, took my TULIP stack, and did some short CT. I plan on finishing another chapter or 2 of The Machines of Life today outside in the sun while grounding with intermittent Buteyko Breathing/meditation. Also gonna do some Tabata sprints - I will probably have consumed more carbs today due to the "holiday" and enjoying a BBQ with my family, so I may as well cause a glycogen deficit, lol. Don't worry, though, I'll be eating pretty much epi-paleo: A pound or so of fish with a bunch of guacamole, roasted onions and garlic and tomatoes, cilantro, maybe find a way to incorporate some lime/lemon juice/rind, etc. I feel a little bit off today, but at the same time more motivated and optimistic. Probably the hormetic affect of the LEDs done only about 13hrs ago.

Found some more interesting information on IR LEDs/Lasers for health, recovery, and performance. I'm not going to copy the entire post because it is far too long, but it is interesting and if anyone wants to know more I can post the rest or post a link:

"Copyright 2004 James Clayton Roberts

What is the Euon?

The name, Euon, comes from the prefix "eu" , which means "good or normal" , and "on" from "photon" , a wave-particle of light

The Euon is an infrared (IR) light. It is, in part, a special LED that uses an alloy of aluminum, gallium and arsenic to turn electricity into a specific wavelength of light, which is 880 nanometers (billionths of a meter) in length. The LED employs three "chips" of this alloy encased in an epoxy lens, mounted on a steel TO-66 case. The case and the electrodes are gold-plated, to avoid corrosive damage with age. The LED is large, with an active surface area of 7/16 inch (.4375 inch, about 11 millimeters) . This LED is much more powerful than the ones used in television remote controls, etc.

The Euon's output power is 170 mW/Steradians, which is a measure including the beam angle as a factor. It is difficult to represent light output power in the same way we would electricity. The average output power of the Euon's LED is 53 mW. The output from the LED spreads from the active surface area at 120 degrees, a third of a circle.

A small amount of the Euon's light output is slightly longer or shorter than 880 nanometers in wavelength.

The LED is attached to a heat sink (a piece of aluminum) especially made for it's case style, which increases the life of the LED. The heat sink, as with those inside a computer, draws heat away from the LED and radiates into the surrounding air. The case allows maximum airspace around the heat sink for greater efficiency.

The light from the Euon is steady and constant. It is not modulated.

What is the Euon's purpose? What is it good for?

The Euon is what is called a "mind machine" , although it has many more uses over parts of the body other than the brain. A mind machine is an electronic device which uses some form of magnetic, electromagnetic and/or sound energy to alter or enhance awareness. Other mind machines use flashing LEDs in goggles or eyeglass frames, headphones, electrodes to deliver low-level electricity, electromagnets, or biofeedback (EEG, etc.) to manifest positive effects in the user. The Euon is the first infrared light mind machine. The Euon enhances mental and physical function. The specifics, in myself and those who have used the device, are:

(...)" goes on to list some benefits, then:

"Other effects of infrared light (from 660 to 880 nm) that have been documented in many scientific studies are:

1\. Increased ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) synthesis in cells. ATP synthesis is a measure of the vitality of cells. ATP is involved in most chemical reactions involving energy production in cells.

2\. Increased growth rate of new cells, and an increase in the numbers of new cells.

3\. Greater RNA and DNA production. In a patent for a nootropic (a drug that enhances cognition), a doctor named Glatzky posited eloquently the theory that RNA production in brain cells is the substrate of memory, with documentation for this theory.

4\. Angiogenesis stimulation. Angiogenesis is the formation of new capillaries, which are the smallest blood vessels. This improves microcirculation in the area of IR application.

5\. Reduces swelling and edema through enhanced blood and lymph flow.

6\. Inhibition of the firing of non-adrenergic and non-cholinergic nerves, through nitric oxide release. Firing in these nerves is how non-sensory pain from internal organs is mediated.

7\. An increase in collagen synthesis.

8\. Vasodilation.

I must reiterate that I am stating the above effects as effects of infrared light in general, for educational purposes only, and I am Not claiming that the Euon treats disease or heals."

This is only about half of the post because it is very descriptive, gives contact information, information on how to use it (even for sexual performance, lol), why to not use it near or at the eyes or thyroid or areas that stimulate the amygdala, and then cites a bunch of studies on wound healing and ROS attenuation from IR lasers.

If I had the money, I'd get some safety goggles, the VetroLaser, The Euon, and some less powerful 'pen lasers' to use up my nostril and I'd laser everything! Lol. Again, I'm not neglecting the fact that Magnetism and water and cold and sun are all essential (and primordial). I actually plan to drastically ramp up CT, and consume as much RO water and salt as possible, if it comes down to my last dollars, they are going to clean water rather than even food, I can handle a fast, if I have to I will, lol.

Hope someone, anyone(!!) can give me some input on anything. Thanks, happy Memorial Day to those of you who celebrate it.

The Euon is an infrared (IR) light. It is, in part, a special LED that uses an alloy of aluminum, gallium and arsenic to turn electricity into a specific wavelength of light, which is 880 nanometers (billionths of a meter) in length. The LED employs three "chips" of this alloy encased in an epoxy lens, mounted on a steel TO-66 case. The case and the electrodes are gold-plated, to avoid corrosive damage with age. The LED is large, with an active surface area of 7/16 inch (.4375 inch, about 11 millimeters) . This LED is much more powerful than the ones used in television remote controls, etc.

The Euon's output power is 170 mW/Steradians, which is a measure including the beam angle as a factor. It is difficult to represent light output power in the same way we would electricity. The average output power of the Euon's LED is 53 mW. The output from the LED spreads from the active surface area at 120 degrees, a third of a circle.

A small amount of the Euon's light output is slightly longer or shorter than 880 nanometers in wavelength.

The LED is attached to a heat sink (a piece of aluminum) especially made for it's case style, which increases the life of the LED. The heat sink, as with those inside a computer, draws heat away from the LED and radiates into the surrounding air. The case allows maximum airspace around the heat sink for greater efficiency.

The light from the Euon is steady and constant. It is not modulated.

What is the Euon's purpose? What is it good for?

The Euon is what is called a "mind machine" , although it has many more uses over parts of the body other than the brain. A mind machine is an electronic device which uses some form of magnetic, electromagnetic and/or sound energy to alter or enhance awareness. Other mind machines use flashing LEDs in goggles or eyeglass frames, headphones, electrodes to deliver low-level electricity, electromagnets, or biofeedback (EEG, etc.) to manifest positive effects in the user. The Euon is the first infrared light mind machine. The Euon enhances mental and physical function. The specifics, in myself and those who have used the device, are:

(...)" goes on to list some benefits, then:

"Other effects of infrared light (from 660 to 880 nm) that have been documented in many scientific studies are:

1\. Increased ATP (Adenosine Triphosphate) synthesis in cells. ATP synthesis is a measure of the vitality of cells. ATP is involved in most chemical reactions involving energy production in cells.

2\. Increased growth rate of new cells, and an increase in the numbers of new cells.

3\. Greater RNA and DNA production. In a patent for a nootropic (a drug that enhances cognition), a doctor named Glatzky posited eloquently the theory that RNA production in brain cells is the substrate of memory, with documentation for this theory.

4\. Angiogenesis stimulation. Angiogenesis is the formation of new capillaries, which are the smallest blood vessels. This improves microcirculation in the area of IR application.

5\. Reduces swelling and edema through enhanced blood and lymph flow.

6\. Inhibition of the firing of non-adrenergic and non-cholinergic nerves, through nitric oxide release. Firing in these nerves is how non-sensory pain from internal organs is mediated.

7\. An increase in collagen synthesis.

8\. Vasodilation.

I must reiterate that I am stating the above effects as effects of infrared light in general, for educational purposes only, and I am Not claiming that the Euon treats disease or heals."

This is only about half of the post because it is very descriptive, gives contact information, information on how to use it (even for sexual performance, lol), why to not use it near or at the eyes or thyroid or areas that stimulate the amygdala, and then cites a bunch of studies on wound healing and ROS attenuation from IR lasers.

If I had the money, I'd get some safety goggles, the VetroLaser, The Euon, and some less powerful 'pen lasers' to use up my nostril and I'd laser everything! Lol. Again, I'm not neglecting the fact that Magnetism and water and cold and sun are all essential (and primordial). I actually plan to drastically ramp up CT, and consume as much RO water and salt as possible, if it comes down to my last dollars, they are going to clean water rather than even food, I can handle a fast, if I have to I will, lol.

Hope someone, anyone(!!) can give me some input on anything. Thanks, happy Memorial Day to those of you who celebrate it.

# Thread: Carbon nanotubes you say?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbon-nanotubes-you-say.7768/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-01T02:32:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbon-nanotubes-you-say.7768/post-91299>

[http://www.forskningsradet.no/en/Newsarticle/Cell\_signals\_via\_membrane\_nanotubes/1253966097275](http://www.forskningsradet.no/en/Newsarticle/Cell_signals_via_membrane_nanotubes/1253966097275)

Most of the bodyâ€™s cells communicate with each other by sending electrical signals through nano-thin membrane tubes. A sensational Norwegian research discovery may help to explain how cells cooperate to develop tissue in the embryo and how wounds heal.

For nearly ten years, researchers have known that cells can â€œgrowâ€ ultra-thin tubes named tunnelling nanotubes (TNTs) between one another. These nanotubes â€“ the length of two to three cells and just 1/500th the thickness of a human hair â€“ are connections that develop between nearly all cell types to form a communication channel different from any previously known mechanisms.

Dr. Xiang Wang and Professor Hans-Hermann Gerdes of the University of Bergen have discovered a previously unknown communication system between cells. (Photo: UiB)
In 2010, Dr. Xiang Wang and Professor Hans-Hermann Gerdes â€“ colleagues at the University of Bergenâ€™s Department of Biomedicine â€“ discovered that electrical signals were being passed through nanotubes from one cell to another at high speed (roughly 1-2 m/sec). Their research receives funding under the Research Councilâ€™s large-scale research programme Nanotechnology and New Materials (NANOMAT).

The breakthrough
In their key experiment, Dr Wang used fluorescent dye that changes in intensity as the electric potential of the cell membrane changes. When two cells connected by forming a nanotube, he poked into one of them with a microinjection needle to depolarise that cellâ€™s membrane potential. This caused the fluorescent indicator on the cell membrane to light up like a firework, and it was soon followed by a similar light display in the cell on the other end of the nanotube.
The breakthrough discovery began with an experiment demonstrating intercellular transmission of electrical signals via nanotubes in 2007. The researchers then carried out similar trials with a number of other cell types, observing similar occurrences.

â€œWe confirmed that this is a common phenomenon between cells,â€ explains Professor Gerdes. â€œStill, this characteristic is not in every cell type.â€

The experiment was replicated a number of times to obtain statistically reliable data. The electrophysiology group at the University of Bergen took precise conductivity measurements of the cell systems to determine the strength of the electrical coupling. In autumn 2010 the results were published in Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS) and were highlighted as top news in Nature News

Here a cell has coupled with another cell by growing a long nanotube which enables it to exchange electrical signals. (Photo: UiB)
Short lifespan
Intercellular nanotubes are far from permanent. Most of them last only a few minutes. This means the researchers cannot predict where and when the cells will form nanotube connections.

â€œIt is truly painstaking work,â€ says Professor Gerdes. â€œYou may sit there examining cells for hours through a microscope without seeing a single tube. If you are lucky, however, you catch sight of a nanotube being created and can film the event.â€

To raise the likelihood of finding nanotubes, the researchers developed a micro-matrix consisting of thousands of points and bridges on a plate surface. Smaller than a postage stamp, the plate is covered by a nano-structured material to which the cells adhere. The researchers place one cell onto each point and hope that nanotubes will form along the bridges between the points. The camera is focused on these bridges.
Once the nanotubes have been established, the researchers manipulate the cells at specified times; meanwhile the microscope is programmed to photograph, say, 50 preselected points every five minutes. The team can thus obtain data about many nanotube connections in a short time.

How do cells do this?
Dr. Wang quickly discovered that the mere presence of a nanotube was not sufficient to transmit an electrical signal. There had to be another mechanism involved as well.

Many cells form tiny membrane pores with each other called gap junctions, which are made up of ring-shaped proteins. Back in the 1960s it was discovered that directly adjacent cells could exchange electrical impulses through these gap junctions. What Dr Wang found was that one end of the nanotube was always connected to cells by a gap junctions before it transmitted its electrical impulses.

He also found that in some coupled cells voltage-gated calcium channels were involved in the forwarding of the incoming signals. When the electrical signal being sent through the nanotube reaches the membrane of the receiving cell, the membrane surface is depolarised, opening the calcium channel and allowing calcium â€“ a vital ion in cell signalling â€“ to enter.

â€œIn other words,â€ explains Professor Gerdes, â€œthere are two components: a nanotube and a gap junction. The nanotube grows out from one cell and connects to the other cell through a gap junction. Only then can the two cells be coupled electrically.â€

Controls embryonic cells?
Now the scientists are seeking answers as to why the cells send signals to each other in this way.

â€œItâ€™s quite possible that the discovery of nanotubes will give us new insight into intercellular communication,â€ asserts Professor Gerdes. â€œThe process could explain how cells are coordinated during embryo growth. In that phase cells travel long distances â€“ yet they demonstrate a kind of collective behaviour, and move together like a flock of birds can.â€

Nanotubes may also be a factor in explaining cell movement associated with wound healing, since cells move toward a wound in order to close it. We already know that electrical signals are somehow involved in this process; scientists can only speculate as to whether nanotubes are involved here as well, stresses Professor Gerdes.

Perhaps brain cells, too?
In terms of electronic signal processing, the human brain surpasses all other organs. If this same signalling mechanism proves to be present in human brain cells, it could add a new dimension to understanding how the brain functions. Communication channels involving synapses and dendrites that are already identified differ widely from nanotubes.
The Bergen-based neuroscientists see this research as an opportunity to formulate better explanations for phenomena related to consciousness and electrical connections in the brain. In the project â€œCell-to-cell communication: Mechanism of tunnelling nanotube formation and functionâ€, they are now studying precisely how nanotube mechanisms function in brain cells.

Professor Gerdes is currently conducting research at the European Molecular Biology Laboratory in Heidelberg. By studying the electrical connections in vivo he hopes to figure out how the mechanisms work in live subjects. The results could enhance understanding of diseases that occur when cell mechanisms fail to function properly.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-of-women-are-iron-deficient.7769/post-91311>

Did you know iron deficiency also impairs control of body temperature by interfering in conversion of the thyroid hormones thyroxine to triiodothy ronine?

Without free T3 from iron guess what happens?

Impaired ketone synthesis is one aspect of brain development adversely affected by hypothyroidism of any cause.

Connecting any dots now hypothyroidism leads to fat depletion in the brain long term?

Anyone see the link to neuro-degeneration? Diabetes? Leptin resistance?

since we are on an energy kick on the blog........ya know the whole E=MC2 thing........did you know that the auditory cortex and inferior colliculus of the human brain is the biggest energy hog in the entire body? It uses more Oxygen than any region. In fact, Ammon's horn in the temporal lobe is a marker for oxygen loss from poor mitochondrial function or environmental supply. In fact, here is another unusual fact of humans alone.......hypothyroidism causes serious hearing impairment in us by impeding ossification of the organ of Corti in the inner ear. This is why adult humans who are energy starved often complain of tinnitus.......See, E=MC2. And now you know why tinnitus really occurs. Tinnitus tells us the brain is starved of energy

As products of fatty acid oxidation, long-term ketone availability is directly linked to subcutaneous fat stores. Unlike glucose synthesis, ketone synthesis does not draw on amino acids so it does not threaten organ integrity by depleting tissue protein levels. Brain uptake of ketones occurs through the monocarboxylic acid transporter which rapidly ramps up ketone transport in proportion to rising ketone levels in the blood. The ketones main goal is to provide the brain lactate.

Neurons in the CNS and PNS love lactate, especially when they are suboptimal in ATP production or suffering from a neurodegenerative disease. Neurons become inefficient because of poor mitochondrial function due to lowered ATP production. This poor ATP recycling is the signal to the neurons and the brain mitochondria that they are trying to increase proton superconduction in CSF (density of CSF water) to improve CMRO2 and the current in the brainâ€™s other semiconductor, water. The higher CMRO2 is, the less the brain relies on ATP and the more it uses water superconduction. Lactate is like a high octane fuel for neurons.

The lactate supply in the brain is made by up regulation of MCT1 which is called mono carboxylic acid transporter 1.

Ketones can replace much of the human brainâ€™ s requirement for glucose, but they cannot meet the brain's entire energy needs (Cahill, 2006). In addition to providing the carbon to replace oxaloacetate in the tricarboxylic acid cycle, glucose is essential for the brain as the precursor to lactate , which is exchanged when ketones are taken up by the brainâ€™ s monocarboxylic acid transporter.

Ketone utilization is a constitutive feature of brain function because the amounts and activities of the relevant enzymes are not changed by starvation and always exceed the amount necessary to supply the brainâ€™ s energy needs. Hence, the brain is always ready to burn ketones as soon as they are available; a situation consistent with their function as its main backup fuel. The factor that limits ketone use by the brain is therefore the rate of ketone production by the liver and not the brainâ€™s ability to use them. Ketone availability is principally dictated by fatty acid release from fat stores, which in turn is determined by how much insulin levels fall in response to decreasing plasma glucose; it is low insulin that permits the mobilization of fatty acids from fat stores and their conversion to ketones by the liver.........

Ketones come from fat and no where else. This is why humans are designed to have subcutaneous fat and other primates do not.

People with T2D, ME/CFS, AD, PD generally generate a lot of superoxide in the brain as the cause of their disease because they eat too much sugar; Doing so does not allow them to make a lot of ATP (38 vs 147) and not being able to make a lot of ATP and this is why they eat way to many carbs to fast recycle ATP. They can not make ketones well because high insulin levels block ketone production in the liver. When this happens..........superoxide rises..........a lot.

Since superoxide ions and transition metals act in a synergistic manner in the creation of free radical damage, iron supplementation must not be done in patients with any active infections or in general any diseases. Very few people know, much less talk about it. And when iron is liberated in tissues it happens when they lose electrons. This means iron exacerbates all neolithic diseases. This is why so many women have low iron stores.

Neurons in the brain try to avoid superoxide generation at all costs and neurons usually attempt to increase lactate production as a consequence to offset superoxide generation to use lactate as a secondary fuel source to ketones. CSF lactate is a sign.......and this signal is sent through the CSF and into the circumventricular organs. This is where the leptin receptor senses........things from our environment.

Now go re read QB 4 blog post........closely. Add the quantum effects to the story.........you'll see things clearer.

The factor that limits ketone use by the brain is therefore the rate of ketone production by the liver and not the brain’s ability to use them. Ketone availability is principally dictated by fatty acid release from fat stores, which in turn is determined by how much insulin levels fall in response to decreasing plasma glucose; it is low insulin that permits the mobilization of fatty acids from fat stores and their conversion to ketones by the liver.........

Coconut oil to the rescue...

### nonchalant — 2013-08-01T16:30:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-of-women-are-iron-deficient.7769/post-91320>

Yes, I guess "Insulin resistance" as a reason for obesity is a bit simplistic. We really have to switch back to our original source of nutrients; start using our primary sources of power, instead of the backup to the backup.

In datacenter terms, the primary feed has failed, so we relied on the redundant power source. That failed, so we went onto backup generator. That ran out of diesel, so it's UPS battery backup now. And we are frantically swapping out batteries, left and right, trying to keep up, while the computers shut down memory banks and bypass more and more CPUs to conserve power. The government is insisting that the manufacturers send more batteries pronto!

### HoneyChild — 2013-08-01T18:21:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/20-of-women-are-iron-deficient.7769/post-91323>

It has helped tremendously. I have re-started the Leptin Reset/Ketogenic diet and I am noticing favorable changes in sugar cravings.

# Thread: Strange question about body shape
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2013-08-01T22:50:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/post-91333>

Hi friends,

This is mostly a question for the ladies but gents feel free to chime in too. I was wondering if any of you have experienced your body shape changing after doing the leptin reset. I don't necessarily mean weight loss. For example, I have an apple shaped body courtesy of PCOS and insulin resistance. I am all belly and I have zero butt and boobs. I was wondering if following Jack's protocol would help my body even out if that makes sense. It's not that I want a huge butt or expect miracles without plastic surgery or extreme workouts, but it would be nice if things started to look more proportional. I wasn't sure if or how doing the reset would affect fat distribution since it seems to be tied to hormones. It would be nice to change from an apple into a more feminine pear, but could the leptin reset help with that? Am I asking too much?

### caroline — 2013-08-02T00:41:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/post-91338>

Hi Annie! I am an apple shape too. After doing the reset for about 3-4 months I went down two sizes although my weight was up about 2 Kilos. In my opinion the apple shape is driven by hormone issues. It was last August that a young woman in a store asked me why I buy my clothes so big! When you are an apple shape - everything is always too tight and uncomfortable ....at least that it how it was for me. I always longed to be a pear shape too! At least it is more feminine. I didn't seem to notice tho that my clothes were falling off me! Very strange - I guess that I was focused on everything else like food and C/T and trying desperately to understand all the science.

So, now, here we are a year later...my weight is down around 8 kilos and I am down 3 to 4 sizes. I actually have found my rib cage and ribs and my waist! This is the least I can ever remember weighing by quite a bit and I look very healthy ...and feel great.

I was always a yoyo dieter ....as soon as I would lose the weight it would come back on no matter how hard I tried and I would always look gaunt and drawn.

I have noticed lately - my friends don't even mention weight loss . I was talking to a friend the other day and all of a sudden she stopped and said - you look great.
.
So - yes - your body will reshape but it happens bit by bit and sometimes you don't really notice....it is cumulative. Don't make the same mistake I did.....I bought new clothes last September /october and now they are way too big.

For the last month I have been doing kettle bell swings and it is making a big difference in my core strength and my shape and I love it. If I was younger I would definitely do a movnat with Erwan Lecorre!!

It will happen - no question ..... As long as you are 100% committed to dr. K's protocols. Are you epi paleo? I did much better when I added more fat in.

However - don't forget ......food is only 10% ......following light cycles and elimating EMF exposure and Get sunlight and grounding as much as possible - these are way, way more important. Don't under estimate that.

Good luck on this crazy and amazing journey to optimum

### MrPinkies — 2013-08-02T01:15:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/post-91344>

everyone hates me.. I have a natural hour-glass figure.. It used to be a very extreme hour-glass figure.. but now that I've lost all this weight with the leptin reset and am very close to my goal, it's not as extreme of curves.. google waist to hip ratio and you will know what I mean!  Size 26 down to a 12 (almost 10)!

I agree that the light cycles, etc are very important too..

### caroline — 2013-08-02T03:00:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/post-91349>

You are doing great Kate!

### AnnieW — 2013-08-03T04:00:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/post-91400>

Thanks for sharing your stories. Right now I'm between a 24 and 26. If I could get into a 16 again I would be happy. The problem is that I'm tall and I have to special order all my stuff because I can't find tall plus sizes in the stores where I live. The other issue is that most pants aren't cut for apple shapes. This means I have to buy huge waist sizes and I have all this extra fabric in the butt and legs. I think there's an hourglass hidden under the fat somewhere. There's a very defined waist even though I have a ton if abdominal fat. I can see it if I lift my arms over my head. Lol I know it sounds funny but that's the best way I can describe it.

I'm doing my best to eat epi paleo. Truthfully I haven't read about EMF or grounding so I need to look into that.

Thanks again for your help!

### caroline — 2013-08-03T04:38:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/post-91402>

Annie -,when you are an apple shape it is a nightmare to buy pants. It is like buying pants for a beach ball! I have definitely been there and you are tall as well.

For me - the most important thing was following the circadian rhythm and limiting EMFs. But - C/T is huge too.....I put bags of ice on my tummy.

Please don't underestimate how all this works together -,you will get there.....

There is a ton of reading I know and you have to read it all more than once to fully grasp it and it takes time to connect all the dots. Don't forget that JK is 7 or 8 years ahead of us all...

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/strange-question-about-body-shape.7779/post-91404>

I forgot to ask. ... Have you had a saliva ASI done? Are you working on your hormones?

Have you looked at the webinars? There are some great podcast too. And have you read Jacks book? These things really help to bring things into focus quickly.

Thanks to the Blue Blocker glasses I was able to reset mine for the first time in 15 years

Thanks for sharing this article!

See, I knew violence is a preventable disease. Chill.
[http://www.sciencemag.org/content/e...ract?sid=59cd8fac-3931-445d-86e7-855855c577da](http://www.sciencemag.org/content/early/2013/07/31/science.1235367.abstract?sid=59cd8fac-3931-445d-86e7-855855c577da)

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-03T18:05:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everybody-just-chill-out.7795/post-91431>

Dali

you make me smile.... I love my cold... wishing for some right now...

### Inger — 2013-08-03T20:35:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everybody-just-chill-out.7795/post-91437>

Interesting. I have been thinking about this. Why people seem more violent in warmer countries... I always felt so thankful for living so far north. Here people live with doors open.. day and night, no one cares. Of course, not in the city, but on the countryside this is very common. I always thought it was so cool to live like this! I actually hate locked doors. To not need to be afraid, not at home, not when you walk alone in the woods.. to me this is like paradise for sure. No dangerous animals either! Just freedom..!  Except of one snake, maybe. But easily avoidable.

And what is better than to have another person skin to skin to warm on when it gets chilly... huh! The very best heater I can think of...  pretty useful when it gets a little too cold at times
Maybe that is what makes it more anti violent.. because we need each other for the warming effect, who cares to fight

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-03T21:48:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everybody-just-chill-out.7795/post-91443>

This is great, just what I needed to see. I made a recent thread about tDCS to basically stop some symptoms I've been experiencing for a long time (psychological/emotional) and they've been randomly coming back (probably due to stress of moving again/brother's surgery/etc.), but yesterday was terrible. I got extremely anxious in Aldi while looking for some dark chocolate and fish, and everything just got weird. I was on the verge of having a panic attack - a bad one; thoughts of this mother whom I knew who killed her youngest son and the girl she was baby sitting flooded my mind - just a lot of negativity. My mind kept wandering over the question as to why _some_ people seem to snap while others can deal with high amounts of stress for their whole lives (such as my mother).... An epsom salt bath followed by a **cold** **bath** afterwards calmed my nerves, yet I woke up in an odd state of mind again today until I swallowed down a bunch of sea salt and took a **cold shower**...

That was a long way of saying that this was perfect timing to see that cold can repair/enhance one's psyche so to speak. It makes a lot of sense now too because I was more or less a 'winter baby' (well, born in November but during a snow storm) and loved the cold all my life. I'm also from a north/eastern European descent where at the minimal there are very cold winters. I think I need the cold, I should live somewhere cold, lol. I can't wait for winter now.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-04T01:07:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/everybody-just-chill-out.7795/post-91453>

This is just another reason why I get pissed when I hear somebody tell me or tell others cold is just hormetic.......if you do not know what you're talking about just remain quiet.

# Thread: What's Causing This to Happen and How Do I Stop It?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-causing-this-to-happen-and-how-do-i-stop-it.7799/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Huck — 2013-08-04T14:38:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-causing-this-to-happen-and-how-do-i-stop-it.7799/post-91479>

I need some help determining what is causing the itching.

A few weeks ago, I upped my iodine from 25 mg/day to 50 mg/day and then 75 mg/day. I experienced itching, although not too bad.

Then I had a urinary problem and doctor started me on flomax. One of the side effects of flomax is itching. About the same time I started flomax, I stopped taking one benedryl at night to help with sleep. Benedryl and other antihistamines are often listed as aggravating to bph and I thought it might have been a cause of my urinary problem.

So now my itching is severe and I noticed a few hives.

So what is causing the itching?

-   Detox from iodine
-   Stopping benedryl
-   Flomax
-   Combination of 2 or more of the above

I took a benedryl this morning to see if it helps. Last night, I applied some benedryl lotion I had for poison ivy and it gave me some relief, which is why I took the benedryl this morning.

I believe flomax has an accumulative effect, so I don't think stopping flomax for 1 day would have much of an effect. I could be wrong about this, but many guys who have reported itching, say it started after a few weeks of taking flomax, not on day 1 or 2.

So how do I do my n=1 study? How do I control for 3 variables, both separately and in combination? Or do I just tell the doc I am allergic to flomax and ask him to prescribe something else?

I've tried ct with epsom salts and baking soda. I get relief when I'm in the tub, but itching starts soon after getting out.

ETA: Other side effects of flomax I am experiencing are light headedness (it is an alpha blocker) and fatigue.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-04T16:10:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-causing-this-to-happen-and-how-do-i-stop-it.7799/post-91489>

itching like tinnitus is a sign of energy depletion along sensory microtubules........it means cellular signaling is off for some reason.

### Huck — 2013-08-04T16:45:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-causing-this-to-happen-and-how-do-i-stop-it.7799/post-91491>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=91489)
>
> itching like tinnitus is a sign of energy depletion along sensory microtubules........it means cellular signaling is off for some reason.

Any suggestions on what labs might help figure it out?

The benedryl has greatly reduced, but not eliminated the itching.

I'm at a weight I haven't seen in about 30 years, so I imagine my body is dumping a lot of toxins into the blood stream.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-04T18:57:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-causing-this-to-happen-and-how-do-i-stop-it.7799/post-91499>

I think what I would do, based on my experience, is to stop taking the iodine, for a while. I think you are detoxing, yes.
However, I would switch to painting on the iodine, so you still get the benefit, but the effect is localized. Do you have a Magnetico?

I had to stop taking Lugols for a month, to let things settle down and get my selenium caught up. No, I didn't have itching. The max I was taking was 50mg of Lugols, but that was only for a few weeks before I had to stop. I have recently started taking it again, 25mg.

### GlenPDQ — 2013-08-04T19:46:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-causing-this-to-happen-and-how-do-i-stop-it.7799/post-91503>

Have you thought about trying a warm bath with sodium bicarbonate added to the water? Bicarbonate is cheap and seems to be used for all kinds of things.

I had awful itching at night at one point in my life when I was drinking lots of diet pepsi sweetened with aspartame. Then there was another point where it felt like swarms of tiny gnats were biting me in bed. That was about the time a cell phone tower was built about 1/2 mile from my home. There is a bank at the base of the cell tower, and sometimes I wonder about the health of the employees.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-08-05T01:09:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/whats-causing-this-to-happen-and-how-do-i-stop-it.7799/post-91514>

It sounds similar to a situation I experienced a couple of years ago. I was building to do some high dosing on the iodine and all of a sudden I broke out in a rash on my back. It itched like crazy and yet was slightly raw and had hives all over it. I ended up stopping everything I was taking, except thyroid meds, and it slowly went away. However that took almost 5 months for the itching to leave. My suggestion and I wish I had known this then is to do a salt flush or some kind of flush to get the toxins out quickly. I'm not sure of the exact measurements but 1/2-1 tsp with approx 24-30 oz of good water drank down all at once and it should help. I've since done that with a bromide flush from iodine at a different time and within two days and two doses the rash had subsided considerably and the itching was almost gone.

# Thread: Stronger Heartbeat - Annoying
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-08-04T18:59:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/post-91500>

Last week I noticed something that has got to be really annoying.

My heart seems to be beating stronger.

I really only notice it when I lay down at night but it is making it harder to go to sleep.

It's also probably about 10 bpm faster but I would attribute that to not working out - Used to be around 55bpm now it's around 64bmp.

I should also not that my last blood pressure (8/1/13) was 100/60 - I'm a male at 195 lbs - not sure if that means anything but I've never been that low.

Lastly, the thrush on my tongue seems to be stronger than ever.

Could these all be related or any thoughts at all on the reason for the strong heartbeat would be greatly appreciated

My last TSH was 3.96 down from 4.89

Thanks for your input

### GlenPDQ — 2013-08-04T19:25:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/post-91501>

The stronger heartbeat after going VLC used to almost drive me nuts, but I figured it was a good thing overall. Its been about 15 months since then and my heartbeat is not trying to imitate a sledgehammer any more. Better circulation should speed recovery time if its any consolation.

I have considered that the effect may be because the heart prefers saturated fats as fuel instead of carbs, and most VLC diets have extra fat, so it may be acting like a kid in a candy shoppe. Then the extra ketones being circulated might contribute to the effect. It might also be that the thryroid hormones, parathyroid hormones, magnesium & calcium are being produced/used in better ways. Calcium is used to contract a muscle and magnesium is used to relax it the way I understand it. Or could it be your body is settling into a new cortisol strategy? Oxytocin near bedtime might be beneficial. Is there a dog around you could pet for 15 minutes before bedtime?

A higher protein diet with gelatin, peanut butter, shellfish, & red meat, for example, will contain more arginine. It is a precursor of nitric oxide (NO), and NO relaxes the blood vessels which might contribute to a lower Blood Pressure. (Many individuals take arginine and citrulline to get this effect.)

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-04T19:31:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/post-91502>

Thank you for your input.

Interesting points.

I do have a dog that I can pet for 15 minutes before bed.

I don't think it has anything to do with improved cortisol as it's still pretty jacked do to getting up multiple times per night at work.

### Lahelada — 2013-08-23T21:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/stronger-heartbeat-annoying.7801/post-93533>

I have the same problems in the last two days . I wear earplugs and the thump kept me awake the past two nights. I am not sure what it is but I did have coconut oil before bedtime. I also reintroduced coffee. Well the coffee goes again and the CCO moves to a different hour.
