I removed my 150# magnet from under my chair last night. I have been having some hip pain (which was a big problem years ago) that just won't go away. This morning my hip seems better. A few minutes after removing the magnet, though, I started feeling warm. This chair has a microfiber upholstery which traps body heat. A nearby ceiling fan came to the rescue. I am positive that for me, magnets allow me to be comfortable in high or low temperatures.

I think my hip pain started the combination of my grounded computer and my grounding sheet on my chair, over a period of several months. It was also recently affecting my shoulder and elbow.

I still have magnets on the floor near the sink and stove, and under the car seats. And I wouldn't CT without one.
At least maybe I won't have to fight the cat for my chair any more. There is still a small one under DH's chair, she can be content with that. And my Magic Magnetico never irritates my hip.

Hope I don't have to fight the munchies again. That was the main incentive for the magnet in the first place.

Perhaps some day I will be able to transfer the energy more efficiently. Working on those nanotubes!

Sally I did not even think about me being cold adapted (because I am NOT sitting an hour in ice water like Nicole..huh! ) until I talked to my brothers girlfriend the other day. She told me they have so cold a bedroom! She always freeze in the night. I asked if she keeps the window open, but she said no... They live in a 2 room apartment. I guess they have around 15-18 degree C or more... so way warmer than my close to freezing temps (even below if cold nights) and she said she have to use wool socks and pajamas and she still is cold. Then I realized I really must be cold adapted to some degree. It is like fueling on fat. It is so natural after years on that WOE you really forget normally people feel bad if they do not get their CH. You get so used to how you live - weird to realize it is pretty different than most

Was driving pretty far with hubs today to buy some organic hay for our sheep and we stopped at a fuel station where was also bistro, pizzeria MC Donalds style.. I walked through the room full of people eating to come to the bathroom and I watched them.... and it was so weird... I have not been in such a place for so long and now it felt like coming to another planet? So weird things I saw in peoples face. Interesting experience. maybe it was a little sad

I just started to feel way too warm. I wondered how is it so warm? No heating on at all? I watched the temps and it was 18 degree C. Holy moly I have now opened the balcony door to get some cold in. ahhhh amazing fresh air. I have no heating on at all but hubs have it so warm his side of the house! And his freaking Sauna is poisoning me...  When he is away I keep it cool in both apartments. nice and cool and fresh.

### nonchalant — 2014-03-10T04:31:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fun-with-magnets.7853/post-114453>

When the magnet is lying on the floor, north side up and south touching the floor or carpet, the positive field is directed under your house, while the negative field is directed upwards. So as long as the south side of the magnet is not elevated above your floor, you should be fine.

I don't know if anyone besides myself would get bad effects from small magnets. I do know that occasional use (such as when I CT for an hour) does not give me any side effects.

# Thread: Bifrontal Lobe Atrophy (maybe) of a relative

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-11T07:07:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bifrontal-lobe-atrophy-maybe-of-a-relative.7854/post-92082>

Hey all, I wanted to ask a question about this. Is there anything one can do to reverse this, or at least slow this down? I'm not certain on all the details because I don't stay in contact with this side of my family, and they've also had 'scares' before with other things that turned out not to be true. Regardless of the relationship and what not, I must help when I can. I'll attach a photo of her brain (my aunt) to this post. I exchanged emails with her daughter about a lot of things I've learned and delved into ketones, paleo diets, mct oil, tDCS, neurofeedback, yada yada yada. I sent a lot of clinical studies her way and I also posted a few of Dr. Kruse's blogs that delve into disorders of the brain (Is your protein bent/How to avoid the fates of Michael J Fox) and a couple of the CT blogs and Brain Gut 6. I sent a lot of Dr. Mary T. Newport's presentations as well - my cousin bought Upgraded Brain Octane Oil and Upgraded MCT Oil, and they stocked up on coconut oil. I tried to stress that all facets need to be looked at - the whole diet spectrum (not just including coconut oil), starting to move, doing brain exercises, looking up more practitioners, looking into advanced brain therapies, etc. I spoke about cold thermogenesis and even hinted towards EMFs. After seeing the picture of the brain my dad asked me to ask on the forums I go to because I was going on and on about Dr. Kruse and how apt he is to help his members on here and how knowledgeable he is. So, basically anything that is said here I will send his way (unless it is really really negative) and he can forward it to her. I'm going to try and get my dad to get her to seek out these forums as well.

I was assuming the starting point would be something like this:
1) Change diet ASAP (vegetarian-SAD diet currently) to epi-paleo and probably loaded in MCTs
2) Do lot's and lot's of CT
3) Maybe throw in the initial recommended supplements
4) Start moving and using the brain a lot (from some of the smart life forums presentations on AD and Dementia, physical exercise trumps mental exercise in warding of brain disease, especially vascular dementia)
5) Seek out additional testing, imaging, and therapies

Anything would be helpful!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-12T00:57:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bifrontal-lobe-atrophy-maybe-of-a-relative.7854/post-92172>

The main NT of the frontal lobe is dopamine and ACH. When Energy goes down in a system guess what happens to these two NT"s? They go down.......as they go down and when they do you do not make neurons and you develop neuronal atrophy. Neurosurgeons call this hydrocephalus ex vaceo.......when it gets bad......it turned into a Neurodegenerative disorder called dementia.....

# Thread: GERD going
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerd-going.7862/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T17:52:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerd-going.7862/post-92103>

A very interesting thing happened yesterday with DH. He forgot to take his morning Nexium. This does not happen very often. I noticed at about 1pm that he hadn't taken it, and made an executive decision (yay dopamine!) to not tell him. I hesitated, because he suffers quite a bit when he gets heartburn, and he doesn't get true relief the entire day. But I knew that a year ago he would have realized his mistake in mid morning, more recently he wouldn't get tummy problems until the afternoon, and once lately not until evening. This morning I saw that he still hadn't taken yesterday's pill, though he did take today's. He has no complaints about heartburn.

This is significant, imho. I'm not sure what has caused this improvement. His diet hasn't changed much, though recently he has agreed to cut down on commercial fried foods (but I fry stuff for him at home in CO, bacon fat, etc.) For the last year maybe he has slightly reduced his wheat consumption. He has slightly increased his seafood consumption this year. I have found HFCS free catsup, sweet relish, & BBQ sauce for him. I make his salad dressing. He haphazardly takes chewable enzymes with meals.

Other factors are that the fluids he is drinking are non-fluoridated, for the most part. I pack a quart and a half of tea in his lunch so that he doesn't drink as much of what's available at the work place. I use some parts of the Mitochondria Rx in his weekday morning shake. Also he is used to having low lighting at night. And we have been sleeping on the Magnetico for 2 months. His sleep has improved; he falls asleep within 5 minutes of hitting the mattress. But he still wakes up in the middle of the night sometimes. He doesn't want my grounding sheet on his side because it curls up, but he often crowds my side now.  He hasn't increased his sun exposure, but he always has a fairly good tan. He has been wearing shorts more often this past couple years, and lately he has decided that he doesn't dislike sandals anymore. He goes barefoot a little more often.

From a tantalizing hint Dr. K gave out a while ago, I'd guess that it is the better water that is helping him the most. If he decides to kick the Nexium habit, I will be SO relieved. He has been taking prescription drugs for heartburn for over a decade. I imagine his body would really like some more protein that is actually digested first, for a change. I tried before to wean him from Nexium using Betaine HCL, but it didn't go well. I guess you really have to want change...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-11T18:07:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/gerd-going.7862/post-92108>

Photoelectric effect, water, and magnetism.......3 legged stools needed for life evolved on this planet. So far your hubby is apply two of three.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T18:38:14+0300

Thanks for the reply, Jack. Yes, I've been contemplating your three-legged stool. I think I'll up his exposure to the 3 factors. Perhaps put a small magnet under his recliner.

I forgot to mention that he hasn't had sunblock (with a few exceptions) for about a year. That has helped.

Actually, your three factors will be easier to incorporate into his life than dietary changes. I don't begrudge his stubbornness against dietary changes because he also dug in his heels when I went low fat. That was a good decision on his part!

# Thread: Coursera stuff
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coursera-stuff.7865/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Albert83BCN — 2013-08-11T20:21:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coursera-stuff.7865/post-92127>

Hi there people!

I recently discovered all that Coursera thing and the possibility to learn from online courses, cool thing!

I did a search on the forum and I have already seen that some of your are taking some of the courses (I saw some threads regarding one about epigenetics). I'm enrolled right now in two and I just wanted to share with you all as they might be of some interest to someone.

First one is by the University of Pittsburg - Nutrition and Physical Activity for Health
[https://www.coursera.org/course/nutritionforhealth](https://www.coursera.org/course/nutritionforhealth)

As an endurance runner, gym guy and nutrition enthusiast I couldn't help but enroll into this one! It's easy stuff and most people here I bet already know most of the concepts, some bad things is that it falls a lot into CW from the SAD, yet you can always learn something new (and challenge what you already know).

The second one by Duke University - Introductory Human Physiology is way more difficult, I think it could be easier but the material is not very well presented, they mix a lot of non-fundamental concepts without proper introductions (IMO) but well, there's the internet to look for 'dummies' sources of info so you can rule out the meaning of all this. The concepts by themselves seem very interesting and I think they can help undestand many things of the quilt for the poor laymen as I am.

[https://www.coursera.org/course/humanphysio](https://www.coursera.org/course/humanphysio)

There are many others that seem interesting! Once I finish one of the two, I'll keep looking for more to enroll!

### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T22:01:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coursera-stuff.7865/post-92146>

Albert, be sure to check out the forum for each class. You can get into some interesting discussions in those. I have squeaked in a few of Dr. K's ideas in a couple classes. Most people have typical responses, but in a Physics class I was in several people were interested in his ideas about quantum water and humans.

# Thread: In Search of Optimal

### Nathan — 2013-08-11T21:16:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-search-of-optimal.7866/post-92137>

Hello! I am so glad to have found Dr. Kruse's website. I have been dealing with health issues for the past 8+ years despite avoiding most processed foods for most of my life. A year ago I was diagnosed with Lyme disease and Candida among other things and have been working towards healing myself ever since. I have made so much progress and reduced nearly all of my symptoms and the main thing I am working on now is improving my sleep. As luck would have it that's actually how I came across this blog. One of my many Googles landed me on a CT blog post and I've been reading away ever since.

I started the Leptin Rx and CT about 2 weeks ago with positive results. I'm actually struggling with being underweight (went from a consistent 170 to <150 since starting treatment of antibiotics and my yeast free diet to deal with the candida. I lost a lot of weight from cutting carbs out of my diet when I went yeast free but once I got rid of the candida I went back to carbs hoping to put back on a few lbs and the carbs just go right through me and I have not been able to gain back any weight....until the Leptin Rx. I've actually gained 4-5 pounds since starting the Leptin Rx so am very happy with that and look forward to the other positive benefits.

Currently on Phase 2 of CT and will be going to Phase 3 very soon. Can't wait to get the benefits of a solid nights sleep.

Also just discovered the effects of blue spectrum light and wearing my blue blockers every night. Trying to reduce EMFs and have ordered some earthing bands and grounds for our laptops.

Anyway, I am very excited and look forward to my continued journey down this new path.

### Nathan — 2013-08-13T06:11:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-search-of-optimal.7866/post-92330>

I did my first CT bath yesterday. I did it for about 20 minutes and it felt good during and afterwards. It was actually easier than I thought it would be. It sure does take a lot of ice to get the bath to temp so I'll need to start making some ice blocks rather than buying bags of ice.

Sleep was no different and I did experience some mild herxing and guess I must have burned some fat and released some toxins. This morning was rough and I felt better as the day went on. I have upped my chlorella and activated charcoal to help deal with the detox effects.

CT is actually something my body has been craving most of my life. I grew up on Lake Ontario and when I was a kid I would always jump in the lake in the dead of winter. Not sure why but something always drew me in. I wouldn't stay in long, more of a jump in and then run into the house and dry off but I always liked that feeling of cold radiating out of my body. Now I know why.

I rented a ski house with some friends a few years back. A few of us were hanging in the hottub after a day of skiing and a buddy and I kept challenging each other to see who could lay in the snow the longest. Of course we had a few drinks in us so that made it easier. I think we each maxed out around 2 minutes. I'll be prepared for the next snow challenge.

I CTed in the tub again tonight and everything started out fine. Was going for 30 minutes and a little over 20 minutes into it I started shivering like crazy. I held out as long as I could and made it 28 minutes before giving in. It took me forever to warm up. I just had uncontrollable shivers for 40+ minutes. Back to normal now.

Time for bed and hoping for a long, deep sleep.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-search-of-optimal.7866/post-92580>

I would like to echo what Caroline said . Prance around naked after the CT for a while. Dressing immediately after can give you worse shivers than walking aound naked. # Thread: RT3 after being on  NT. Advice and help needed.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### karicat — 2013-08-11T22:49:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92151>

Hi everyone !! I need some advice.

I'm currently on Dzugan's program but I'm concerned because he doesn't test for RT3.
A few years ago, before starting any thyroid meds, my RT3 was off the range (high) and my ft3 was low. I don't have the exact numbers with me right now, but I remember clearly it was something like:
RT3 3.75 (range 1.50-3.50) with a ft3 of 1.9 (range 2.0-4.5)
This RT3 was only lowered with T3 meds.

But when I started with Dr Dzugan last year, they put me on NT. I told them T4 was nasty on my body, and the only time I have felt a truly difference on my symptoms was when I took only T3 (no t4 at all, just straight t3). The difference was like night and day, taking T3 was like waking up after many years, no more brain fog, energy, my mood was 1000 times better, my body was stronger, etc (my sight was also better, that was kind of weird, everything seemed much clearer). The problem is that these effects never lasted long, perhaps a week at the most, and then I started feeling bad again. I still believe that my problem was with the dose, perhaps too high too fast, too low, perhaps I needed a little t4, I still don't know the solution. My body loves the T3 but I don't know how to dose it correctly.

Last year, after being on NT 0.5 grains for a couple of months, I got some labs, and just out of curiosity, tested on my own the RT3. Results were:
Rt3: 2.10 (range 1.50-3.50), free t3 2.0 (range 2.0-4.5)
I really didn't think much about it at the time, and as I was also having side effects from some of the supplements, and perhaps of too much progesterone, I didn't pay the necessary attention to it.

Unfortunately, I've never felt quite good on the program, but I must admit I've had some good days. But for the past few months, I've been feeling worse and worse. I've been making my updates telling them how I was feeling and among other changes, my nt has been increasing (0.5, 1.0 and 1.5).
I was on 1.5 grains for approx two months, when I told them I was really tired, incredibly brain fogged and feeling just awful and even depressed. And as I was feeling so lousy I made an experiment on my own and used some t3 I had left and started taking 0.25 mcg t3 plus de 1.5 on NT (probably too much T3 but I wanted to see what would happen). The same day I took the t3 around noon, I started feeling already better by 5.00 pm. As usual it was amazing, the good mood, the sharp mind, the focus, improved sight. I kept on this dose for 5 days, and then, around the 6th day I started feeling bad again. So I made my new update telling them about my symptoms and my experiment with the T3 and they just raised the NT more (to 2 grains) and no additional t3 of course.

I was on this new dose of NT for 8, 9 days and feeling even worse. And then I remembered about the RT3 test, and wondered if maybe that could be part of the problem. So I tested it a couple of days ago, and the results were:
RT3: 3.45 (range 1.50-3.50) with a ft3 of 2.4 (range 2.0-4.5), for a ratio of 7.
I'm now almost out of range with my RT3.

I'm going to make my update do Dr Dzugan in a little while, but I would like to know what you guys think about this situation. It worries me that they don't test for the RT3 lab, and if it wasn't that I though about testing it, they would have probably given me more NT or kept me at the same dose.

I don't know if I need to stop the NT completely (I think so, when I looked at my journal, it seems that with every higher dose of NT I wasn't feeling better, just slowly getting worse). And I don't know whether to ask them for just t3, or a little T4 with more T3 or what to do. I need someone really skilled on these issues and with the right T3 meds and amounts in order to truly help me and guide me through this process. Clearly the NT is not working and I think it is making me worse. I've always had this problem with the T4, it seems I don't tolerate it well. On the other hand, the T3, when it worked, was like being truly alive for the first time in many, many years.

Any feedback or advice will be greatly appreciated.
Best regards from Lima

Karina

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-11T22:57:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92154>

Karina

I always run Free T4, Free T3 and rT3 as well as TSH when I do labs for Dzugan, I run the labs that help me the most. I always make sure I have the labs I think I need to help me figure out what's actually going on.

If you don't feel good on NT - put that in your update, and comment about how you felt on T3 - you know you the best. ASK for T3 for the next 2-3 months to see how you feel on it. make sure Dzugan knows how you felt when on T3...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-11T23:05:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92156>

I agree with Gretch. You decided what to run not them. And then when you prove why you are not doing well you help suggest an acceptable change to you both.......When I hear this type of story I suspect an microglia problem.......meaning neurons cant make pregnenolone from cholesterol cause free T3 in brain is low........you might want to try a GABA test to see if you have a leaky BBB.......then for sure you know the gut is likely bad or you have a bad EMF field across your brainstem.........another way to look at this is to look at your progesterone and LH levels.......if both are low I am betting on a field effect and not a diet effect. If this is found.......you also bet phase 2 and 3 detox in liver sucks too.......and your dehydrated inside your cells losing Magnesium all of which make you feel like shit.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-11T23:22:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92157>

I have noticed, as the years go by and I progress on my health journey, that when I find something good, I feel the effects only temporarily. I was giddy when I started a low carb, high fat diet. Incredible mood. It lasted about a month. I didn't stop the diet, I just found something else I needed, like the Leptin Rx. And CT, seafood, grounding... all of these (and others) made large jumps in my health, with good mental effects also. But the feelings normalize.

Perhaps stay on T3 and pursue other ideas. It seems that so many different things are wrong with everyone. It's amazing we are alive still.

### karicat — 2013-08-12T00:17:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rt3-after-being-on-nt-advice-and-help-needed.7867/post-92164>

Thank you so much for the quick answers !!

Gretch, I usually run other tests that are not required on Dzugan's panel. But as I have to pay for everything (I'm from outside the US and my insurance in Peru won't pay for any of those labs) sometimes I admit I skip some labs that are not on the panel. And I have sent them these additional labs but I don't know if they even look at them or what. For instance, a couple of months ago, I sent them some labs of progesterone and estradiol taken on the 20th day of my cycle and the estradiol was really low (18 pg ml). the progesterone was also low (0.9 ng/ml). I was on just a small dose of p as I was having a very hard time with it, and no triest. They said nothing at all about this, and it is only a couple of weeks ago when I complained of having incontinence issues and a couple more things, that they have given me a dose of triest.
Perhaps I'm wrong but if they are seeing such low levels on the labs, why wait till I start getting hot flashes or some other symptoms instead of acting faster based on labs.

I used to think if they are the hormone experts why don't they ask me for these other tests if they consider them relevant (like the frees, or reverse t3, estradiol or ferritin, etc). I mean, I try to read everything I can regarding these issues, but surely it is not my main area of expertise and that's why I searched for what I thought was one of the best hormone specialists. I'm only depending on them for my current treatment, as the doctor where they sent me in FL (the one who writes the prescriptions) knows nothing about these problems.

I have already told them many times on my update how I felt with the T3 but I was still given the NT, just more of it.
I think it makes sense asking them for some t3. Thank you for your input

Dr Kruse, you are absolutely right. Now I know better than to wait for someone to adjust my program based on just labs, or random symptoms. I have to start making my own suggestions on what to do and what to change.
I'm going to find out if we have that gaba test available in Lima.
I haven't tested my LH levels for quite a while, but when it was done in the past (because of my period stopping when I was 33), all sex hormones (specially e and p, but also t) were very low as well as the LH. That's why I was told that I had a problem with my pituitary or hypothalamus, it didn't matter which they told me, because there was no cure for it, just replacing hormones.

Nonchalant, I understand what you say, but with the t3 I'm sure it is because of the effects (physical and psychological) it has on my body. The first thryoid replacement I had was t4 and I begged the doc to give it to me thinking it was the answer to my problems. To my surprise it was the opposite, and I felt quite lousy on it. When I tried the T3 (which I didn't have much hope for after my t4 failed experience), I couldn't believe the difference and how I felt on it. It was like a thick fog was lifted almost instantly. And every time I have tried it, at different times in the past few years, it always had the same effect. It is like I reach the so called "sweet spot" most of the times in the first day I take it, only to last around 5 days, and then I go back to the fog and feeling bad. It is interesting now that I think about it that when I was given welbutrin I had a similar experience the first two days, and after that I started feeling my normal self again. When I was given zoloft or paxil I didn't feel anything similar to the good feeling of welbutrin.
But yes, I definitely agree that implementing other changes like CT, seafoood, etc also helps.

Any additional suggestions on what to do with my current thyroid dose (2.0 nt) until I receive my new program ? I'm feeling quite lousy and don't really know what to do in the meantime. Between them reading my update and the Dr in Fl approving it, it might be at least 4 days till I get some answer.

Thank you once again !

Just to let you know about my experience, frustrating really.
Have a nice day everyone !

### nonchalant — 2013-08-13T16:57:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-update.7870/post-92357>

Caroline, I'll just repost this here.

Well, let me tell you a bit of my story. I came to this place fairly early, and was able to implement each protocol as Dr. Kruse slowly introduced them. With each one, I found some improvement. Some remarkable changes, like my skin texture changing completely for the better, and the scoliosis in my back going away, and sleeping through the night, and excellent hormonal changes just from cold water, and improved vision, and dramatically better night vision, and loss of anxiety, and and interesting mechanism that deadens pain to the skin in a few minutes (like when you run into a stinging plant). What else... Skin that rarely burns, and when it does it is fine the next day. Hearing that doesn't ring. I'm sure I'm forgetting several things.

I came here to lose weight, and I discovered the way to health. And I discovered that it wasn't just one thing that was wrong with my diet/lifestyle. So there isn't one fix. Oh, and the Magnetico? I think it's magic. I believe it is detoxing heavy metals for me. And recently it looks like it is what my body needs, to finally let go of the fat. I'm getting a little giddy recently about it. I like the Magnetico so much I'm working on convincing my elderly parents with Parkinson's to buy one. Do they need to lose weight? No. But they need better health. More energy. And the Magnetico gives energy in spades. Pure energy that the body doesn't have to digest. Energy for anything the brain thinks it needs.

As someone (Jonathan) said on this forum, everybody is at peak physical health, given the situation (field) they are in. That is what the other 90% of our brain is for, maintaining health and function. Your brain has a prioritized Christmas list it is waiting to fill, as soon as it gets the money. When some of us first get the money, we lose weight. Others gain the ability to walk gracefully. (That was another benefit I got from Dr. Kruse's advice.) It just depends on what is needed the most, and I suppose what is easiest to fix.

edit: And plantar faciitis cured! How could I forget being able to walk barefoot again! And relief from occasional constipation. Thanks Dr. K!

### cantweight — 2013-08-13T17:40:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/success-update.7870/post-92372>

I'll play Audrey

My successes have come in very different forms from what I had expected and thought that I wanted.

I had already spent years recovering from illnesses I honestly suspect would have killed me. Big scary diagnoses like LUPUS, Lyme, Babesia, Bartonella, parasites, Bi-polar disorder, Hashimoto's, Epstein Barr, PCOS, sleep apnea, Metabolic Syndome, and Fibromyalgia. Symptoms such as migraines that would render me unable to function and took days to recover from ruled my world, periods with so much blood loss that I would end up in the ER were the norm. My gallbladder was removed at the ripe old age of 28 and by 34 I had had 3 surgeries to remove ovarian and peri ovarian cysts. The amount of medication I was taking was staggering. The destruction of my body took years. Years of poorly trained medical experts giving a name and a drug to every group of symptoms my body produced in its effort to scream for help.

I came upon this knowledge bank of people in March of 2012; I spent hours and hours devouring information I had never seen or heard before. By April of 2012 I was waist deep in cold water and learning to like seafood.

My initial results with CT and epi-paleo were life changing. I had the answer to one little chunk of the puzzle. My application of this knowledge was often less than stellar but it was ok because I knew what to do....I had the tools in my bag. As the information kept coming(and coming, and coming) from Dr Kruse the realization that I had only come a fraction of the way towards my goal of true health really pissed me off. "But I have already done so much." constantly echoed in my head.

But its true...when you know better you do better. So I have and it is over a year a later. I have moved to home with low EMF, I spend my days barefoot, I am mindful of exposures and do my best to counter. We have miles to go in regard to artificial light, EMF, circadian rhythm...etc. But we are listening and learning.

Dr Kruse had a facebook post months ago that is still resonating with me....it was about organization, clearing physical clutter, the mind body connection with the state of the home.

My biggest day to day struggle is housework. Why is it a struggle? Mitochondrial dysfunction/dehydration. We moved into this house last June and endured months of renovation. My dining room had become my "hoarder" room. Full of boxes and crap I had no idea what do with and no energy to address. In the past week I have mowed a big chunk of land with a push mower, almost cleared my hoarder room, moved furniture, cleaned out half the garage....and all with little to no recovery time!! This may sound lame but it is huge for me! To have the energy to do it was massive in itself but to not lose 3 days after doing each job is something I have not seen my body do for close to 10 years!!! My body is ready to exercise now. This is what I've been waiting for; working for. I had defeated so many horrible symptoms, restored happiness and calm to my world, retrieved my brain from intense cognitive decline, but never was able to get even an ounce of energy. It took a damn long time to come...but it is here and I am grateful. To comprehend the extent of my body's decline beyond the illnesses and their symptoms and to respect its need to take years to fix what took years to break....success.

My husband learning that he can control his alcohol cravings with CT....another huge success.

Having more knowledge on becoming well than most doctors....success.

Having the tools to raise healthy children in this mess of a world....success.

I have shared this blog and its infinite wisdom too many times to count. Most people smile and nod politely when I start in on it all. Eventually they glaze over and I realize I have lost them.

One mom I met last fall has a son with an ADD/ADHD diagnosis and had him on multiple meds. I shared this info and at times feared I had pushed her away with my "nagging" about his diet. Two weeks ago we sat by my pool and with eyes filled talked about how her son is off a med and doing so well, how the whole family is calmer and happier than ever. She thanked me. That is my biggest success.

I had initially expected my success to come in a pair of size 6 skinny jeans. My vision was narrow. My eyes are open now.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-08-12T09:04:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/posting-problems.7872/post-92222>

Technology RX #1 - Why do we get cached?

Technically, this is a compromise that many forums -and extensible to other kind of websites- take to optimize access. Each time a webserver receives a request to get a page (from your internet browser) it must be fetched from the database, get rendered into HTML (the hypertext format document that you are just now reading via the browser) and send it back to you. This consumes some processor and memory from the server, and if the database gets hit too much, performance goes down, and if the hardware capabilities of the server are less than the number of requests, the website slows down.

So what many web solutions do is caching the websites that have been requested once, so that they are kept in memory and when another (or the same) user requests it again, the page is directly sent to him based upon what was sent last time, if the server thinks that the document is the same. Problem is, the same document (same means same URL on your browser) can change (and this happens a lot in the forums) and then you get a response that is old. This is a minor drawback that the forum accepts in order to ramp up performance. This implies that the unaware user might think that his post has been deleted or not posted, that's not necessarily the case, it's there but you're still not seeing it because you get an old version of the thread until the server deletes the old cache and refreshes it with the latest content.

The trick to get the latest content always would be to request the server the full page, this can usually be forced down by hitting the F5 several times or depending on the browser there are some other combinations.

Hope that clarifies the issue!

### caroline — 2013-08-12T11:11:17+0300

# Thread: Tips to prepare for a long flight?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 5

### BJK77 — 2013-08-12T17:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-prepare-for-a-long-flight.7873/post-92234>

Hi All,

Long time, no post  Some of you may remember me and some not! I haven't posted in quite a while. The beginning of the summer I went as "off grid" as I possibly could and I've been totally reaping the rewards. I've been feeling the absolute BEST I have in years. I definitely still have a long way to go, but my belief in all that Jack has been saying about EMF has strenghened tenfold! Earthing, swimming and staying away from EMF is definitely where it's at for me. I've pretty much been living outside and limiting myself to about an hour or two per week of electronic use (aside from work, of course) and it has been glorious. Any time I use my laptop for more than a few minutes my entire right hand goes numb/tingly and ice coold. It's a definite sign for me that I am on the right track. I will try to write a post detailing all my improvements in the next few weeks, but for now I'm back asking for some help.

I found out a few weeks ago I have to fly across the country for work - Seattle to be exact. We decided to make it a family vacation and bring the hubby and kids along. I've been forcing myself away from electronics even moreso than before - handwriting things instead of typing them at work, basically anything I can do to limit it. I need to know what else we should do to prep for the flight though and we leave tomorrow! Nothing like a last minute request, right?

I've been popping the CoQ10 and trying to drink even more than I did before. I'm up to about 1.5 - 2 gallons per day. I know Jack mentioned what he did to prepare for his Santa Fe flight on a webinar, but I can't remember which one and I don't want to have the laptop on long enough to listen to ALL of them. I'm thinking it may have been the mito rx . . . does anyone know for sure so I can just listen to that one? Any other tips on what to do besides push the CoQ10 and water?

We'll only be spending 1 night in Seattle and then we're heading onto Olympic National Park where we'll be soaking up nature! I plan on tons of grounding and swimming in ice-cold lakes  So yes, the long flight has me very concerned, but I'm hoping to make up for it when we get there both in CT/nature, but also with family bonding time as we always find we grow and heal immensely on these family trips. That being said, I know the flight itself is not going to be the best for me, but stressing out over it will certainly not do me any good so I'm choosing to focus on the positive and benefits I WILL see!!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-10-04T20:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fukushima-disaster-sea-veggies-food.7875/post-142805>

Fast forward to today.........Oxygen Bankruptcy is a problem chronically not acutely for humans........but it is a benefit short term if they know how to bio hack it. This is why my bio hack on the paleo diet and hypoxia recently had to go 14 months and not a few months to overcome the short term benefits.

Oxygen bankruptcy for guys like [Ben Greenfield](https://www.facebook.com/bengreenfieldfitness) works wonders. But for most people who live a hypoxic life because of excessive carbohydrate loads........they have to worry. This like the Jimmy Moore's of the world. Hypoxia that is shorter term and in bursts normally involves extreme physical exertion, going past the point of sweating and into the zone of oxygen debt. Fast treadmills, powered staircases and other fiendish machines force the exertion. A “drink” consists of a timed ride on such a machine/activity. All drinks at the "oxygen bankruptcy" bar cause short term pain that can benefit you longer term. They all stimulate a lot of Nrf2 and AMPK. The issue becomes if you live your life like this chronically due to non native EMF exposure circa, Mr. Moore. This is how Ben and Jimmy differ. That difference is a quantum one. If you have too much mTOR or NF-kB in your system, these activities/drinks will get rid of mTOR and NF-kB and help you. Ben does this all the time. After the pain, you will feel better. If you're an over weight podcaster who abuses non native EMF and blames carbs.........You have to wear a cardiogram monitor while on a machine and it will shut down automatically if there is any sign of an impending heart attack before you use hypoxia. Why? Ben trains his muscles to offload the risk.......Jimmy can't because of all the blue light and non native EMF. Context is huge with hypoxia. This is the lesson I learned in my latest bio hack.

Consider the Sherpa's.......hypoxic by nature but they don't pound carbs, because they don't grow there often naturally.......they actually eat butter to maintain weight they lose because of the intense cold at 15,000 ft plus........they also are 100% connected to Earth and the sun........unlike modern podcasters inside their house and constantly on their cell phone.

If you checked Jimmy's and Ben's VO2max, EEG, or MEG data you would see a massive difference in it........and the result is in higher magnetic flux in Ben's mitochondria making him a machine and Jimmy a zoo animal. It all comes back to paramagnetism of O2.

# Thread: Healthy begginning
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raylynn — 2013-08-12T21:57:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/post-92274>

I started the leptin reset 8/3 and hope to continue to be healthy. I have reset before, about 2 years ago, but went downhill with a hospitalization for asthma and pneumonia. Lost my way until now. I have always eaten a BAB, but ate horribly the rest of the day and am now up about 30 pounds from 2 years ago. I will have surgery in one week and be home for 3 months. I am trying to get set so I can eat my best the next three months in hopes of healing faster.
I love and believe in Dr. Kruse's advice. I know it works, since I did it before. I felt the best I have ever felt as an adult at that time.
Since I started last week, I have begun waking up at least once a night sweating. I know Dr. Kruse says women will have a change in sweat patterns to begin with, even before losing weight. I hope this is what is happening to me. Also, I have begun having very vivid dreams. I did this last time, so I hope this means I am on the right road.
I have one question? I wonder if I am eating too much food. I eat til I am full, but afterwards feel too full. Should I slow down when I eat to let my brain catch up with my stomach? I hate that bloated feeling and want to feel full but not bloated.
Also, I am an operating room nurse, so don't get but 30 minutes for lunch, and it is hard to feel full in that short time.
Hope to continue to post on here after my surgery to keep me motivated.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-12T22:18:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/post-92277>

Hi Raylynn, welcome!
I rarely eat until full; I eat until I am disinterested. I'm not sure when I got to the point in which my body started telling me that. I just eat plenty of seafood and meat and fat, and at some point a switch seems to go off when I realize I have lost interest in eating and I start think about doing something else. It may be leptin, not sure about that. Even though I have lost interest, I may or may not finish eating. Depends on what it is. Usually I would eat the protein/fat first, then if I still wanted something, I would eat the veggies until I got bored.

You aren't eating too much, provided you are chowing down on animal/seafood fat and protein.

### caroline — 2013-08-13T01:06:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-begginning.7878/post-92295>

Hi Raylynn.....I definitely do much better if I add in more fat and less protein - like you I would feel bloated and lethargic with too much protein. Have you tried having a BPC for breakfast? I usually have 2 and some raw meat and I am good to go. For me - I space my meal out over an hour or so - kinda gives my brain a chance to catch up!

# Thread: Raylynn's new healthy life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 37
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raylynn — 2013-08-12T22:07:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92276>

I am 48 years old and a wife with one 13yo son.
Started leptin reset 8/3 and hope to continue to be truly healthy.
I am about 60 pounds overweight and having surgery in one week. I will be home for 3 months after so want to get healthy so I can heal faster.
I have started having changes in my sweating patterns and very vivid dreams, so think I am on my way!
I have eaten a BAB for 2 years after starting a reset back then. Love me some bacon!! The rest of my meals are harder for me, but I am working on meal planning and stocking the house with appropriate food. While off work, I plan to continue to eat paleo, and when I am rehabing, I will start the exercise program. Rehab will start about 6 weeks after surgery, so it should be good timing for my reset.
Can't wait to feel better, energetic, and just generally happier!

### nonchalant — 2013-08-12T22:26:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92278>

Raylynn, check this out for Jack's pre-surgery protocol:

[http://www.jackkruse.com/peri-operative-optimal-surgical-considerations/](http://www.jackkruse.com/peri-operative-optimal-surgical-considerations/)

### caroline — 2013-08-13T01:01:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92294>

Hi Raylynn .. and welcome to journal land!

### Raylynn — 2013-08-14T21:36:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92545>

Thanks for that link!!
I will read and re-read before next week. I am already doing some of those supplements, so am on the right road.

### Raylynn — 2013-08-14T21:42:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92547>

I have a thought for the day. I am trying to see how much carbs and bad food I can give up, instead of thinking what I am giving up. Might not make much sense, but getting my brain right is helping me get over the carb craving. I will not be regretting what I am giving up, instead concentrating on how much I can pass up. Kinda like a challenge to see how much I can say no to.
Last night, I cooked sloppy joe sandwiches for my family with venison and homemade recipe. My husband and son used buns for their meat, but I used pork skins to dip with instead. (Please don't berate me for my meal plan just yet. I have to feed my family who are not on the plan, and I find it easier to figure out something different for me instead of the whole family.) Great salad with it and ice cold water to drink.
It was very filling for me, and had a crunch with it to make me feel like I ate something bad.
I hope to do well again today.
7 days til surgery.

### nicld — 2013-08-14T22:59:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92557>

Look up coconut flour tortillas. It might work pretty good for the sloppy joes and well as alot of other meals.

Oh and venison, yummmmmmmmm. I am trying to get some more myself.

### caroline — 2013-08-15T04:06:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92593>

That sounds perfect .....fat is your friend

### Raylynn — 2013-08-16T22:22:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92833>

I did really well yesterday. Went to lunch with DILs and DS at Cheesecake Factory. Had a ceaser salad wtih chicken. No, I did not eat bread, and no I did not eat cheesecake. MIL and DS shared a piece and offered some to me, but I refused. I just kept thinking, look how much I can give up. Thing was, I was full from salad and didn't even finish it. Not really hunger for dinner either. Wow, hope this continues.
I have upped my vitamin D supplements, and have started CT on my foot. Can't go too cold since I have Raynaud's and don't want to freeze off some toes!
With even a week of therapy, I hope that will help me after surgery.
5 days til surgery.

### Raylynn — 2013-08-16T22:23:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92834>

nicld, thanks for the reference of coconut flour tortillas. I'll look that up!

### caroline — 2013-08-17T01:50:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-92871>

I wouldn't think of it as giving up anything - you are gaining so much more ......you are on a journey to optimal health and those things don't fit in with your new life plan. Honestly - I think most here would tell you that they wouldn't even be able to eat a piece of cake etc .... I can't even imagine having a mouthfull....
If you are eating enough fat and protein - your cravings go away .....honest! Why would I want to go back to how I was feeling - always out of control, unhealthy, always bloated, always fat and unhappy ....and missing life... I missed so much for way, way too long.....

### Raylynn — 2013-08-20T16:51:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-93228>

Caroline, you are right. I am gaining so many things. Health for one. Energy that I haven't had to sprint with my DS. Wonderful sleep, that leads to better cognition and fruitful days. The cravings are almost gone. And I am not thinking about food as much as before. Being able to focus on other things and actually getting things accomplished is a real eye opener.
Thanks for the encouragement!

### Raylynn — 2013-08-20T16:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-93229>

I had a good weekend. Really got a lot accomplished. Cleaned house, spread mulch in flower beds, washed everything in my house that could be washed, and cooked. Did not sleep alot. My nerves are really getting to me. I am worrying about surgery even though I know it is in God's hands now. Finally, Sunday night I slept and only woke up once. Monday night was even better. Slept all night, and even got up an hour earlier because somehow my clock time got changed. After I woke up my DH and DS, then sent them back to bed to sleep another hour, I did 2 loads of laundry, cleaned out the microwave, read emails, and sat down to eat breakfast. Got to work on time.
I have surgery tomorrow, and pray that I sleep tonite. I am physically ready, but not sure about mentally. Again, I just pray that God will take care of me.
Hope to journal again next week.

### caroline — 2013-08-21T01:38:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-93297>

I will be thinking of you........I can't wait for you to be back home and really get into all this .....you are doing great
just imagine where you will be one year from now.... Tell us your plans...

### caroline — 2013-08-21T01:39:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-93298>

You must have the cleanest house in town!!! when something here needs to be cleaned ....I go to the beach instead

### Raylynn — 2013-09-02T20:22:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94690>

Finally able to get online for a few minutes today. Surgery went very well according to surgeon. Took him longer than he thought, but he was able to repair everything and only used 2 screws. I have 2 incisions, and 2 puncture wounds from the screws. Don't mean to be gross, but I am an OR nurse and we tend to talk like this. They put a block in my leg which made it completely numb until 2AM Thursday morning. No pain until then. I came off the narcotics 4 days after surgery and am only using ibuprofin as needed for muscle aches. Having a lot of those with hopping around the house. I can't stand how drugs make me feel, and my foot pain has been minimal. Elevation helps the most with the foot pain. Who'd a thunk it??
So anyway, on to my diet. Very hard to keep paleo when everyone is bringing food and actually fixing a plate and bringing it to you! We have had a lot of carbs, but I am doing my best. I am doing well with the BAB, but after that it gets dicey. Funny how I can't tell my MIL no when she makes me food and brings it to me without asking. Anybody else have that problem? Anyway, my husband is cooking this weekend and will leave leftovers for me this week, including a smoked boston butt. Yummm!!! I am also eating a lot of salads and a piece of fruit each day. Love me some almond butter too. Not perfect, but it is what it is. My motto is, it doesn't have to be perfect, it just has to be. So I try to eat well and fill up on meat and salads, then have a piece of fruit if I am still hungry.
This week will be the first time I will be alone since surgery. Very hard for me to get around, and with only one foot, I can't carry anything. So I will eat in the kitchen at the butcher block, and continue drinking lots of water.
And resting a lot with my foot up. I realized last Friday, a week postop, that I have nothing but time. I have 3 months off, with 2 of those non weight bearing on one foot. Can't get too upset. I will rest and do what I can.
One great thing, my 13yoDS has been a God send. He has helped me and remembers what I need without me saying anything. He knows what I need at bedtime and has it ready for me before I get there. I have profusely thanked him, and hope he will remember that I love him and this is only temporary.
I am getting tired with my foot down, so I must go rest!

### caroline — 2013-09-03T00:31:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94707>

I am so happy to hear how well you are doing! you have a great attitude ....that is invaluable on this crazy life journey we are on. It is really hard when a loved one is bringing you food. But I think that you do need to have a heart to heart with her soon and explain your plans for taking your life back. You so appreciate everything she has done but you need to reclaim your life and tell her how you plan to do it so she kind of understands. These are the choices you are making and you won't compromise. People need to respect that ...and if they don't....too bad....this is your decision and your life .....

### nicld — 2013-09-03T04:24:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94721>

Good to hear that it went well. A friend just had foot surgery and she too has to stay off of it for 8 weeks. That would drive me NUTS. Wishing you speedy healing and patience.

Nicole

### Raylynn — 2013-09-03T20:18:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94749>

Thanks for the reply Caroline. I have explained in the past to my MIL, but she is die hard nofat/nocholesteral diet and will not recognize any other way. Luckily, I am much more self sufficient now and she will not be here any more. Now, I can eat a BAB without problems, and a great lunch by myself.
Speaking of, I had a good bkfast, and will reheat some boston butt with a salad for lunch. I continue to take L-glutamine in the AM, and feel a little stronger today. A big problem I am seeing is not getting enough sleep. I am still on my work schedule, so have to go to bed about 8:30, and then up at 6:00. My DH and DS get up at 6, so I need to force myself to sleep at 8 or 9. Feel much more energetic when I get 8 hours of sleep. And I am dreaming vividly again, so I know I am sleeping well. Love me some sleep!!
Still taking some ibuprofen for muscle pain, but hope to ease off that as the days go on. Using ice and elevation to help with those muscle aches.
Looking forward to walking and working in the yard soon too. My soul needs the outdoors to feel better, and accomplishing something in the yard helps. And my dogs love to stay outside with me when I am gardening. I have so many ideas for winterizing my beds and working my compost pile, I can hardly stand sitting in side. Was out on the deck this morning watching the hummingbirds and dogs, wishing I could go look at my compost pile. I also need to work the soil around my blueberry bushes with compost, but (sigh) I will get to it soon. Never wanted to work this much when I could!! Now I see everything that needs to be done. Isn't that the way it works, we never seem to be happy just to BE!
Wish me luck since I am going to try to wash my hair by myself in the sink today! Hopefullyl I won't flood the kitchen, nor leave soap or conditioner in my hair!
Good day!

### Raylynn — 2013-09-03T20:24:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94751>

One other thing before I go, saw a quote on Dave Ramsey this AM that hit home with me: Proverbs 3:5-6. Trust in the lord with all your heart, and lean not on your OWN understanding; In all your ways acknowledge Him...
I acknowledge you Lord, I don't understand why I am going thru all this, but I know you do!!

### cantweight — 2013-09-03T20:36:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94752>

My MIL is a fat free everything person. The food at my inlaws just makes me shudder. Diet cranberry juice with splenda, fat free deli turkey, fat free processed cheese, fat free yogurt and sour cream, and artificial coffee flavored fat free creamers...ugh I wish they would eat food!

She acts like i am going to kill her son with bacon and eggs and coconut oil and constantly reminds my husband that heart disease and high cholesterol are in their family...she drives me nuts. For the longest time we subjected ourselves to what they eat when we went there but stopped about 4 years ago. I got sick of detoxing my kids every time they went for a visit...lol!

Hope your hair washing went well and good luck staying off your feet

### Raylynn — 2013-09-04T20:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94815>

Hey cantweight, if you didn't live in Virginia, I'd say you are related to me! Sounds just like my MIL! She has had 4 bypasses, just like her DS, my DH, and is constantly on us about our diet. I also just want to tell her to eat real food!! When we eat there, I make my DS eat some fat before we go so he won't eat a bunch of processed carbs cause he stays hungry. She is a really great MIL, other than her diet. But, like I tell my DS, everyone can have an opinion, and ours differ on diet. He understands and eats well at home and school. He rarely gets sick, even when kids in his class are sick. Everybody to their own.
So proud I got my hair washed, dried, and styled by myself yesterday. I have long hair so don't wash everyday. Feeling much stronger again today. The extra sleep is really helping. I am still getting tired about 7:30, but I just go get ready for bed and am lying down by 8:30. I hope I am more in tune with my body and treating it well.
Had another BAB, and rested, then got up for some internet time. I get my cast off Friday, and am so excited to shave my leg!! I know the girls will understand, even if guys get grossed out. I just don't feel like I am taking care of myself when I can't shave. Okay, enough about that!
Still having muscle cramps from hopping about, and am trying to just use meds first thing in AM, and at bedtime. Using ice in between. I was so physically out of shape before surgery since I had 7 months of no activity before. Feel like an idiot that I get so tired hopping around. Kids hop around on one leg all the time, right? When did I get old?? My hair is not grey, well maybe under the color. I know I will be stronger soon, just very impatient.
Still taking my supplements daily. (Another thing MIL commented about when she was here.) L-glutamine in AM, Magnesium in PM, along with the others that Dr. K recommends. Hope my bones heal fast and ligaments knit well.
Gotta go rest!

### caroline — 2013-09-05T01:10:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-94824>

We feel so much better when our hair is clean and styled!! Keep up the good work!

It is something how stuck to their diet people can be - even when it is not working and they are terribly unhealthy. If people saw how much butter I eat they would be shocked.....actually - I am shocked.

### Raylynn — 2013-09-09T22:27:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-95353>

Having great day today. lots of energy, no pain, getting a lot accomplished. Had a good bkfast, and good lunch, and will have pork chops for dinner.
Had cast and stitches removed last Friday. Had a really tough time with that. Lots of pain, and they put me back in a cast since my doctor wasn't there, and his partner said to. Was hoping for a boot, but stuck again. Had to take a pain pill that night and was very lethargic the next day. Hadn't had a narcotic in 10 days, but couldn't stand the pain. Just needed that one, and I am pain free again. I guess narcotics serve a purpose, but I sure do hate to take them. I don't know how people get addicted to them. Anyway, Back on the mend and hope to be cast free in 10 more days.
On to the important things, how do I get to my hair appointment tomorrow? Very important, right ladies?? I never miss one, and I make them 3 months in advance, or can't get an appointment. My husband won't let me drive with a cast on my right foot, and he is not sure he can get home to take me. I told him I would drive the truck regardless of what he said, but I'm not sure I can get to it or get in it without help. Guess I'll see tomorrow.
Hope to go grocery shopping tonight with DH, and get some fresh okra. Saw the recipe on MDA for grilled okra, and can't wait to try it. DH and I both love it, but I don't see recipes using it without frying.
Will have to either cook more food to have leftovers, or buy some things I can easily cook so I can have a good lunch this week.
See ya!

### caroline — 2013-09-10T00:04:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-95360>

Good luck with your hair app't!

### Raylynn — 2013-09-24T20:59:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-95960>

Have had a rough time the last couple of weeks. Got cast off and now have a removable boot. Easier in some ways, but tough in other ways.
About my diet: Had to take some narcotics for pain relief for a few days. Hate that stuff, as I have said before. Makes me drowsy and lethargic. Now I am better, so I am eating better and sleeping better. I realized that I was still not getting enough fat, so I started drinking some bullion and coconut oil midmorning to help me through the day. Amazing that I am not very hungry for dinner. Still hungry at lunch, but not so much for dinner! I am eating a BAB, then salad and meat and sometimes fruit for lunch, then meat, vege or 2 for dinner.
My sleep has gotten so much better. I am able to sleep on my side without discomfort now. Just like Dr. Kruse has said, sleep makes a huge difference in how we feel. I have more energy, and a better mood when I get enough sleep. I know it helps with healing also. My scars are looking better every day.
I hope to start rehab on Friday, if my heel sore has healed completely.
I am also going to start rubbing my scars with coconut oil. Hope to help scars heal quickly.
Good day everyone!

### caroline — 2013-09-25T00:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-95994>

Glad things are turning around for you! Do you have a magnetico? ....I suppose it would do wonders for healing?

### Raylynn — 2013-09-27T22:25:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-96302>

Don't have a magnetico. But I have started the coconut oil and the scabs and dead skin are coming off now. One scar not quite closed completely. Will continue and see if it helps in the next week.
Got to drive today for the first time. I feel liberated! Went to physical therapy. Not too hard on me yet, but have exercises to do twice daily. Go back Tuesday of next week. She has me doing side to side ligament stretches, and I can feel that tight ligament now. Been wanting it tight for 8 months, and boy is it!! Still a lot of fluid in foot and she massaged to move it out. She showed me what to do so I will be doing that also twice a day. Therapist really made me feel good when she said, wow you are ahead of schedule to start rehab. I told her the doctor said my heel was healed in 2 weeks. That is good for bone healing. I think it was due to my preop diet and supplementing with vitamin C and D. Those have shown to aid in bone growth and wound healing. My pressure sore is getting better each day.
Food wise, I started drinking coconut oil everyday before lunch, but must have started with too much since it really got my stomach moving and I will have to lay off a couple of days. It really cut my hunger pains down, and I was not eating as much for lunch and not much at all for dinner. I will restart that Monday, but with a lower amount to begin with.
Funny story about my DS: He does not want my DH to cook him eggs in the AM because he is not hungry for lunch! But, after he gets home from basketball practice in the after noon, he is starving. I make him eat some protein as a snack before dinner gets ready. I tell him if he eats eggs for breakfast, he won't be so hungry after practice either. He is only 13 and still learning, but he listens to me and tries to eat well. He is the only kid I know that doesn't drink soft drinks. Other parents ask me why he only drinks milk or water, and I tell them he doesn't need the caffeine. He orders water at restaurants and is happy with that. Training from the beginning helps.
Gotta put my foot up.

### Raylynn — 2013-10-04T19:43:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/raylynns-new-healthy-life.7879/post-96880>

Caroline, thanks for the reply, and I never thought about CT for my son. I am only thinking about myself. I will discuss with him and see if he is up to the challenge. Thanks for the suggestion, it is a great one!
My foot is healing really well now. Wounds are pealing, and looking better every day. Again, I contribute that to the supplements and healthy diet. I still have one wound that is draining, but the amount is getting smaller each day. I also wonder if the drainage is due to the swelling in my foot. The swelling is also going down each day. My therapist started with massage, and I do it the same way twice a day. After one week of therapy, I can see my ankle bone on the inside, and the veins on top of my foot. I am working up to 50% weight bearing the next 7 days. Makes it easier to walk since I can balance now. Still don't know when I can return to work as I don't see doc for 2 more weeks. Since I a on my feet all day, I will have to be fully ready for that before returning.
I am not sleeping well due to hot flashes. They started this summer, and waking up 4-5 times a night does not work well for my body. It is vital that my body gets 7-8 hours of uninterrupted sleep a night. If not, I am tired and crave sugar. I slept last night from 10 to 5 and I fell 100% better today. I have more energy, my mood is much lighter, and I can focus easily. People do not realize how much sleep means to our bodies and cells. A friend visit visited me yesterday and said she was so tired. She is very active and exercises daily. I asked her if she was getting enough sleep and she said no. I encouraged her to go to bed early a few days and see if that makes a difference. I hope she does. I will search and see what natural things I can do for the hot flashes.
In the meantime, hopefully I will sleep again tonight.
Later!

### David — 2013-08-12T23:06:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-area-for-relocation.7880/post-92280>

I am convinced that I must move from Northern Virginia and I am considering Northwest Florida. I vaguely remember Dr Kruse stating that the Gulf Coast from Tampa to New Orleans may offer the most benefits, from a health perspective.

Can anyone verify that idea or at least lead me in the correct direction?

I would also like to know what are the most attractive characteristics that I should be considering in selecting a locale. My present thinking leads me to consider Pensacola or a nearby area of the Gulf Coast. Access to saltwater for a daily swim and a large selection of seafood makes that area seem attractive. Opinions would be greatly appreciated.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-12T23:08:21+0300

So, I totally agree that EMF is the Number One Concern.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-13T00:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-area-for-relocation.7880/post-92288>

> [David said:](/goto/post?id=92285)

I have lived in that area before and experienced a hurricane or two; and I experienced a terrible typhoon when stationed on Okinawa, Japan. It's easy to say from my present position, which offers a great distance in both time and miles, but I am less concerned with a weather event than I am with the constant bombardment by EMF in the Washington DC area.
>
> So, I totally agree that EMF is the Number One Concern.

David

I'd caution you - on the survivability of hurricanes here in the US b/c we don't build to the same standards as they do in Oki or Guam to survive typhoons. We haven't seen a Cat 5 make land in the US Since Andrew in 1992. I lived in Guam when Super typhoon Paka came through and set a land speed wind record of 233mph and broke the windometer in the 1st 4 hrs of that lovely storm. building standards are for withstanding both Super Typhoons (Cat 5 hurricanes) and 8.0 earthquakes. in addition to that the land we're building on in the US isn't as stable as coral reef and remnants of volcanic eruptions...

That said - if you're far enough away from the 500 year flood plain and the flood surge - you should be good to go.

We're considering Maine as a possibility, BC north of Vancouver, Utah, upto Yellowstone and on to Glacier national park, as well as Texas. of course we'll be testing, but we're also looking for opportunities to continue being able to pay the bills...

my labs go to crap in summer - most likely field driven - but I can only do hot and humid if I live on an island (think Guam) Okinawa was great, but I had some sever allergies - and migraines there - and If I think about it - there's a heck of a lot more EMF getting pumped out of Oki and into Oki - so that might actually be what was going on - only i didn't recognize it.

The reason I ask this is, my fiance has family out in Poland. We may be able to live with them for free if we were to move out there (or at least very cheaply). I'm worried because of my current HPPD symptoms, but I've got ways to keep that under control; and if I were to relocate to a better 'field', it may help me further. For instance, my fiances cocia (if that's how you spell it, it means grandma) calls off of a land line phone rather than cell phone, and her siblings/nephews can only communicate through skype off of a older desktop computer.

### Eve — 2013-08-14T04:20:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92455>

...There was a credible, longitudinal study that followed adolescents who smoked weed into adulthood. Their findings...Their IQ on average dropped 10 points (if I am remembering this correctly, but I do remember it was a significant amount). This may not mean much if you are a genius with an extremely high IQ, who can afford to lose 10 points. The average IQ is about 100 with a standard deviation of 15 or so...so approximately 85-115. Borderline intellectual functioning, also called borderline mental retardation, where the person has below avg cognitive ability falls in a range of 70-85. If you have an IQ of 75 at 15 years old, can you afford to lose another 10 pts by the time you are in your 20s?
I've also read studies indicating that chronic use of marijuana makes one more susceptible to psychosis. Additionally, the toxins that are produced by smoking one joint is drastically more than a cigarette. Overall, however, I've learned that the research thus far on the (negative) long-term effects of marijuana are inconclusive and that more research is needed before any definitive conclusions can be made.

### Eve — 2013-08-14T05:21:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92464>

I am aware of clinical studies that have suggested a potential role for cannabinoids for AIDS patients and I can see how they have the potential to be helpful (for this group). However, I wonder how the body can account for potential respiratory damage or even cancer, especially in the immunocompromised AIDS patient, since smoked marijuana is deemed more effective than dronabinol or nabilone (both synthetic forms of THC) according to anecdotal reports. Raw marij. also can contain bacterial and/or fungi, posing a serious risk of infection for this group. (Regular marijuana smoking is associated with an increased risk for bronchitis and microscopic examinations of bronchial biopsies from marij. users have shown several kinds of cellular abnormalties, some of which are considered precancerous).

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-14T05:30:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-on-the-use-of-medical-cannabis-to-fight-inflammation-and-high-blood-sugar.7892/post-92465>

What if one ingests it orally? or transdermally? Smoking is not the only method of ingestion. Those studies showing degraded iq could be propaganda from the pharmaceutical companies that don't want to lose a ton of money. Cannabis helps me when I blow out my back. It has happened once every couple months for the past 5 years. I have taken steroidal anti-inflammatory medicine that did work, but sublingual cannabis oil worked much faster and effectively. You aren't suppose to take steroidal anti-inflammatory medication for too long. I've treated myself with much better success than if I were to go to the hospital. When my disc herniates, I can barely walk. If I take sub-lingual or a capsule of cannabis oil immediately upon herniation, I can walk within hours almost like nothing had happened. There is nothing that compares to the effectiveness and lack of toxicity in my opinion.
