### Nathan — 2013-10-16T03:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-97945>

Been continuing to do CT. Had some good sessions over the weekend. Saturday was 57F and rainy so I went in my back yard in just my swim trunks and flipflops for 35 minutes. I have to say it was a lot easier than doing the tub. I also did 30 minutes in the tub on Sunday and on Monday and I'm getting ready to hop in the tub here in a few minutes.

Sleep was not good last night. I forgot to take my desmopressin and had to get up to pee twice so that was the main reason. I am definitely sleeping deeper though. I went to bed around 10:30 and woke up to pee and thought it was the early morning but it was only 11:30. Not ideal but I am glad to be sleeping deeper.

I ordered a 23andme test for my wife and me so interested to see what that looks like.

Reading the CT series now so it's keeping me motivated to take my baths. Great stuff!

Hi Nathan,
My most difficult to shake symptoms are fibro type pain deep in muscles and cognitive symptoms. I still need to pace really carefully.
CT, being strict about avoiding blue light post sundown, and a Ketogenic diet (after Leptin reset) together were the winning combination for improving things in my case.
I improved fitness, respiration, autonomic function, sleep...stamina...probably a few other things. I have to keep those things up to maintain the gains I've made so they're a lifestyle thing, which is fine! No pharmaceuticals. Go me!

### Nathan — 2013-10-19T19:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-98346>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=97944)
>
> Thanks Nathan .....I do have to get some and just go for it......good luck with yours!
>
> Are you feeling better? any progress you care to share?

Hi Caroline,

My oysters were great and can't wait to eat some more!

My health continues to improve slowly. I've definitely been feeling better overall however occasionally I have a bad day. I think at this point it's a matter of my hormones continuing to balance and going from good most of the time to great all the time. Sleep is still my missing link although it has improved a lot. I still wake up once on average during the night and have been waking at 4:30-5am on most days. I'll be tuning into the upcoming sleep webinar for sure.

I'll have my alpha MSH retested at some point but I'm guessing it continues to climb. I have much less pain than I used to and my sense of well being has really improved.

Still doing CT. I've managed to stay in for 35 minutes in 53-54F water with an icepack on my torso. Hopefully I can get up to 40 minutes by next week. I am noticing slow changes. I walked my dog this morning in shorts and a t-shirt and it was in the high 50sF and felt great. I'm actually looking forward to the temp continuing to drop.

I had a dr appointment the other day and told my dr I was done with antibiotics and she backed me given the overall improvement in my symptoms and put me on Banderol and Samento, which are herbal. It does feel good to be off antibiotics and hopefully I'll be off all pharmaceuticals very soon. I also started drinking again. Not a lot but a few glasses of wine on the weekends. I did miss my wine! What a difference in makes with dinner on Friday and Saturday night.

Still haven't had my HS-CRP tested. For some reason my doctor has resisted doing it. I asked her about it on my recent visit and she finally said ok and said she would look up the code and they actually draw blood at her office. I got the test back and she pulled the wrong code and tested my CRP-quant. Soooo I went to Lab Corp's website and looked up the correct code and sent it to her and will get it in a month or so when she checks my blood again.

I need to start meditating again. I haven't done so well with it in the past but I really want to give it another shot. Just need to get back in that groove.

My MiL has lost 9 lbs on the diet so that is great and she has also decreased the amount of insulin on her insulin pump. This in the span of 2 weeks so that is just amazing!

My wife continues to shed weight. Most of her clothes are now really loose on her...the down side of weight loss...having to buy new clothes.

I am almost back to 160. I was stuck in the low 150s for months so I'm very happy about that. I can wear my wedding band again without fear of losing it. My goal is to get back to 170 at some point. This past week my appetite has been huge so that is helping me get there. Looking more and more like my old self every day.

Soooo...what is new with you? Still soaking up lots of sun on the beach and checking out the hot men? ;-)

### caroline — 2013-10-19T23:53:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-98362>

Hi Nathan ... yep still soaking up the sun ...and checking out the hot men! It is not so much that they are hot - just very vibrant......unfortunately these are the young men - the older men \[my age\] always seem half dead ....extremely uninteresting and uninspiring and, might I say, misshapen ........

You are making great progress - I am very proud of you .....just imagine where you will be 6 months from now

I understand completely your wife's shrinking body....on one hand, it feels so fantastic .....but on the other, it is pretty costly. I have gone thru 3 different sets of clothes now in the last 16 months.

Everything has changed ...... mind, body, spirit .... what is not to love about this journey.......

### Nathan — 2013-10-20T19:28:11+0300

Caroline,

Once again I must thank you for helping me keep things in perspective. Your comment made me reflect on where I was 6 months ago and I was in a very different place. I have always been a very social person but for the past year I have been in a bit of a personal hibernation from my friends. I just didn't have the energy or desire to socialize. My wife would have to drag me along to social events and I just wanted to chill at home and watch a movie. I'm now back to an active social life and spending a lot of time with friends and doing various activities that I hadn't done for a while. It feels good.

Looking back just 6 weeks I have come a long way. Sometimes I loose track of that and want to be 100% immediately. Well...I can only imagine where I'll be in 6 months.

And look at you...3 different sets of clothes in 16 months! I hear you with the cost aspect but that is just fantastic! I am so happy for you! Well put...mind, body, spirit...what is not to love about this journey. I may have to use that myself

### Nathan — 2013-10-20T19:32:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-98422>

Doing my bath today I started to shiver uncontrollably just 5 minutes into my bath. I didn't feel that cold but I was just shivering away. I did make it 35 minutes although barely. Still hoping for 40 within the next 5 days or so.

I did not sleep well at all last night. No bath yesterday so maybe my body was missing that. I went to bed at 10:30 and woke up at 1:30 and had to use the bathroom. I checked the time after and was thinking it was 4:30-5, the time I've been waking but what do you know it was only 1:30. Tossed and turned for a while and was pretty restless the rest of the night. Hopefully today's bath leads to a solid nights sleep.

### caroline — 2013-10-20T23:23:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-98437>

Hi Nathan..... I always find now - if I really think about it...... a definite correlation between my sleep and what I have done during the day. Sometimes on my computer way too much ..... TV really bothers me - seems to be the flickering I am discovering. My ipad puts my whole system in overdrive and I don't know if you read about my encounter with a copper bracelet. I have now taken off all adornments!!!

A really big thing for me is eating early. I feel great if I eat around 3:30 - 4:30 pm - last night it was around 5:30 - 6:00 because I had been out. That seems to be way too late for me - I struggled all nite. I also do way better if I eat over the space of an hour .... sounds crazy I know ...

Just some random thoughts....

I am so glad you are interacting with friends again .......really important for your wellbeing.....ups the happiness quotient

### Nathan — 2013-11-07T04:07:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-100035>

It's been a while since I've posted. Just been busy with work and always seems to be lots of stuff on the weekends. Plus after being on the computer all day at work I usually have no desire to get on at night.

I would say overall I am continuing to see improvement. My sleep has been improving too and still needs some work. I have been getting to bed around 10 lately and I fall asleep pretty easily and I sleep solidly as far as I can tell until 4:30 - 5am and then I'm wide away and can't go back to sleep. If I could just make it to my alarm at 6:30 I would be a happy man. My sleep quality has come a long way so I am making progress.

I am also noticing that I am adapting to cold. I was able to get up to 40 minutes in the tub last weekend. I was also outside last weekend all day and into the night. We were at an outdoor festival and it was a beautiful day in the 60s and sunny. The temp dropped to around 50 later in day. I was fine and definitely felt some pride as I saw people putting on their jackets. We then took a water taxi back to my friend's house and everyone on the boat was freezing with their jackets on. I was still in my t-shirt and felt fine. I also drove to work a few days last week with the windows down and it was in the 40s in the morning and it actually felt pretty good.

So life is good and I am definitely on the right path. I've been spreading the word and am definitely getting some people interested. My wife's friends are even more interested because they have never seen my wife this skinny. She has really noticed a difference in how she feels as well.

I looked into getting a mold test done but we knew the person and they recommended we get our vents cleaned first so that is scheduled for about 2 weeks from now. I noticed that when I slept at my friend's house last weekend that I did not wake up stuffy. Sooo...I'm thinking my vents are really dusty or maybe they have mold in them but getting them cleaned should make a big difference. We'll see.

Not much new other than that. All I can say is thanks JK, your stuff works and I'm a believer. I just need to get more of my friends to do it so they can be optimal with me.

### Nathan — 2014-01-16T04:53:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-105860>

Happy New Year! It's been a while since I've logged on to this site. Far too long. I've been dealing with some major life changes. My wife and I separate at the end of November so I've been dealing with that. As a result my sleep has been horrible and I just haven't had much extra energy to do what I need to do. Anyway, I had my pity party for myself and now I am moving on.

I recently returned from a week in St. Lucia. I was up in the mountains and spent some time on the beach and in natural hot springs and it was all very beautiful. I thought it would be very restful however I barely slept the whole time. I did manage to eat pretty clean and did a lot of earthing on the beach, in the ocean, and on the mountain. I also cooked up some local fish and veggies and that was really good. Due to some recent hurricanes the fish was limited but what I was able to get was very fresh and tasty.

Since I've been home my sleep has actually been improving. I think I finally just wore my body down so much that it just gave in to sleep. Hopefully that continues. This is a new year and this is my year to get really healthy and back to 100%. I'm starting to work out some more and need to start doing CT more regularly. It was pretty sporadic over the holidays and now I plan to start doing it consistently again. I also plan on finding a good endocrinologist to fine tune my hormones. I think that's my one missing link.

I definitely indulged in bad food over the holidays but have been back on track as of late and plan on staying that way.

I hope everyone else is doing well!

### caroline — 2014-01-16T05:28:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-105862>

Hi Nathan .... I am so happy to see you back here. I am so sorry you have had a tough time. It is time now to totally focus on yourself and live the optimal life that you deserve .... That we all deserve.

I never did get to St. Lucia .... Always wanted to. I am surprised that your sleep was so bad there - but I suppose you Need time to process stuff.

Dr. Tim Jackson is going to be doing a program with consults etc. maybe email Misty if you're interested.

May I just say that you have always been so lovely when you have posted. I always appreciated your kind words...... And I have missed you.

### Nathan — 2014-01-17T06:29:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-105987>

Hi Caroline,

Thanks and it's good to be back. So how have you been? Still enjoying nice strolls on the beach? What else have you been up to? Did you enjoy your holidays? And thank you for your never ending support and kindness! I hope you are well and enjoying your journey to optimal.

I forgot to mention I finally got my HS-CRP and it is 3.9. I was surprised it was so high considering all of the progress I've made. Not sure why I still have so much inflammation. I've been going heavy on chlorella in an attempt to detox. I think it's helping because my sleep is better when I do that. I've also been eating lots of sauerkraut and ume boshi to fix my gut. I actually got the idea to take a lot of detox agents before bed from Dr. Bronli (sp?) of magnetico. I called to say I wasn't haven't good results with my magnetico and that's what he recommended due to all of the antibiotics I was on. He said my body has a heavy toxic load and when I sleep my body has a hard time getting rid of them all which affects my sleep. I have to say it has helped. He was really nice and spent a lot of time with me on the phone.

I'll keep Dr. Tim Jackson in mind. Ideally I would like to find a local doctor that I can meet with in person. If not I'll look into him.

So at the villa we stayed at in St. Lucia, the owner has a bit of a mystical/healing background. His grandmother new a lot about local herbs to use for healing and people travels many miles for her help. He learned a lot from her. He was a bit of a drinker so I didn't get a ton out of him but his daughter told me about a horribly itchy skin outbreak that she had. She told me her father had her crush turmeric and boil it in water and drink it 3x a day for a month and it did wonders for her. I've been taking turmeric supplements but I think I'll try using the root and making a tea like she did. Should help with inflammation. He was also telling me to drink the water at the hot springs. I asked how much and he cupped his hands and said like this (making drinking motion) three times. The water is high in sulfur and pretty stinky so I wasn't quite ready to try that. I actually did take a tiny sip but it probably wasn't enough to do any good. The hot springs actually did help with some of my minor aches and pains.

### caroline — 2014-01-17T07:04:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-105989>

Hi Nathan. I am just fine - thankyou for asking. We are having beautiful weather - 28C today with clear blue skies. The sun is very strong this year because of the donut hole so I finally bought myself a sun umbrella yesterday! I need to start being more careful.

How interesting about the herbs.....If you look up an early post by Ricardo - he told us about a anti cancer brew with tumeric. It is in the epi Paleo diet thread.

Have you signed up for the free 90 day reset? Dr. Jackson will be somewhat involved it seems and Dr. Bonlie too.

One thing that Dr. Jackson said that really stayed with me ....sometimes when you are trying to detox and not methylating properly - you could just be recirculating all the toxins back into your body/cells/tissues. He said he has seen people very sick and not able to get out of bed for a couple of weeks. Anyway - food for thought!

your hsCRP is high ... you have work to do! At least you are sleeping better..... I seem to think if we start sleeping better - other things will fall into place.

How is your non native EMF situation?

### Nathan — 2014-01-28T05:21:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-107110>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105989)
>
> Hi Nathan. I am just fine - thankyou for asking. We are having beautiful weather - 28C today with clear blue skies. The sun is very strong this year because of the donut hole so I finally bought myself a sun umbrella yesterday! I need to start being more careful.
>
>
> How is your non native EMF situation?

Hi Caroline,

Glad to hear you are well and enjoying your walks. Yes, better watch out with that donut hole.

I've done what I can to reduce my non native EMF but living in the DC area it's hard to escape. I keep my wifi off for the most part and hard wire to my modems, I don't have much plugged in my room but still need a kill switch for my circuit breaker. At work is another story. My entire building has wifi so there's no escaping it. Hopefully my ice baths and magnetico are helping mitigate my exposure.

I haven't signed up for the 90 day reset. I've been super busy with work so have been slacking somewhat. Things should slow down in a month or so and then I can get started doing additional things to improve my health. are you doing it? What have you tried/likes so far?

You're right, I'm not a good methylator. That's why I take a ton of chlorella every night. I think it's helping but sometimes it's hard to say. I also got some detox foot pads and have been using them nightly for the past 2 weeks. They seem to be working too. I'll just keep at it. I have made turmeric tea a few times and it's pretty good. I need to get some gloves though because it stained my fingers pretty bad! I was afraid people would think I took up smoking with my yellow fingers. Seems to have finally come off after multiple hand washings.

So have you tried oysters again? I had some tonight and they sure where good.

Ok, take care and talk soon!

I feel so much better when I have mostly uninterrupted sleep, it's the universal panacea I'm sure and quite possibly the reason why folk fall ill and find it difficult to recover. I'm watching with interest the thread of the person who bought the Earthpulse. It's a lot of money but nowhere near as expensive as the Magnetico. I have looked at a few diverse reviews and it doesn't seem to work for everyone unfortunately. I have a granddaughter who desperately needs to improve her sleep pattern and wonder if it would work for her, it's a lot of money but worth it if it did. The usual dilemma I guess, money v results and no certainty it will work.

Thanks sooperb.

Yes, it's a difficult call. When you can't sleep you will do just about anything to improve it. I was starting to sleep well but the past few nights not so much. Magnetico does allow returns after 6 months (they want you to give it at least that long to work) and I think you loose 1 month's payment and have to pay return shipping so there is that to consider. I haven't heard anything about Earthpulse. Has she tried herbs such as passion flower or valerian root? I've had pretty good luck with valerian in the past and ran out so need to get some more. I haven't tried passion flower yet but have heard good things about it and have some ordered. I'll keep trying...

### Nathan — 2014-05-14T05:22:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-126820>

I've been meaning to post to my journal forever and am finally getting around to it.

I went through a bit of rough patch and took a few steps back but things are heading in the right direction again. I had a stretch where I was feeling super tired in the morning for the first few hours so I did another ASI and my Cortisol was way low. My doc diagnosed me with stage 3 adrenal fatigue. Here are my numbers:

Morning Cortisol: 11 ref range: 13 - 24
Noon Cortisol: 3.4 ref range: 5 - 8
Afternoon Cortisol: 2.9 ref range: 4 - 7
Nighttime Cortisol: 1.2 ref range: 1 - 3
DHEA-S Average: 4.9 ref range: 2 - 10
Cortisol/DHEA-S ratio: 3.8 ref range: 5 - 6
Melatonin (bedtime): 11 ref range: 12 - 35

She put me on 25 mg of Pregnenalone 2x a day and 25 mg of DHEA 1x a day. It's been about 3 weeks and I'm slowly feeling better and sleep has been improving.

I think my adrenal fatigue can be attributed to the divorce I'm going through. It's been harder on me emotionally than I wanted to admit but I think I'm finally making progress and am feeling better. I also started drinking more than I should be. I only drink on the weekend but I was getting too comfortable and having a few too many glasses of vino. I had a good friend in town that I haven't seen in years so I did have a few drinks last weekend but that will be it for a while.

I've been increasing my CT. I was only doing about 2x a week before for 30 minutes and now I'm doing about 5 x a week for 35-40 minutes in 45-50 degree water. I seemed to have plateaued and can only make it to 40 minutes on occasion. I'll stick with it though.

There's also good news. I started working out again doing crossfit and yoga back in February. I hadn't worked out in over a year and it feels so great to be working out again and has been a great confidence boost. My weight is now in the low 170s. I was stuck around 150 for a long time and looked completely emaciated. Nearly all of my weight gain has been muscle so the epi-paleo rx has helped. My white blood cell count has also been inching up and has been about 5 the past few times I've had my blood drawn. I just need to feel as good on the inside as I look on the outside and I'll be in great shape.

I dug into my 23andme results and have the following MTHFR defects:
C677T +/-
A1298C +/-
A2756G +/-
BHMT-02,04,08 +/-
VDR Taq +/+
MAO AR297R +/+
CBS A360 +/+

My doc recommend Sparga to help with sulphur detox and also has me on methylated folate and N-AC. I have been eating a lot of cysteine rich foods as well and have done a few glutathione ivs and suppositories. Also put me on some adaptogens for my adrenals.

Has anyone tried B-12 injections? I take it sublingually but may try the injections for an extra boost.

I've also been re-reading some of the EE blog series and need to re-read the Redox rx. Sticking with the program and getting back to business. Not that I strayed too far but time to focus again.

I've listened to most of the webinars and they have been great and are such a huge help. I'll listen to them a few more times and they really make my commute to and from work a joy.

Let's see...I have also started journaling. Have been at it for a few weeks and it's pretty good and helps keep me positive. Now I just need to start meditating .

I'll plan on doing a better job of keeping this up to date moving forward.

Hi Caroline,

I've checked out most of the webinars...I ended up buying access so I could download all of them. I got behind because I have been doing crossfit on Tuesdays and Thursdays and yoga on Wednesdays after work and have been pretty beat after that so didn't have the motivation to do anything else. Starting to feel better again so I'll be more current on the blog. Speaking of which, I saw that you have a guest blog post. That is so great! I look forward to reading it in the next few days.

Thanks for the recommendation...I'll check out Ricardo's post as well.

I've been eating my share of oysters...I sure do love them! I love all shell fish but oysters of definitely my fave. I love shrimp too but they've been pretty pricey here so I haven't had them much. They tend to get cheaper in the summer so I'll be loading up soon.

I will be heading to the Gulf but not until July. I have some friends in Naples, FL so I'll be spending some time with them.

So what else is up with you? Is your health where you want it to be?

N

Hi Caroline,

Glad to hear you are doing well! I did listen to the Ben Greenfield webinar and recently listened to Bhavesh Patel's and both were really great. I'm a pretty regular listener of Ben's podcast too. I'll check out the others as time permits.

I made it through Friday and Saturday without drinking. I actually went to bed shortly after 10 last night. I fell asleep pretty easily however I woke up at 4:30 and couldn't fall back asleep. Sleep is still my main issue.

I had some new labs run recently so interested to see the results.

Did 30 minutes in the tub at around 45 yesterday and getting ready to hop in after my journal update.

Have had a lot of shell fish this weekend and made some new bone broth and have consumed some of that. I went for a long walk in the sun with my dog and did most of it barefoot. I drink a ton of RO water and have been minimizing non-native emf as best I can. I also started cold laser therapy to help with my detox. I've done 2 sessions and have no idea if it's working. The woman did some emotional exercise where she had me do visualizations and tapped on my head to break the connection to negative thoughts. She said most people have incredible sleep after that therapy. Not so much for me. I'll give it a few more tries..

I am slowly coming to the realization that I may need to move. I'm giving myself the rest of this year and if I continue to be stuck I'll bite the bullet and move to a more ideal geography. Originally, I thought I could never move but I need to put my health and well-being first and foremost. After all...I am worth it!  I'm just getting tired of being tired and not being optimal. Every now and then I have glimpses of what I should feel like and think I've turned a corner and then I fall back.

Going to a party this afternoon and am bringing some Kavita's to drink to help avoid temptation. But before that I have a date with my bathtub

N

### Nathan — 2014-05-21T05:23:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-127762>

Last night I slept through the night for the first time in a very long time. I did 30 minutes in the tub at around 48F and went straight to bed. It probably took me a good half hour or more to warm up. I just have a light blanket on my bed and had the window open and fan on. Once my shivering subsided, I fell asleep pretty fast and didn't stir until my alarm at 6:30am. I was even dreaming when my alarm went off. That is definitely progress for me. I did wake up feeling tired and groggy however. Still need to work on getting my cortisol levels up.

I'll be going camping this weekend so it will be a nice getaway from nn EMF and I'll be sure to do a lot of grounding. There's a lake so maybe I'll be able to jump in and do some CT as well

Was at a work meeting tonight at a restaurant and everyone was drinking and I was good and didn't drink any alcohol. It's actually a lot easier this time around to not drink. When I quick before it was so hard to so no and now it's no biggy. I did cheat a little and ate some flatbread pizza. My legs were a little sore from working out and after eating some carbs I could feel them a lot more. Amazing how sensitive I am to carbs now that I'm off them for the most part. I ate some fish and cauliflower when I got home to make up for it.

Time for bed and hoping for another solid night's sleep..

### Nathan — 2014-05-28T04:22:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-128644>

I had a nice time camping. Did some grounding, soaked up lots of radiant heat from the camp fire, and had a nice long hike. Was nice being in the great outdoors away from the city, wifi and other non-native emf.

Sleep has been pretty mediocre lately. I did 30 minutes in the tub at 45F before going to bed last night and was really hoping for a good nights sleep. I woke up 3 times to go to the bathroom however so that was not ideal. While these baths haven't done much for my sleep they have improved my strength. When I used to work out pretty hard in college my max bench press was 225 lbs. The other night in my strength & conditioning class we maxed our bench press. We don't even do bench normally in class. I was going up by increments of 20 lbs at first and then 10 until I couldn't do anymore. I had no idea what I maxed out at but when they added up the plates I had done 235 lbs. So without doing bench press my max went up. I was completely amazed.

I read the Organization Structural Failure 1 blog post while in the tub and really liked it. Here I was chewing my food well my whole life only to learn I should be doing the opposite. Well it sure did come in handy tonight when I had around 10 minutes to eat before heading to my strength & conditioning class. Don't know about swallowing oysters whole though. I just love the taste too much but maybe I'll swallow every other one whole as a compromise.

Off to the tub and a reread of Org Structural Failure 1 and hoping for a good nights sleep..

hi Caroline,

I actually did 2 baths yesterday. Both around 50F for 35 each. For some reason it's really hard for me to stay longer than 35 minutes. I've lasted 40 minutes a few times but that's rare. Anyway, my second bath was around 5pm and I still had to get up to use the bathroom a few times. I think I'm just detoxing a lot.

### Nathan — 2014-06-03T03:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-129443>

Not much new to report. I've been eating well and do cold baths 5-6 days a week. I had some oysters and a lot of mussels over the weekend and more mussels tonight and shrimp for breakfast and I ate some of the shell and tail. Ummm...love that chitin. I haven't been quite as tired in the mornings. I seem to be improving in very small increments, like 2-3% a week. I also feel fine after my hard workouts where before I would be pretty beat. I am so glad I started working out again and it that has really helped me mentally and physically.

I've been thinking more and more about moving and am starting to think through doing just that within the next few months. I just need to figure out where to move to and what I want to do for my next career. Something along the lines of health coach. Just need to work through the details. It will be a major change and is kind of scary but also very exciting too. More to come..

### Nathan — 2014-11-02T18:18:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-145931>

Hello folks! A lot has changed in the past few months. Let's see, my last day of my job was on 8/1. After that I visited my family for a week and spent the rest of the month of August getting my house ready to put on the market. Had to do a bunch of painting and mostly superficial repairs. I went to Asia for 3 weeks in September to visit a friend in Macau and spent time in Hong Kong, Thailand, and Vietnam. I fell in love with Thailand and plan to move there at some point.

When I returned from Asia I purged and got rid of a ton of stuff that I hadn't touched for some time. It was very emotional digging up a lot of old memories from my marriage and life for the past 15 years. It did feel good to get rid of a lot of stuff I don't need. Trying to become more of a minimalist, which isn't easy for a life long pack rat. My house is now on the market and once it sells I'll be moving to Ithaca, NY. I've been in the DC area for the past 20 years so it will be a big change. It's close to my family though and Ithaca has a decent amount of diversity and access to quality food.

I spent some time up there and found a beautiful house 10 miles from downtown with an amazing view and you actually can't see any other houses from it. There is not much of a cell phone signal so I will greatly reduce my EMF exposure, which is a major reason for my move. Now I just need my house to sell . I'll also have lot's of exposure to cold.

I started a year long program with the Institute for Integrative Nutrition to become a health coach in October. Very exciting and glad to be heading into a new phase of my life. I spend my time studying, reading books about great people, watching/listening to podcasts, cooking, and working out. I'm actually just as busy as I was when I had a full time job. My state of mind is so much better though and I have finally found my purpose.

Overall my health has continued to improve. I'm still off all prescriptions and have greatly reduced the number of supplements I take. My sleep has improved a lot and there's still plenty of room for improvement. It goes in phases and sometimes I'll sleep really great and other times not so great. I had to pack up my aquarium chiller for showing my house so have been missing my cold baths but it's cooled off here in the DC area so getting a fair amount of cool air exposure. I'm looking forward to the cold NY winter.

I hope everyone else is in good health!
Nathan

### Nathan — 2015-08-23T22:06:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-175462>

Since moving to the country in upstate NY my health has had some ups and downs. When I first moved here in December 2014 I was going through a divorce that went from bad to worse. My health declined from December through April/May. I was completely stressed and upset from my divorce dragging out, my legal bills going through the roof, and was not in a good place mentally. I was getting chronic cold sores, which was even more horrible than normally getting a cold sore is, since I was in a new town and trying to meet new women to date. Then in May I met and fell in love with an amazing woman. We are still together and things are going great (and I haven't had a cold sore since). My divorce finalized in May and my life and health continued to improve. Amazing what a toll stress can take on one's body.

My energy continued to improve and my sleep was having incremental improvements though still not great. I had been doing cross fit before moving up here and decided it was just too much for my body and I shifted to lifting weights and doing home exercises like pull ups, burpees, kettle bell swings, and jumping rope. I gained a lot of strength and was feeling and looking great. My diet has been good, though I was eating less seafood, mainly because this area does not have near the selection that I had in DC and it's so much more expensive so I've been eating more grass fed beef and pastured eggs. I do eat an abundance of veggies and they have an incredible farmer's market here so they are local and organic. I drink a ton of reverse osmosis water. I wear my blue blockers at dusk and there is only one cell phone tower within 10 miles of me and no wifi. I have also been getting a fair amount of am sun. The good thing about living in the country is I can walk around in boxer shorts 24x7 and there's no one around to offend (or the wrong people to turn on).

I left my corporate job last August and have started working with clients as a health coach. It has been great and one of the first gifts I buy my clients when they sign up with me are a pair of blue blockers.

About a month or two ago my health started declining again for no apparent reason. My sleep is not as good as it has been, I wake up groggy, and I get really bad upper back pain and pain in my shoulder joints. I wake up with a kink in my neck a lot or just really stiff. My bowels have been soft as well. I believe my candida is back and I have been drinking a ton of pau d'arco tea that I make in a glass jar using the suns rays. That has actually been helping some. I've been pounding a lot of curcumin and resveratrol and I take oregano oil. I've also been eating more seafood in the form or sardines and anchovies along with wild salmon when I can get it for a decent price and oysters now and then.

I was doing my own labs however NY state recently passed a law that requires me to go through a doctor and I can no longer use direct labs so I'm flying a little blind as far as that goes. I just went to a doctor that does Field Control Therapy, which is a combination of bio-resonance and kinesiology and he said I have lyme, mercury, antibiotics, candida, EBV, and various environment toxins in my bone marrow. He gave me about 25 homeopathic medicines that I'll be taking over the next 10 days. I didn't think it possible but this guy seems to hate EMFs even more than Jack.

Anyway, I've decided to sleep in a tent in my yard for the next 10 days to further reduce my EMF exposure. I'll be pounding a ton of seafood and getting as much am sun as possible and plan on reading a lot and keeping my pc use down to 2 hours a day (or is close to that as possible).

**Question:** I'll be sleeping on an air mattress in my tent. I've tried sleeping on the ground before when camping and I am so uncomfortable and end up getting no sleep. Is there a benefit to me dragging my magnetico out in the tent or do I get the same affect from being so close to the ground?

Also, has anyone had success with homeopathic medicine?

Any other recommendations are welcome.

I hope others are doing well on their road to optimal.

Nathan

### Nathan — 2016-04-26T17:36:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-193550>

I'm way overdue for an update. Just joined Gold Club and was listening to a Q&A and am taking Jack's advice on keeping my journal up to date. My health has been pretty good overall or so I thought. I have been feeling good, at least relative to how I had felt in the past. I am sleeping better and have more energy. I recently found a functional medicine doc in my area and she ran some labs and they tell another story. My vitamin D, DHEA, Testosterone, and free Testosterone are all very low. This doesn't make sense to me because I have very good muscle tone. I also ran some labs on myself a few months before then and my Hemoglobin A1C was 5.8 and I've been low carb for close to 2 years now. I test my fasting blood glucose and it's consistently around 80-85 so not sure what's going on there.

My HS-CRP was high in my recent labs from 3-4 weeks ago but I had food poisoning the night before so I think that's a false reading. I'm not sure how much it would throw off the my other labs but I wouldn't think by much. My HS-CRP was .3 the time before so I think my inflammation is in check for the most part.

I just had a new ASI run and I have stage 2 AF. I did have stage 3 so this is an improvement. I think I was normal for a while but I think I got sloppy with fake light. I do have a positive slope so that's good but my cortisol is below the optimal reference range at all 4 points. My DHEA is within range but not top 25%.

Anyway, I've made some changes. I started doing CT again. It has been a few years and when I finally did it I thought to myself, what have you been waiting for. Even now I always make it out to be so much harder in my head but then I do it and I enjoy it. I was doing it 3 days a week in 50F for 35-40 minutes. It's been a week and I'm overdue...just had a lot going on and I shiver for 45-60 minutes after so it can be a big time commitment. I've been spending more time outdoors. First thing in the morning I go outside, ground, stare at the sun and do Wim Hof style breathing. I also focus on positive emotions like being confident and this really helps me start my day off well.

I recently got a pair of blue blocker prescription lenses (Costco sells them now and they are very reasonable) and wear those anytime I'm around fake like. I'm on the computer a lot and I have flux on all the time and have some blue blocking screens so I should be pretty well protected there. I also got a laptop so I can do work outside when it's nice. It's raining now so I'm on my desktop.

I finally admitted to myself that I had a serious addiction to Netflix. I originally signed up for Netflix because I love movies. Somewhere along the way I started watching series. And I have not stopped since. I would usually watch 1-2 shows a night but I realized that every time I would finish a series I would have to find a new one. I quit cold turkey on Season 2, Ep. 8 of Dare Devil. It wasn't that great of a show anyway. It's been 5-6 days with no TV and I am starting to think better. These shows have a way of getting in your head. At night instead of tv I'm reading more and listening to positive things like Jack's webinars and self improvement people like Brendon Burchard and Michael Bernoff.

I have had some emotional drama in my life and I'm trying to manage it as best I can. In addition to the fake light I think that's why my ASI was starting to tank again.

Overall my health is much better than it was a year ago and way better than 2 years ago. I feel the best I have in as long as I can remember and I also know I'm not even close to my potential. Especially after seeing my labs. It's funny how us humans can so easily adapt to our new normal. This stuff works. I am very excited to be getting more UV now that's it's getting warmer out.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-26T18:07:56+0300

### Nathan — 2016-04-29T17:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-193823>

One thing to note. I've been grounding/sun gazing in the morning for about 3 weeks now and my cravings have completely disappeared. I didn't really have cravings that much for a while but then about 2-3 months ago they started. My weakness is ice cream and I was having some on most nights along with a sliced apple or some nuts. My cravings are gone and I have a pint of unopened ice cream in the freezer that I have zero desire for.

My sleep has taken a nose dive lately. Funny that I cut out evening tv and my sleep gets worse. I think it may be from my deep breathing releasing toxins in my body. I started the Wim Hof method a week ago so that may have something to do with it. Last night was really bad and it felt like I had an adrenaline surge. I could feel my heart beating faster. I'm increasing my water consumption to flush everything out and hopefully my sleep gets back to normal soon.

I've been doing 30-40 minute walks in the early evening on most days too and that has been a great stress relief.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-29T20:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-193847>

Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. If it reduces the quantum yield in your food, you better believe it reduces the quantum yield in your environment. The net effect, in plain English: if you are living in a low quantum yield environment, you have to work harder/do more to be healthy. Our cells, specifically mitochondria, read and react to our environmental choice we ALLOW; this includes those forced upon you by others. It shapes us and we are shaped by it. The six best healers in the world that I know: 1. Sunlight 2. Un-fluoridated water 3. magnetism/grounding to Earth 4. seafood 5. Self Confidence 6. Friends/supportive spouse That is all...........Carry On. [http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fsrep12029&h=hAQGdTPtr) Here is the key that most of the critics miss..........the order of the recipe is critical to the success. Your perception will be thrown off if you do it other ways. I can think of 3 recent members who went of the rails this way. The last 4 series pointed this point this out but few understand the implications. Neil Billeaud and I have been offline talking about it. Your BUN/creat ratio and ketosis must be started before DHA loading. The blogs have been clear that the exclusion zone inside of cells manifests due to proteins within becoming hydrophilic first. People understanding of the recipe construction have not been correct. How do you do you make things hydrophilic? You slowly add in electrons. The way to add them is step 4 above. The closer you are to the equator means you have more push from your current (DC) and then you may not need as much........unless there is something peculiar about your environment, job, eyes, skin.

I decided to document my Wim Hof breathing method course for those that are interested in my results.

Week 2 video will be uploaded soon. I've really enjoyed it so far and am glad I finally got started on it since I've been meaning to for some time now..

### Nathan — 2016-06-18T17:36:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-196882>

Life is good though a bit of a whirlwind. I recently moved and had some trips out of town and feel like I'm finally getting caught up and getting back on track. I went almost a week without getting much sun and got between 8-10K IU the past 2 days according to d-minder and today I'll get at least 10K. The next 2 days are a UV of 11 and I have noticed a great difference since I've been soaking up stronger UV and other frequencies from the sun.

I finally had a chance to meet and spend time with Jack along with Rubin, Joe, Dan, Josh, and Devin at Nourish VT. I learned a ton and had a really amazing time.

So I moved from a house in the country to an apartment building just outside of town. I do have more emfs around me, mainly from other people's wifi but aside from that it's not too bad. When the guy came to set up my cable I told him I wanted an old fashioned modem without wifi and he looked at me a little confused and I gave him a lesson. He was interested so we walked outside and I was barefoot and explained grounding to him and talked up the sun. He was around 50 and looked great and told me he had been healthy his whole life but was recently put on blood thinners and was concerned. I told him to get his morning sun, ground, and drink lots of water and he said he could do that.

I did walk around with my trimeter prior to signing the lease and aside from the wifi the electrical and magnetic fields don't register much on my meter. I have a balcony and can see the sunrise coming up over Cayuga lake so I've been getting lots of morning sun which is great. I feel a little weird sitting in the grass in nothing but shorts but suppose the residents will get used to my ways soon enough.

My sleep continues to be solid and I'm wide awake by 5-5:30am. I feel like my testosterone is finally increasing and my morning wood is back with a vengeance. Will be interesting to see my next labs.

I had a recent experience with quantum entanglement. My girlfriend is due to give birth on June 20th. We had a bit of a falling out and there was a 2 month period without any contact except for her finally returning my calls/texts and telling me to leave her alone and stop calling and some other not so nice things. So last Saturday I was heading out of town to go to an alumni reunion for my fraternity. I stopped by a store to grab some Kevita's since I wasn't planning on imbibing much and was putting them on the check out and heard an Oh My God and looked up and it was my girl. I told her she looked amazing. We made some small talk and made our way to the parking lot and talked some more. She was getting hot from the sun so we went to her house and talked for about 3 hours.

We covered a lot of ground. I'm back in the picture and will be a part of my child's life without having to fight for it. It's a huge weight off my shoulders. I have been spending a lot of time working on myself not just on my health but my emotional state and on intentionally feeling confident, happy, and great about who I am. I made a lot of progress but it was overshadowed by the lack of contact with my girl and not knowing how she was doing or the health of the baby.

I was pleased to learn that she has made a ton of positive changes. When things were good with us we did a lot of research into home and natural child birth. Ithaca has a beautiful birthing center and that was the plan. Well some time after I was out of the picture she found out the baby was in breach. She met with a doctor from the birthing center and he told her her only option was c-section. She asked about wiping her vaginal secretions on the baby if that was the case and the doctor said he didn't get involved with things like that so she kindly told him to f off.

She did her research and found a doctor about 50 minutes away experienced in delivering breached babies. She had been doing yoga, acupuncture, hypnosis, manual version, walking down hills, moxa, and some other stuff I can't recall to get the baby to turn. Well last Monday she had a doctors appointment and the baby finally turned so that's a huge weight off her shoulders.

She is a changed women. I'm still working on her with blue light and emfs and that's been a big sticking point. I'm doing my best to give her space and not be overbearing on it. I think she will slowly come around.

We have both learned a lot and grown a lot from this experience. I don't know what will happen between us in the long term and I'm doing my best to take it all one day at a time and am just focused on being there for her and our baby and being a loving and supportive father.

I have 3 trigger words that I focus on every time I see her. Loving, patient, understanding. I used to laugh at people that said patience is a virtue but now I have a new respect for it. Gratitude and listening/reading to self improvement/personal growth got me through the tough times. I reached out to 2 women I recently met that are mothers and they were extremely supportive. I've learned that gratitude is like a muscle and the more you practice the stronger it gets. I have been on a gratitude high since she has come back into my life and am incredibly grateful for all of the great changes she has made and that I will soon have a baby boy.

Time for a walk and some sun.

### Nathan — 2016-07-01T17:54:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-197688>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=196907)
>
> tell us more about Vermont and what you got out of it.....

Caroline,

Vermont was a great experience. I'm grateful that I was able to meet and get to know fellow members from this site and it was awesome spending time with Jack and Ruben and learning from them in person. I arrived at the event about 30 minutes into Beth Lambert's talk on autism and as soon as I walked in I saw Jack and Ruben and said hi. A lot of what Beth was saying was about how our environment has changed and it resonated with a lot of what Jack teaches us and I kept looking at him and Rubin and they were both nodding and smiling away.

Dr. Magda Havas spoke next and her presentation was great and really highlighted the harms of EMFs and while none of it was new to me I was glad to see a lot of the people in the audience nodding their heads and learning something new. She did a demo with an audible RF meter that made different sounds for cell phones, wifi, baby monitor, etc. and I think that really hit home for a lot of people.

Ruben and Jack spoke later in the day and both of their talks were excellent and well received. It was interesting because the event was sponsored by Weston A. Price foundation and none of the talks I saw talked about food. The videos of the talks should be released soon so be sure to watch them.

I got to spend some time with Jack, Ruben, Josh Rosenthal, Dan Neyland, Joe Gavin, and Devin Shey in between talks and we all hung out drinking wine into the wee hours of the night and that was the latest I had stayed up in some time. Beth Lambert happened to come by and she joined us and Jack and Rubin praised what she had right and recommended what she needs to do to bring her message to a wider audience and have a larger impact.

Jack can come across as very blunt and arrogant and when I saw him speak I was concerned his message would be lost. I was wrong. People got what he was saying and were extremely interested. The next morning I stopped by where the event was held on my way out looking for Jack & co. and I bumped into a girl and we started talking and I asked if she saw Jack speak and she missed it so I filled her in on what she missed. She was intrigued and then a friend of hers joined us and she had seen Jack speak and I asked her what she got out of it and she told me drink lots of good water, get lots of sun, avoid blue light, and watch out for EMFs and I was pleasantly surprised that she picked up his key points. She was very intrigued as well.

Since the event I have changed my approach when talking with people and am being more blunt and cutting to the chase and some people are eating it up and some look at me like I'm crazy and I'm not really concerned about them and would rather focus on those who get it and are frustrated with the shit advice they get from CW, reading the latest in Men's Health, whatever latest thing Dr. Oz is spewing, etc.

### Nathan — 2016-07-01T18:17:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-197690>

And meeting Jack continues to impact me to this day. I am super pissed off right now. Well first I have some amazing news, my son was born on June 22 at 3:01pm and I'm so in love with the little guy. He's super healthy and we just had his second appointment with the pediatrician and everything is great. I've spent a ton of time with him since his birth and that's really all I've been doing since he's come into this world.

My son is really healthy but it's really hard being a parent and seeing all the f-ing bullshit recommended and I really feel for parents trying to do their best for their children because most don't question anyone in a white coat. He had a little jaundice and it cleared up with a little sun and I was taking him out for 10-15 minutes at a time a couple times a day. I spend a lot of time with him skin to skin on my chest and am giving him as many of my electrons as I can. He eats and sleeps well and is a happy little guy.

So we told his pediatrician about his jaundice and how the sun cleared it up and he frowned a bit and he's pushing supplemental vitamin D and says zero sun exposure until 6 months and told us all the risks of sun exposure and that we get moles from the sun and they turn into cancer later in life and all this other bs. Luckily my girlfriends father was there and he feels the baby should be getting some sun but it's been a battle with my girlfriend and her sister's a dermatologist and wants us to limit sun exposure as well. Her father's also a retired doctor and he thinks most doctors are full of shit but my gf defers everything to him and her sister and most of what I say falls on deaf ears. But the fact that this pediatrician and most every other tells every mother and father with a newborn this same advice just really set something off and I understand Jack's frustration with CW so much better now.

I asked the doctor what kids did for vitamin D prior to supplementation being available and he brought up rickets and the latest research and I knew there was no arguing with the guy because he read one research paper and that's the gospel to him despite childhood disease exploding in our advanced country and he can't think beyond what he was taught in med school. Then he gave us some papers pushing sunscreen and some other shite advice and I can barely think right now I'm so frustrated.

Her family is very opinionated and I know they want the best for the kid and I'm on the same page with a lot of what they say but most of them are in complete denial about the harms of EMFs and blue light so it's a constant battle. My little boy is only 10 days old and I'm already scared to death of the potential things that can happen to him. I'm so grateful for his health right now and I'm just going to love him and do my best to be patient and get through to my gf and her family.

I've been feeling pretty well despite my lack of sleep and luckily I've had mostly good sleep up until this point and I'm so glad it's the summer and I have strong sun to recharge my batteries.

### Nathan — 2016-07-08T17:48:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-198061>

I had an interesting experience today. I've been doing the Wim Hof method and did it pretty much straight through week 6 however I haven't done it much over the past month due to moving and having a baby.

I have a morning routine and part of that is doing 10-20 deep nasal inhales and holding my breath for 30-60 seconds and there are times when things go black and I get a sort of tunnel vision and get very light headed. This usually only happens in the morning and not throughout my day.

I started up with week 7 of the Wim Hof method this morning and it felt good to get back into it and surprisingly I didn't lose a ton of ground. I did 4 cycles of deep inhales and held my breath on the exhale and then did a 5th round of breathing and did 51 pushups after exhaling all of my air and then I did some stretching and yoga type moves.

I then hit the gym for a quick workout and then spent some time in the sauna and was doing deep breathing of 11 inhales and then doing short breath holds of 5-10 seconds and I was getting this same effect of things going black, tunnel vision, and going to a very deep place mentally, almost like being asleep or in a deep trance. I did this for 30 minutes or so and it felt really good but I'm not quite sure what is happening here. Anyone have any ideas?

### Nathan — 2016-07-08T17:58:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-198062>

I gave a talk last night at my local health food store on how to have more energy and feel great. I talked a lot about getting sun, grounding, drinking lots of water, and avoiding blue light/nnEMF and there was a lot of interest and questions. It was clear that most of the people didn't have the first clue about blue light and EMFs but there were a lot of dots being connected.

The woman who coordinates classes for the store commented afterwards that my talk covered a lot of the same points that I covered in my class on how to reverse Lyme disease and I could tell she was making connections. She commented on how she still listens to an old transistor radio and notices how it gets all staticy when she walks by the new fluorescent lights that her husband recently put in and she said now she understands why and is going to talk to him about switching back to incandescent lights.

Another guy came up after and told me his refrigerator was on the other side of his headboard through the wall and asked what he should do and I was glad to see he made the connection.

### Nathan — 2017-07-12T18:15:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nathans-journey-to-optimal.7907/post-218595>

My energy had been on the up and up and I was doing great. Then I got sick and had a mild fever a month or so back and a really nasty intestinal infection that had me running to the bathroom ever hour or so for 3 nights in a row. It really threw me for a loop. I think I cultured something in my water dispenser due to lack of cleaning. My fault. Will do a better job of keeping it clean.

I was also quantlet free during this time because I left mine with my parents because they needed it more than me. My energy plummeted and I was discouraged to say the least. I was feeling like I was in the dead of winter here. I knew my dopamine was running very low and that was having a big impact on me. I was getting a decent amount of sun and that still wasn't getting me back to where I had been.

I saw a chiropractor I know that does energy work, kinesiology and some other stuff and he has a body scanner called Asyra that he ran on me. He said he picked up some Lyme that may have become active, a poorly functioning ileocecal valve, low dopamine (surprise, surprise), poor synapse function, and some other emotional stuff.

I scheduled a consult with Jack and told him I want to focus on increasing dopamine and he recommended I add in cold with sun and the addition of cold has definitely helped. I've been wearing my ice vest while sunning on my balcony and what do you know but my dopamine is creeping up. The keys for dopamine are sun, cold, and the right people. I've worked on the people in my life too and just doing stuff like reaching out to friends I haven't talked to in a while and that helps as well.

Then last night I got a really great dopamine kick. The power company where I live is rolling out smart meters and I went to a meeting they held where they had a panel of so called experts to allay any concerns of the public. I did my best to channel my inner Jack Kruse. The PhD/Consultant they had there to talk about health was talking a lot of nonsense. I decided to call him out. The first time I felt rather meek and was pretty nervous. I pushed myself forward and called him out some more and a shift happened and I started feeling really bold and confident.

There was another guy there asking really good questions as well and we started tag teaming the PhD and the moderator said since our discussion was getting so technical that the rest of the people probably wouldn't be interested and we should move on and I said no, the people here need to hear this and some of the crowd chimed in and said yes, they wanted to hear it. It wasn't much of a victory because the smart meters are being rolled out regardless but we did get the attention of a few people and that felt really good.

I was hopped up on dopamine for the rest of the night and woke up early today before the sun rise still feeling the dopamine coursing through my veins. I was a little down because I thought of a million other thing I wish I said but also happy that I did speak up even though it was uncomfortable at first. So keep pushing yourself out of your comfort zone and you'll increase your levels of dopamine and feel totally awesome.

# Thread: Sean's very un-optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 458
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-08-15T12:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-92621>

Hi guys, starting this journal in hopes to paint a better picture for those trying to help me and to provide information to anyone who finds themselves in a similar position... and because caroline told me to.

Backstory
Was a bit chubby as a kid. At 16 decided to get rid of the fat by skipping breakfast/dinner for a few months. This worked-ish and I returned to normal eating and was a stable skinny fat for the next few years.

Around 19 I was getting my life in order so I decided I should eat healthy. Got recomended mark sissons primal diet (paleo) on a random forum and started that. I got some results, felt healthier, got rid of some of the fat, etc but never got ripped and never felt full. Decided that calories in calories out sounded about right so reduced calories a lot whilst intimitantly fasting and managed to get my bf% down so I had a six pack, etc. This was very unsustainable as I was hungry all the time, felt freezing cold, and a bit weak. Gave in trying to keep this up and gained back the fat.

At 20 I stumbled across the leptin reset thread on marks daily apple and thought I'd give it a go, not expecting much. Results were instant and awesome, my extremities stopped being cold for the first time in my life, my excema went away and my hair wasn't dry all the time, effortlessly got ripped, high performance when kickboxing, etc.

Trouble in paradise
Not 2 weeks after finishing the leptin reset and feeling great I shot myself in the foot and life slapped me in the face...

I got tonsillitis thanks to freshers week. So for 2 weeks I was not really eating, and taking lots of NSAIDs to mask the pain. Tonsillitis went away but was replaced by an extreme gnawing sensation just below my sternum at 5am every morning. Went to doctors who gave me PPI's, it was still there so went back to docs who gave me PPI's + Antibiotics to take for 2 weeks. This eliminated it somewhat although it was still noticeable some days.

I noticed during this time that my stomach would get quite bloated some times, ignored for a while but then decided to try do something about it. Amongst other things I tried cutting out dairy, this seemed to work and my bloating was completely gone in 2 days. However this only lasted until the next time I went drinking, then the bloating was back and cutting out dairy did nothing to stop it. The same thing happened a few months later when I cut out FODMAPs, bloating was completely gone for a couple days until I went drinking again. Decided to cut out drinking entirely at this point but still the bloating remained. This bloating would happen everytime I ate anything.

In this time I noticed my gums had receded massively, my breath had started to smell, my BM's seemed to have slowed.

Over the next 6 months I noticed I was getting fatigued way more easily (had to quit kickboxing as I couldn't keep up), I had heart palpitations, I felt pretty weak, I noticed large dark circles appear under my eyes. Because this seemed to happen over time I assume this is a result of whatever my stomach problems are and thus are not directly related.

This all started almost 2 years ago.

Tests that have been done
\-H Pylori Stool Test, came back normal.
\-Upper Endoscopy, came back normal.
\-CT scan, came back normal.
\-Barium swallow, came back normal.
\-Basic blood work, mostly normal, mean cell haemoglobin concentration below normal, serum albumin level above range.

Current Symptoms
\-Strong fatigue and weakness
\-Tiredness
\-Heart Palpitations
\-Dizziness whilst standing
\-Low libido
\-Heavy bloating/distension
\-Constipation
\-Heaviness/pressure in stomach (after eating)
\-Reflux without burning (happens sometimes at night)
\-Cold extremities
\-Dry Hair/Skin
\-Bad Breath / Coated Tongue
\-Back ache (probably from change of posture due to bloating)
\-Feels like food sits in stomach for too long
\-Bags under eyes

It seems like all these symptoms relate to how much food/drink I put into my digestive system. This seems like the only variable. Doesn't matter what the content of the food is, symptoms always the same.

What I have tried
Elimination diets:
I have tried just eggs, fodmaps, GAPs, just fruit, just water. Nothing diet wise seems to matter.

Betaine HCL/Digestive Enzymes:
Didn't notice any burning sensation even when going up to really high doses. Bloating was reduced at real high doses but I also got burning diarrhoea. I think bloating was reduced because food was being rushed through digestive system thanks to too high enzymes. So didn't work.

Candida diet:
No difference.

Parsite cleanse:
No difference.

Bowel cleanse / constipation supplements:
No difference. It takes a lot high dose for me than others to get the desired effect.

H Pylori diet:
No difference.

High dose probiotics:
Didn't notice a difference really with anything apart from raw sauerkraut. When I was eating a LOT of this I noticed my hair got healthy again. I am thinking that maybe it stopped some things getting through my leaky gut or maybe it was the structured water rehydrating me. Cant afford to keep this up and every batch of sauerkraut I make always goes wrong.

Cold thermogenesis:
1 month of cold baths, 1hr/day @ 55f. 2 Months of spot icing 4hrs per day. Turning all radiators off and living in minimal clothes. I maybe noticed a slight increase in tollerance to cold however this was really hard for me to do as I was freezing cold all the time, especially my extremities. I was eating entirely epi-paleo at this time, lots of fish and shell fish. Didn't help my symptoms noticeably.

30 day fast:
Started w/ 7 day dry fast where all my bloating was gone for the first time in a year. As soon as I started drinking water for the rest of the fast the bloating was back. This suggests to me it is not a problem with bacterial overgrowth or stomach acidity.

I have tried various other things and supplements that I cannot remember at this moment in time. None of note

I have recently been taking pregnenolone which improves my hair and skin quality slightly, d-ribose which seems to have reduced my heart palpitations, and magnesium malate which helps with constipation.

The only consistent way I can reduce bloating and some other symptoms is by reducing total volume of food and drink that passes through my digestive system.

I suspect I have adrenal fatigue and leaky gut/dysbiosis which is caused by whatever problem my stomach has with food/drink and the resulting stress response.

### Linz — 2013-08-16T13:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-92767>

Sean,

Starting with a positive: Don't forget the leptin reset worked. You thought you were finished and then destroyed all the good work in freshers week. An epic week in life at just the worst time as students are leaving home, eating the cheapest food, wrecking circadian rhythms, drinking heavily and using technology at all times of day and night (usually keeping it right beside them day and night), living in small insulated boxes. We all like to think we can outwit the rules of life but think about your friends in that first term and how many of them were pale and tired, gaining weight and sometimes becoming depressed, even those that didn't start with previous weight problems. I guess you weren't alone.

I have gut problems that began in a similar but less dramatic way and I too think the final straw was a prescription for NSAIDs. I am much older than you (survived freshers week nearly intact) and I think I was already vulnerable before I started the medication because of an emotionally difficult year when I had also started working on a bright computer screen in late evenings and bought a mobile and dect phones for the first time ever in the previous months. Tightening up my diet really helped me for a few years but I was only just containing it until I added in the circadian rules and more time grounded. I might never have believed the difference the little things can make if I hadn't already tied down the diet but I didn't notice them today, only last month, last year etc. Skin improving and no longer burning in the sun is a very visible sign you are on the right road, or realising the next meal is two hours late and you didn't even notice (without deliberate fasting).

Back to the Leptin reset. KEEP GOING, the timing issues -meals, light, sleep etc- are incredibly important as you already discovered - and don't think of finishing it ever, just gradually changing towards the next stages and making it into a sustainable way of normal life for you once you know better. As Caroline says - give it time. Do you have f.lux on your screens and amber glasses? Better still, turn them off and unplug after dusk. The cold may be an extreme way to actually allow recovery but the other things are really just tightening up everyday life. Deal with the emf, the flouride and the water the best you can and read the squat blog if you haven't already. I think your experience with sauerkraut is a sure sign dehydration is involved (mine goes wrong too. I'm a little bit frightened of it!!). I don't have any advice for your specific problems, just overall strengthening of everything. I'm still working on finding which part actually broke and if it can be actually mended for myself.

With malabsorbtion your mineral status is probably poor. I certainly think that was one of my problems early on when my gums and teeth deteriorated - and fully recovered, the dentist just looked confused. Look again at the early paleo supp blog for the best vits and mins, find a good vit K2 mark 4 supp to help teeth and gut ( I use Thorne drops with D3 as well and I notice differences fast). Perhaps investigate Quinton marine plasma. Make sure to add liver and heart to your diet. The full complement of mito supps (ubiquinol and acetyl-carnitine as well as Mg and d rib) helps me a lot. Raw fish helps promote estriol (safest oestrogen) in the gut. Just don't ask me about gut mos. My worst experience ever was a result of annoying them with pro and pre-biotics - I've still got a lot to learn.

Do you use coconut oil? And do you ever go camping? I think a wild camping holiday somewhere by a sea loch - persuade some easy going friends, cook on your own fire and drink boiled stream water (phone off, no tech)- would be an interesting experiment to see how your gut responds. Or at least make a point of doing a regular walk in the woods, along the coast, up a river, stopping to sit on a tree branch or a big rock just watching the world passing by for a little while and letting everything slow down. Modern life has forgotten this.

Best of luck. There is a huge quantity of information to think about here but when you begin to understand the underlying reasons for things it really does become easier to make your own choices.

Caroline, perhaps we should all swap kids sometimes...

Hi, Linz.

Glad you are getting your health issues sorted.

There is some real good information here although I will see I don't think it's a case of tightening up protocols for me...

for example when I first started doing the leptin rx I wasn't following it fully and my diet was like 80% good (still consuming alcohol, dairy, etc), despite this I got excellent results suggesting that I wasn't that unhealthy to begin with.
It was only after I noticed symptoms that I started really tightening up my diet (cut out alcohol, dairy, increased fish, etc) watching light cycles more closely, etc. Despite this "tightening" I still got gradually worse which suggests that the leptin rx etc wasn't targeting the cause of my ills. So whilst it will help to do the protocols the best I can, I need to do something more and think outside the box to repair myself, I think.

Camping seems a cool idea, use coconut oil quite a lot.

I am confused about eating raw foods though. Do you have to buy them like really fresh and then they are okay to eat raw? What about parasites etc?

### Linz — 2013-08-16T17:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-92777>

Inger is the best person to ask about raw food and read the MHS thread if you haven't already. I think freezing first helps to kill parasites and you just have to make your own educated judgement on safety. Sushi is raw and many eat it without problems. Don't use already minced meat and you can sear the outside a little if you want to and don't use anything that has been stored near raw poultry or pork.

### caroline — 2013-08-17T04:33:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-92898>

I am still thinking about your post ..... I am going to the beach now and going to think some more.....

### caroline — 2013-08-17T10:19:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-92911>

Sean - I didn't mean "eating disorder" in the traditional sense. I think a lot of us here did a lot of damage to our bodies by fasting and doing all kinds of crazy things - so, I guess in my mind, I think of that as an eating disorder. I would have done anything to get rid of the blubber and did do all kinds of stupid things.

It seems to me that the key is when you got sick at freshers week ........so do you think that all the stuff you took could have done some lasting damage?

Linz has great imput for you ....maybe just keep reading the BG series - and don't forget the comments. There has to be an answer somewhere.

I do eat raw meat .... but don't know how that would be for you. I am not sure calling it "raw" is accurate tho. I buy GF rump steak with as much fat as possible and put it on a wooden board in the fridge. I sprinkle it with some black pepper and hot chilli flakes and just leave it there - I usually leave it for a few days and it starts to dry a bit and then slice it thin and enjoy. I love it! I now find if I cook a steak it is much harder to digest than the raw and I don't need nearly as much.
I have no problem at all digesting the raw. I just buy it at the supermarket - where I shop is quite a high turnover. Maybe if you went to your butcher and told him you wanted to eat it raw and it needs to be very fresh. Tell the butcher that you want to make carpaccio or steak tartare. Have you ever had that? It is very delicious. Inger has a recipe here or google.

Also do fresh salmon - Inger has a recipe for that too and great pics.....

### Linz — 2013-08-19T12:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-93098>

Sean,
I restrict raw food to oysters, fresh fish (or pre-packed frozen soon after catching) and some marinated heart.

Lamb is mostly grass-fed in UK (and NZ) and should be fine from the supermarket but I look at the beef more closely - roasts and steaks should have a good layer of their own sub-cutaneous fat and some fat marbling in the muscle (choose other cuts from the same source). Bright red with separate fat tied on is not good. I also look for beef-breed labelling and I rarely buy supermarket pork. Like poultry, pigs are fed a much more manufactured diet than the ruminants.

Did you read the CPC 6 blog Dr Kruse has just re-referenced? Lots of good info linking Vitamin A/light/timing/water etc. Keep thinking outside the box.

### caroline — 2013-08-21T11:14:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-93332>

Wow - you are going to start with lamb liver? ...brave boy!

### seanb4 — 2013-08-25T18:41:17+0300
