### seanb4 — 2017-06-16T20:30:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-216362>

@[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/) Very pleased you are doing better, this is great news! Very, very interesting post. Yes, I think virus test results may not be reliable so no point putting too much stuck into them. I have in the past tried most of the supplements you have suggested bt never in combination and I can't remember dosages. It would be interesting to try them out.

> What I have done in the past few weeks is group antibacterial / antiviral components into three basic categories. Rather than draw out the experiment for a year while I painstakingly find which agent has the most lasting effects, I tested with only 3 groups of substances, each containing 10-15 agents. I quickly found that my symptoms did not get any better or worsened with the first two groups, which contained, among other things, Berberine, Bismuth Citrate, Anise, and Oregon Grape. Group 3, however, had a very noticeable effect. It contains French Tarragon, Indian Tinospora, Horsetail, Thyme, Pau D' Arco, Stinging Nettle Extract, and Olive Leaf Extract.

How were you separating the groups? Was it based on mechanism of action, or was it arbitrary, just to use process of elimination?

> Needless to say, I was blown over. This lesion has been on my temple for ~2 years now and within just a few days, it was literally falling off. The skin revealed underneath is smooth and without any sign of aberration. I have continued to massage 2 drops of Lugol's 5% solution into the area this past week, and it has nearly completely disappeared. Another week's time, and it will be as if it was never there!

That really is quite incredible. I have been using Lugols 7% 4 drops orally for about a month or so. However I have been applying butter on my skin thanks to advice from sueuk and I am now able to tolerate the sun much better. Sweating is also increased which is very good.

I wonder how much improvement is due to Florida vs supplements.

I have had some interesting developments this past week. A couple weeks back I decided to go for another round of Helmithic therapy. For a week now my gastroparesis has been significantly better. I do not want to put too much hope into this. It may not last, or may be the results of body increasing transit time to try kick the worms out (not digesting food right?) but I am not so sure. I have a slight growling stomach now when I go a decent time without food (never happened in last 6yrs) and I have begun eating more fat, and only 1 meal a day. I am in some discomfort afterwards but its nothing compared with before. If this sticks, it could be huge. I could go back to ketosis, my stomach won't be full at bed time (messing up sleep) and the transit time should somewhat normalise gut flora. This will mean I will have far less endotoxins and give my immune system a shot at the virus. Just really hope it lasts, so I'm not going to try anything yet that could potentially kill off the worms....

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-17T10:23:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-216412>

> [seanb4 said:](/goto/post?id=216362)
>
> However I have been applying butter on my skin thanks to advice from sueuk and I am now able to tolerate the sun much better. Sweating is also increased which is very good.

Update on my own hack.  I've been experimenting with ghee and surface temperature. Yesterday the forecast was for UV 8 at solar noon. Well it didn't happen here . It was overcast most of the day and windy, not particularly comfortable. I've had better days in October. So I put my ghee in an eggcup and stood it in a bowl of boiled water and went outside, and applied the melted warm ghee to my skin, including my eyelids. It was as if I'd moved further south..... It was like applying the warmth and the yellow of the sun missing beneath the clouds. I've got a 100% cotton cellular cot blanket, ghee coloured that took some of the cold in the ground away from my back. Lay on my back, closed my eyes and it was like basking under a cloudless sky. One of the most relaxing cloud bathing sessions I have ever had. I was out to get the first light as normal this morning. It was clear blue skies, no wind but still very cool. As soon as the sun came up over the tree line, I did the same at the first piece of ground the sun hit. Turned a so-so light breakfast into delicious.  I'm going to continue with ghee for the time being, warmed under cold and/or cloud, used cold set under a high temperature, but from October I'll be hacking butter.

### seanb4 — 2017-07-10T11:59:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-218346>

Just a quick update.

Been feeling better over last couple of weeks. Changes I've made.

1/2 meal a day + ketosis:
As my gastroparesis has lessened, I am able to do one meal a day and increase fats again. This has obvious benefits but also it has helped me identify what my problems are. I don't eat until 2-6, however if I am out in the sun before this, I tolerate the heat just fine. As soon as I eat, my symptoms ramp up, and I cannot stand the heat/sun. It's all about blood flow I think. Anything that increases blood usage causes worse symptoms. I have found that being cold after eating helps with this as less blood flow to skin.

Restarting CT:
I had been thinking about why CT makes my symptoms worse. It was an increase in sympathetic activity when it seems my parasympathetic activity is very weak. Reading the studies it appears that there is a strengthining of sympathetic when not cold adapted, then increase in vagus when adapted. Although I did it daily 30 mins, 6-12C, for 10 months, for whatever reason, I didn't fully adapt. I hated it, was shivering a lot, and I would have many symptoms of Sympathetic dominance. Whatever is causing me to be ill, also means I cant adapt as well. But I did also recieve benefits. So I decide to start doing it again, but this time, go way slower and only use temperatures that didn't involve shivering, as I think this is where increased sympathetic activity comes from.

I have also brought a uv/ir light in to bathroom (have to be careful it isn't to close to tub) and shine it on my head. Both these things means my tolerance is good. I think the light warming my head should be more or less ok as it appears what is important is reducing my skin temp to activate cold receptors (torso), so my skin is still getting cold. Look foward to water getting colder and seeing how this set up holds up.

I have increased libido, so I know it's helping my hormones howerver no longer have servere dry mouth, which I used to get after every session. This indicates my parasympathetic system isn't getting too overwhelmed.

Quantlet:

Been using this 1hr in the morning and 1 hr after meal for 2 weeks now. I have noticed changes in my heart pounding and overal well being.

I now need to focus on keeping this level of improved health and building on it.

### seanb4 — 2017-07-27T20:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-220240>

My improvements have been maintained so far. Ketosis has been hit and miss, sleep is better however POTS symptoms get significantly worse. I figured it was just "keto flu" electrolyte imbalances. I have been in keto 1.5years ago no problem. Now it's big problem. I am eating higher fat but around 70g carbs. If I go lower for a few days I'm in trouble.

uBiome test comes back, not sure what to make of it. Diversity percentile is good, probiotics (Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium) is pretty bad, 20% compared to average. Nothing else really sticks out...

### seanb4 — 2017-09-23T16:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-224186>

UPDATE:
Since last update I have made progress in some areas, albeit small.

Thiamine! Despite my diet having roughly enough I decided to try some transdermal allithiamine after hearing how it helps some with POTS. Instantly noticed improvement in symptoms after eating and noticed slight improvement in POTS overall. Unfortunately I have not been able to build on this, attempts at increasing dosage lead to increases in different POTS like symptoms. I am taking a B-Complex and mg, k but I still am unable to tolerate any more than slightly over the RDA.
Thiamine helps stomach acid production, and the ANS which is how it could be helping my POTS.

Now the question is why am I thiamine deficient since I eat lots of offal and generally get enough through diet. The obvious answer to me is poor digestion. I think the main culprit is low stomach acid possibly from screwed up ANS not letting me get into parasympathetic mode. Could also be gut bacteria eating it up.
I have tried many times to fix my stomach acid but failed always. I can take massive amounts of Betaine HCL with no burning but then develop over symptoms from it, presumably from pushing methylation too hard.
I think the key to fixing this issue would be fixing my ANS which would also fix my POTS and allow proper blood flow to the gut, also allow proper parasympathetic signaling to make stomach acid and increase motility. How to fix this is the million £££ question. Grounding seems to help a little, the sun light if too warm makes me more sympathetic, CT does...

Usually I don't get ill but I got a fever 2 week ago and my heart rate was 110+ all day and night for 4 days. I had to go to the hospital as this combined with my POTS was unbearable, I stayed in for 4 days and now it's mostly gone. I am sick to death of this heart pounding, it's no good for me short nor long term however the doctors don't care.

My vit D3 test came back at 121 nmol/L = 48ng/ml. Disappointing for saying I have spent literally all summer outside as I no longer work. I know that people who have CFS/POTS have lower vit D but I was hoping it would be higher...

There was discussion on Marik Protocol
sulbutiamine (goes thru blood brain barrier, BBB)
benfontiamine(does not go thru BBB)

Possibly you may want to try it (supposedly it wears of, so not sure about increasing doses (assuming that it would initially been helpful)

> Usually I don't get ill but I got a fever 2 week ago and **my heart rate was 110+ all day and night for 4 days.** I had to go to the hospital as this combined with my POTS was unbearable, I stayed in for 4 days and now it's mostly gone. I am sick to death of this heart pounding, it's no good for me short nor long term however the doctors don't care.
>
> My vit D3 test came back at 121 nmol/L = 48ng/ml. Disappointing for saying I have spent literally all summer outside as I no longer work. I know that people who have CFS/POTS have lower vit D but I was hoping it would be higher...

**my heart rate was 110+ all day and night for 4 days.
\--------**
reduce
or temporarily discontinue
thyroid hormone supplementation if any.
**
.**

### seanb4 — 2019-07-05T09:38:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seans-very-un-optimal-journal.7908/post-273712>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=273677)
>
> **my heart rate was 110+ all day and night for 4 days.
> \--------**
> reduce
> or temporarily discontinue
> thyroid hormone supplementation if any.
> **
> .**

Hey JanSz, hope you've been well. The increase in HR is due to POTS which is a form of autonomic dysfunction and this + CFS I suspect is the cause of all my symptoms. No thyroid supps here.

"Possibly you may want to try it (supposedly it wears of, so not sure about increasing doses (assuming that it would initially been helpful)"

I did get significant initial success with allithiamine but like you said it wore off. Could you tell me more about how it wears off? I had assumed I had corrected a deficiency so I stopped gaining further benefit.

I may as well pop on update here as it has been a long time.

Like I said above, I feel I have a decent idea on what has been causing my all these problems, fixing it however is not so easy. My illness started September 2011 after getting Tonsilitis and a stomach ulcer. Afterwards I developed symptoms of CFS and POTS. It is very common in both these diseases for the trigger to be a viral onset. I have received official Dx of these diseases after going through a lot of ignorant doctors.

I have been making very slow and steady progress since I hit rock bottom in 2016. I feel most of the progress I have made has been by avoiding or maneuvering around the disease as opposed to curing it. Examples include, avoiding heat!, keeping legs raised, mirtazapine, adding MCT to carbs, avoiding carbs, soft meals.

With regards to carbs there are quite a few studies showing malfunctioning energy metabolism but particularly glucose. This rings very true in my case as my heart symptoms increase like clock work no matter the carb source after carb meals in correlation with the amount of carbs. It also follows a patern of increasing symptoms up to 1-1.5 hrs, peaking then slowly dropping to baseline over the next 4hrs.

There is particular interested in Pyruvate Dehydrogenase Inhibition. Long story short, I thought I may be able to get around PDH inhibition by eating MCT with carbs as MCTs are metabolised straight away so the liver will have a large supply of acetyl CoA so any Pyruvate ingested will skip PDH and go through PC instead to make oxaloacetate and the TCA will keep spining.

Anyway it seems to have worked as my symptoms are siginficantly reduced after carbs compared to before however they are still present. You may say just eat ketosis however gastroparesis then comes a big problem but I have recently managed to get keto to work by using very soft foods and relying on some excess body fat.

Although I have no real idea what is going on I will say this is my leading theory so far. I get virus that goes into cells yet is non cytolytic. The virus then causes dysregulation in my system (cytokines, IDO, exosomes) that lead to poor energy metabolism and the resulting dysautonomia etc. This is obviously very simplified.

Another idea is tonsilitis caused brainstem compression / CCI / etc which then caused malfunctioning autonomic system. I recently found out that ENT infections can lead to cervical spine instability, I believe it is called Grisels syndrome. There are a bunch of other reasons to suspect this.

I have a number of other theories and things to work on. I mainly post over on phoenixrising forums now. I still do a lot of things I learnt from Kruse but I eliminate that which isn't compatible with my symptoms. For example I regularly go outside and get sun in my eyes but I do not allow myself to get hot (which is very awkward in summer), the heat causes more blood pooling in my legs and I do not sweat at all which make everything worse and absolutely kills my heart.

So I am doing ok for the most part, still lots of things to try and optimistic I will get better however the question is how much more of my youth will be wasted before I do get better?

# Thread: Question for the ladies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-15T16:20:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92636>

I am finding the past two weeks that I am unable to sleep through the night when there were times in the past that I was. I have been waking up between 2 and 3 with hot feet and rarely get back to sleep. It just so happened to be my luteal phase of my cycle. I just started my cycle today and last night I did sleep a little better and handled the CT bath a little better. I am very curious to see if it gets better now that the cycle has started. I am still waiting for some more results of my hormonal testing but in doing more research and really analyzing my labs, I thinking I might be estrogen dominant and this would make sense if I have more issues after ovulation.

**Has anybody else noticed a change in sleep in correlation to their cycle? If they did and went on BIHT did it get better?**

Also, my cold tolerance is horrible right now. I have had to shorted the length of CT and increase the temp due to just not being able to rewarm before bed. I will be curious to see how it goes next week when my hormones shift.

The puzzle is coming together a little more each day.

Nicole

### HoneyChild — 2013-08-15T21:25:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92668>

Yes, I've posted before about how I wake up in the night feeling hot and also very thirsty and noticed that this occurred in the luteal phase. It's happened to me the last couple of nights about 2am although it hasn't been happening every month. When I got saliva testing done from ZRT I was low in both estrogen and progesterone and the PG:E2 ratio was 82.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-15T22:24:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92676>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=92636)
>
> I am finding the past two weeks that I am unable to sleep through the night when there were times in the past that I was. I have been waking up between 2 and 3 with hot feet and rarely get back to sleep. It just so happened to be my luteal phase of my cycle. I just started my cycle today and last night I did sleep a little better and handled the CT bath a little better. I am very curious to see if it gets better now that the cycle has started. I am still waiting for some more results of my hormonal testing but in doing more research and really analyzing my labs, I thinking I might be estrogen dominant and this would make sense if I have more issues after ovulation.
>
> **Has anybody else noticed a change in sleep in correlation to their cycle? If they did and went on BIHT did it get better?**
>
> Also, my cold tolerance is horrible right now. I have had to shorted the length of CT and increase the temp due to just not being able to rewarm before bed. I will be curious to see how it goes next week when my hormones shift.
>
> The puzzle is coming together a little more each day.
>
> Nicole

We've had this discussion in the CT threads as well as several of the BHRT Threads. when my PG starts to drop I can't stand the cold. Remember Pg = Cold; why think hibernating pregnant bears. Estrogen attracts heat and dislikes cold.

As for sleep, for me my sleep issues arise when I'm in the last week of my cycle just before TOTM, and during TOTM + 5 days. for me BHRT has helped I'm sleeping better.. across the whole cycle... but as hormones aren't static there are days where I don't sleep as well as I could.

### nicld — 2013-08-16T16:50:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92775>

Thanks girls for letting me know I am not alone. This is the first time that I have been really looking into how I feel and what time of my cycle that I am in. Bad thing is my cycle has been getting shorter and shorter (much to the hubby's and my dislike). Every 25 days now when it used to be longer....another clue? I have 10 days until I see a new doc and I my latest round of hormone testing is going to be delivered to the lab today and should have results back next week.

I will have to check out the BHRT thread tonight during CT. Hoping I can tolerate it better then last week, that was brutal.

### HoneyChild — 2013-08-16T18:24:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92790>

My cycles have also been getting shorter, 25 days has become normal for me. I don't know how to interpret that, especially because I started quite a few supplements at the same time including maca which is supposed to boost progesterone. I thought that would lengthen my cycles but it hasn't.

### nicld — 2013-08-16T21:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92827>

I am seriously not happy about them shortening too. That means I am going to have almost 15 cycles a year and not 12. Not exactly my idea of fun. I wonder why the shorten too as we age (or that is my case at least).

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T21:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-for-the-ladies.7910/post-92829>

What about synchronizing your cycles to the moon?

I watched a videocast by Dr. Kruse, [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rsUSkVXg9C8](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rsUSkVXg9C8) , where he said bone regenerates using our electric charge. I'm wondering if teeth regenerate the same way. Also, is it possible to have gums regrow.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-16T02:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-do-they-regenerate-like-bone.7913/post-92706>

no they do not........enamel forms once and then ameloblasts go away for ever

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T02:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-do-they-regenerate-like-bone.7913/post-92713>

crapola  Well i'm lucky mine aren't too bad. This makes me want to wear a night guard even more. Thanks for the prompt response Doc

### Dextery — 2013-08-16T03:11:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/teeth-do-they-regenerate-like-bone.7913/post-92718>

But there was that article about growing teeth in urine. [http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/07/30/urine-stem-cells-grow-teeth\_n\_3677753.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/07/30/urine-stem-cells-grow-teeth_n_3677753.html)

"Need a new tooth? Chinese researchers may have developed a way to grow human teeth, but those in need of a replacement may be a bit turned off when they learn how the substitute is created.

In a study published Tuesday in peer-reviewed journal Cell Regeneration, a team from the Guangzhou Institutes of Biomedicine and Health reveals how they used urine stem cells to grow teeth in mice.

Working with cells passed from the body in urine, researchers created stem cells that could potentially form rudimentary teeth and implanted them into the kidneys of mice. After three weeks, the urine stem cells grew into tooth-like structures that contained dental pulp, dentin, enamel space and enamel organ.

The team compared the teeth grown from urine stem cells with structures developed using human embryonic stem cells, the study's control group.

urine stem cells teeth
The above figure shows a tooth structure created from human embryonic stem cells (top) and induced pluripotent stem cells (bottom), which are derived from urine. The images on the left show the structures removed from the mouse kidney. (Image courtesy of Cai et al./Cell Regeneration)

The use of urine stem cells may be an unusual method for growing teeth, but it's not the only ongoing research into bioengineering replacement teeth. A recent study in the U.K. published in the Journal of Dental Research, indicated that cells from human gums combined with mouse embryonic cells may be used to form human teeth.

"It is well known that adult dental stem cells have been successfully applied in tissue
engineering research. It has proven to be possible to generate dentin pulp complexes, even whole teeth out of isolated cells," the Chinese researchers write in the study. "Although these approaches could lead to tooth formation, the sources of endogenous dental epithelial stem cells seem to be scarce."

That's why, as the authors explain, they decided to produce substitutes for the specific type of dental cells using stem cells derived from urine.

While the research provides a potential practical use for urine stem cells, some have questioned the feasibility of the method to produce teeth that would be suitable for adults. As stem cell scientist Chris Mason of the University College of London points out to BBC News, the risk of contamination from bacteria may also be an issue."


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: ZMK for Magnesium?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T02:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/post-92710>

Thoughts on ZMK for athletes? Better than transdermal magnesium spray and Natural Calm at night?
ANyone have any experience using?

### Ricardo — 2013-12-18T05:24:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/post-103724>

I take 1 gram of Magnesium citrate, 50 mg chelated zinc and a sublingual tablet of b-12 (methylcobalamin), b6 and folate. Its a homemade version of ZMA. ZMK is like ZMA with the addition of some other nutes. My bloods showed a magnesium deficiency before I supplemented with Magnesium citrate. Now they are normal.

I prefer Magnesium citrate because its good value for the $. I get it in bulk powder.

My favorite is Magnesium Malate. However, I can't find it in bulk.

Many people are deficient in Magnesium and/or Zinc. I had stiff muscles from working out: the Magnesium solved that.

Magnesium levels fall during the night so its a good idea to take this one before sleep. Its relaxes and seems to calm food cravings.

I have read so many good things about Magnesium that I am a regular consumer of it.

I haven't finished reading all Dr Kruse has written about Magnesium. So far, it seems like he advocates supplementing with it.

My bloods have shown that I need to supplement with Magnesium. Agricultural conditions have changed and we don't get as much of it from food as we used to.

### Shijin13 — 2013-12-18T15:14:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/post-103736>

Megan

just buy magnesium chloride salts - and make your own mag oil spray - that's what I do. [http://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb\_sb\_n...=aps&field-keywords=magnesium+chloride+flakes](http://www.amazon.com/s/ref=nb_sb_noss_1?url=search-alias%3Daps&field-keywords=magnesium+chloride+flakes)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-18T21:11:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/post-103775>

I think this is an old thread. I have not seen Meghan around here for a while now.

### Ed Warnicke — 2014-03-16T16:43:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/post-115786>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zmk-for-magnesium.7915/post-115787>

# Thread: Making all this affordable for us poor folk.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-08-16T03:05:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92717>

I am thinking maybe we should talk about this some more....post suggestions for keeping cost down.

I think it has likely all been said before - but maybe we need a refresher.....

So .....Gretchen is telling/teaching you how to keep your medical costs down - how to leverage your insurance company etc. finding great Docs and being informed.

I think we have forgotten \[a little\] How Dr. K. has said that food is just 10%......

So if we can't afford oysters every day or some of you don't even have access to fresh seafood - what to do?

Dr. K. has said this repeatedly - a can of sardines will do. He has never once said that you need to buy the most expensive seafood ... do what you can.

In my opinion - frozen seafood can be a bargain and often is flash frozen on ships - this is way better than getting fresh at a super market - you never have any idea how long it has been in transit.....

Liver is so cost effective, as well as heart and tongue. One of the paleo sites has some great looking recipes for tongue. I have never had it but I am definitely going to give it a go!

Also - great recipes for ground beef. Do you get emails from nom nom paleo? \[hope that is the right name\]
She just posted a great one the other day. Her recipes are great ....I have had cooking classes with a lot of her mentors and they are impeccable.

What prompted this you might ask? I find I am worried a lot less about food these days. If food is only 10%....we have a lot bigger fish to fry

Any suggestions? ideas? How do you feed a family on epi-paleo?

### caroline — 2013-08-16T03:21:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92723>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=92719)
>
> bingo..........

I like getting a "bingo" from you ..... I always feel like such a lite weight around here....

### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T16:30:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92774>

\- ask the butcher for bones for the dog
\- relearn old recipes from Victorian cookbooks like Mrs Beeton . Fish was a staple for breakfast even then and many recipes for the use of offal and cheap cuts . I suggest a bit more modern spicing though
\- what supplements do you really need ? Narrow it down. How much can you get from food ?
\- Fresh Fish.: look for ethnic stores that have a fish culture. Here it is Asian shops. Their prices are 20%-40% better than local fish shops. Londoners consider Borough fish market.

Thanks for bringing this up, Caroline.

### cantweight — 2013-08-16T17:41:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92780>

Sp appreciative of all ideas on this thread. I am one of those having trouble making it all work. There are so many things I want to purchase.....like magneticos.....that if I could tighten the food cost I might be able to.

My biggest challenge is that we are a family of 5. My kids are 13, 11, and 8 and they can put away some food! I love to see them devour a ton of fish.....but that fish is 9-32 dollars a pound!!

We get our beef from local farms it is better priced than grocery stores but not cheap 5-19 dollars per pound. Eggs from these farms are 4.75 a dozen. Bacon is 7.29 for 12 oz. You can see why I am struggling.

We have replaced our probiotics with raw milk....that is a big savings. And have also pared down the supplements to what really seems to help each one of us.

Bone broths, soups, and home made jerky are my next pursuits. School lunches are my nemesis hoping the broth and jerky will fit the bill!!!

Thanks for the thread Audrey  Asian shops are great idea Lahelada!

### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T17:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92783>

In the spirit of your signature  how about taking up fishing as a hobby ? N.VA has some amazing lake fish, not the same as ocean fish but free well almost. I don't know how feasible that is of course for you.

Your beef prices made me swallow hard. That is heavy duty and I can understand why you are struggling. My beef is from the only supermarket that guarantees grassfed beef and it ranges from $1 for soup meat to 10$$ for a fine steak.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-16T20:34:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92820>

International markets are great. I get a 1lb bag of frozen mixed seafood (squid, cuttle fish, mussels etc) for any where from $2/lb to $4/lb - compare to Trader Joes where a similar bag -is $12/lb

Mackerel, blue fin, this week we bought skate - and made that. it was something like $3.99/lb

the international mkts also carry organ meats

Consider finding a Halal or Kosher butcher/market - better prices, and usually better foods.

look for local farmers and talk to them - see what things they offer people who buy from them directly.... you might be surprised...

and CANNED Sardines, Mackerel and oysters are essential...

### caroline — 2013-08-17T01:32:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/making-all-this-affordable-for-us-poor-folk.7917/post-92868>

A local fish shop here throws fish heads away - no one wants them! great for broth

A recent post of Dan's caught my eye - and, actually was part of the reason for this thread. Someone asked him what he was doing and eating on a daily basis. He is very committed to all of this and when he talked about food he said he doesn't always buy GF.

So - I thought - we would survive if we had regular beef once in a while or chicken once in a while - not the most optimum - but hey ...food is only 10% ....I think we need to give ourselves a break!

Maybe I am just lucky - I have eaten in some incredible restaurants and loved them ..... but ..... I also love meatloaf \[probably my favorite thing\] I adore a recipe from Paul Prudhomme ....and you guys could feel like you are in the Gulf!
I love meatballs and sloppy joes and kibbe etc. - I could go on and on.
It is very easy to make your own sausages - in fact JK had some great looking ones on FB awhile back that he said he made.
What about salmon cakes made with canned salmon? or crab cakes? and you could really feel like you are on the gulf!

I thought I had got over my food passion - maybe I need to re direct it!

Let's talk more .... what other ideas do you have?

what about sushi? If I need to go out for lunch .... i will have sushi - I love it and don't feel as tho I have committed a sin of some kind ..... we are in the driver's seat guys......

If it matters to food........it must matter to us.

Does hot water freeze faster than cold water? This was shown decades ago by Erasto Mpemba, who found that ice cream made with warm water froze faster than ice cream made with cold water. The Royal Society conducted a contest to see who had the best explanation. Some 22,000 people responded. The winner: [http://www.rsc.org/mpemba-competition/mpemba-winner.asp](http://www.rsc.org/mpemba-competition/mpemba-winner.asp) . See if you like his explanation.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-16T03:55:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-just-the-temperature-of-water-matter-to-biology.7918/post-92734>

More to contemplate about heat and cold and energy transfers: Heat transfer is a discipline of thermal engineering that concerns the generation, use, conversion, and exchange of thermal energy and heat between physical systems. Heat transfer is classified into various mechanisms, such as thermal conduction, thermal convection, thermal radiation, and transfer of energy by phase changes. Engineers also consider the transfer of mass of differing chemical species, either cold or hot, to achieve heat transfer. While these mechanisms have distinct characteristics, they often occur simultaneously in the same system.
Heat conduction, also called diffusion, is the direct microscopic exchange of kinetic energy of particles through the boundary between two systems. When an object is at a different temperature from another body or its surroundings, heat flows so that the body and the surroundings reach the same temperature, at which point they are in thermal equilibrium. Such spontaneous heat transfer always occurs from a region of high temperature to another region of lower temperature, as described by the second law of thermodynamics.
Heat convection occurs when bulk flow of a fluid (gas or liquid) carries heat along with the flow of matter in the fluid. The flow of fluid may be forced by external processes, or sometimes (**in gravitational fields**) by buoyancy forces caused when thermal energy expands the fluid (for example in a fire plume), thus influencing its own transfer. This is known as a field effect. The latter process is often called "natural convection". All convective processes also move heat partly by diffusion, as well. Another form of convection is forced convection. In this case the fluid is forced to flow by use of a pump, fan or other mechanical means.
Thermal radiation occurs through a vacuum or any transparent medium (solid or fluid). **It is the transfer of energy by means of photons in electromagnetic waves governed by the same laws**

Cites:Geankoplis, Christie John (2003). Transport processes and separation process principles : (includes unit operations) (4th ed. ed.). Upper Saddle River, NJ: Prentice Hall Professional Technical Reference.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92753>

Yeah, use lot's of it. I believe Ceylon Cinnamon is the ideal form of cinnamon since it won't thin your blood like others - yet it still has beneficial compounds, especially in regards to insulin and blood glucose. Ben Greenfield said something about a few tbsp/shots of ACV before a meal helps the insulin spike of foods and helps you stay in ketosis, but I don't know of any studies that show that affect. In the 4-Hour Body, Tim Ferriss states that he and all his researchers never really found anything in the literature about vinegar and the glycemic and/or insulinogenic load of foods, yet Cinnamon and citric juices helped lower it when taken before a meal or used on a meal.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T17:18:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92778>

Hmmm... I thought it was Cassia Cinnamon according to Tim Ferris.....

Yes I do but I also don't do carbs..... I do: PPP - Epi Paleo - Keto
I take it in the morning first thing before a CT session...... that and Bitter Melon and a big cold class of Perrier water... Then a walk barefoot outside -shorts, no shirt in the sun (sunrise) for some grounding......
Then some Morning BP coffee...... and time with the wife enjoying some more outdoor time talking....

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T20:01:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92808>

If anyone hasn't tried Mark Sisson's idea about adding 3 egg yolks to a cooled down BP coffee, they really should consider. Its delicious. I add cacoa and Ceylon cinnamon too.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T21:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92822>

[http://www.askmen.com/celebs/interview\_400/493c\_tim-ferriss-interview.html](http://www.askmen.com/celebs/interview_400/493c_tim-ferriss-interview.html)
I guess I should have specified... I have Saigon Cassia Cinnamon fresh ground from a local spice company.... Saigon is what Tim recommends...

3\. Cassia Cinnamon Cheat

Cinnamon helps keep blood sugars in check, which helps control weight gain. Both Cassia and Ceylon cinnamon help regulate glucose levels, but some studies show that Cassia does it better.

To determine the difference between these types of cinnamon, Ceylon sticks roll up from both sides like a scroll. Cassia looks more like bark and will often roll up from one side like a cigar. Use Cassia cinnamon in your coffee or even sprinkle it on a bowl of oatmeal or cottage cheese. Cassia cinnamon, it turns out, is the type you find in most coffee shops. Look for it at grocery stores for about $5. If unavailable, look for it at specialty food stores.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-16T21:07:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92823>

Danco which do you use?

### Lahelada — 2013-08-16T21:09:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cinnamon-and-or-apple-cider-vinegar.7921/post-92824>

Cassia in high amounts is to be avoided for its blood thinning qualities due its coumarin content which is according to some source 1000 times higher. The German "FDA" has done some studies on it I believe.

Re your use of lowering food GI that has been making the rounds since at least the South beach diet,came back via a facebook pos but hard data I have not seen. I would take it however for its antinflammatory properties which have hundreds of years of anecdotal evidence .

# Thread: Glass or Stainless Steel for water?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2013-08-16T18:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92792>

I take a BPA free bottle to work and sometimes two...when I can find the lid...Anyway I've been contemplating switching in case the leeching is harming me.

Hopefully its not just placebo...but does anyone have any experience changing from using plastic to metal or glass and actually feeling an improvement in health. Its a hard plastic bottle..I wouldn't think it would leech much if at all...but I don't know for sure. I want a bottle or perhaps a pair of bottles that I can take to work and drink from for the 7 hours I'm there. 3:30-10:30 typically. A 20 ounce bottle like my current plastic ones wouldn't be bad. I've heard much about BPA free sometimes containing even worse substitutes, but I have no clue if thats the case with my bottles.

[http://www.academy.com/webapp/wcs/stores/servlet/Product\_10151\_10051\_26965\_-1?kwid=ps\_pla&mkid=PLA](http://www.academy.com/webapp/wcs/stores/servlet/Product_10151_10051_26965_-1?kwid=ps_pla&mkid=PLA)

So its 24 ounces...anyway I just said 20 because it doesn't actually show 24 on the measurements on the side. I guess cause thats at the top of the bottle.

I posted this in diet because it involves ingestion...even though its generally water. I guess it doesn't matter too much...the monster thread would be the other logical place.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T18:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92795>

I think water from glass definitely tastes better. I'm sure there are other things to worry about in plastic besides BPA. However it's made from petroleum, depending on how you look at that, it is a natural substance on earth. Maybe we worry about its effects on us more than we need to. I'm sure breathing in the fumes from burning gasoline isn't good for us, but is sticking your head over a fire of wood good for you?

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T18:43:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92796>

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T18:51:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92797>

> [ColdKnight said:](/goto/post?id=92795)
>
> I'm sure there are other things to worry about in plastic besides BPA.

I agree. Just because BPA is the first toxic substance they have identified/admitted to, doesn't mean there are more. I believe plastics contain dozens of xenoestrogens.

We've been using glass canning jars. Cheap, but they occasionally break. And they sweat. And the lids have some sort of plastic film on them to prevent rusting.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T18:59:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92800>

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T19:11:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glass-or-stainless-steel-for-water.7924/post-92804>

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T19:16:37+0300

# Thread: Xanthelasma?  LDL turning to foam cells!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/xanthelasma-ldl-turning-to-foam-cells.7925/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### HoneyChild — 2013-08-16T18:31:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/xanthelasma-ldl-turning-to-foam-cells.7925/post-92793>

I'm not sure when this started happening (maybe in the last week or so) but I've now got light orange patches on the skin around my eyes. After doing some research I came across the condition Xanthelasma, which is where oxidized LDL is taken up by macrophages and turned into foam cells. They then accumulate around the eyes to produce pretty horrible looking orange lumps. Mine are nowhere near as bad as the ones in the wiki page photo but they are in the same locations, only mine are flat.

[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma)
[http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818](http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818)

Any thoughts?

### Anna Dunn — 2026-02-02T00:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/xanthelasma-ldl-turning-to-foam-cells.7925/post-366221>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=92793)
>
> I'm not sure when this started happening (maybe in the last week or so) but I've now got light orange patches on the skin around my eyes. After doing some research I came across the condition Xanthelasma, which is where oxidized LDL is taken up by macrophages and turned into foam cells. They then accumulate around the eyes to produce pretty horrible looking orange lumps. Mine are nowhere near as bad as the ones in the wiki page photo but they are in the same locations, only mine are flat.
>
> [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Xanthelasma)
> [http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818](http://www.gpnotebook.co.uk/simplepage.cfm?ID=422903818)
>
> Any thoughts?

I know his post is super old but if u r still on here, did u find out anything? My xanthelasma started about a yr after I swapped to a low oxalate diet and I started excreting them. My cholesterol more than tripled to a whopping 23mmol/l . After 2 years my cholesterol started coming down. Its now 14 but my x hasn't reduced.

# Thread: Smartphones make you shortsighted, doctor claims
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-16T21:09:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92825>

He told the Mail: "If things continue as they are, I predict that 40 to 50 percent of 30-year-olds could have myopia by 2033 as a result of smartphones and lifestyles in front of screens -- an epidemic we call screen sightedness."

Screens place a greater demand on the eyes. Moreover, what's clear is that people are staring into them for previously unthinkable hours at a time.

Whole lives are being lived through screens and in them. Human bodies seem unprepared for this entirely radical shift.

[http://news.cnet.com/8301-17852\_3-5...rtphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims/](http://news.cnet.com/8301-17852_3-57598890-71/iblind-smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims/)

### nonchalant — 2013-08-16T21:56:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92830>

Even if I'm wearing my red lenses, my eyes get tired after being on the computer for a while.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T22:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92835>

Yes. It is not good to look at a screen of any kind for a long period of time without breaks. One should look at things further away to excersize the eye muscles by focusing. It's probably wise to keep the total amount of time to a minimum per day. I could see how keeping your eyes focused on one distance for an extended period of time would make you unable to refocus on objects farther away. This goes back to the old saying use it or lose it. Going with the theme of ancestral wellness I can't see our ancestors starring at the ground for hours on end at a bug or something like that.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-16T22:40:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92837>

I also bet that computer and phone screen's unnatural light isn't the best for our eye health. No matter what time it is.

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-16T22:45:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92838>

I knew something weird was going on... and stopped reading on my phone... I still use my kindle touch (greyscreen black lettters) but wifi is off, and I read it in the dark. I'm actually supposed to use reading glasses but don't... Of course I was already nearsighted and had lasik back in 2000....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-16T23:44:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/smartphones-make-you-shortsighted-doctor-claims.7926/post-92853>

July webinar.........blue light........eMF........early cataracts.......early catacts lead to diseases of aging because you cant sense night and day well.........circle of life.

### Martin — 2013-08-17T17:05:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-kruse-your-opinion-on-this-article.7927/post-92920>

@AutoMoonMonster

I'd nix the home outlet filters. Our EMF Webinar guy Michael Neuert says they _could_ worsen one's situation. It depends on many other factors going on in your home/apt. wiring and proximity to power lines, transformers, smart meters, etc. Too much money at $30 each times number-of-outlets-in-your-home when all they are is a plastic box with a few capacitors inside.

The schumann resonators are intriguing, but I've not found decent research and comparisons on what they do. And it's an electronic device, so I have a fundamental skepticism about using them. Still, if I were to get some good, credible advice on whether to get one, I would definitely consider a whole-house unit.

I think a magnet pad is what one ought to shoot for. So much data coming out, and already here, on the positive effects of magnetic fields. Magnetism is fundamental to life, so why not up the field and grow like a dinosaur when you increase that field.

You will find differing results on all these things because we are all different in how we've lived and the effect it's had on us. That fact alone has helped me to make sense of this whacky world and why one thing works immediately for one person, and not so much for the next. But I always think that if you ran labs on both, you'd see changes in each. Just different values and effects.

The Big Giant Head has spoken.

# Thread: ColdKnight's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coldknights-journal.7929/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColdKnight — 2013-08-17T03:08:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coldknights-journal.7929/post-92883>

So I have been working on my health since I was about 18 years old when I got diagnosed with precancer cells in my esophagus from GERD and a hiatal hernia. This scared the shit outta me. I started with the conventional wisdom bullcrap for the first few years, then about 7 years ago I found MarksDailyApple.com. This lead me to Weston A. Price's and Pottenger's work. This was so eye opening to me. I couldn't believe our government could purposely lead us astray regarding our diet and medicine. I am amazed that Price and Pottenger's work got swept under the rug of mainstream medicine. These findings were revolutionary. So anyway I fixed my acid reflux by using probiotics and raw milk. At first I took those nexium pills, but luckily I was allergic to them so I switched to Prilosec. Then after a year of taking those daily, I found the probiotic solution. I subsequently got another scope to check on my esophagus, and the doctor told me he wouldn't have noticed any abnormalities had I not told him about the previous scope. Now this really inspired me to fix myself, instead of relying on modern medicine. I loved to call modern medicine a quackery. It isn't an entire quack, but most of it is. Natural medicine has much more effective solutions for many ailments. I also had a weight problem because of insulin resistance. I was able to effectively beat my weight problem and lost about 80lbs from paleo diet, weight lifting, HIIT sprints, and intermittent fasting. Well, after fixing my gut problems somewhat and getting to a low bf%, another issue showed up. I had begun to get severe chronic back pain along with herniated discs in my lower back. I've battled this for 6 or so years. I'm now 30. About a year ago I found Dr. Jack Kruse from Mark's website through a blog about ice baths. This really intrigued and scared me at first. I hated cold water, but after reading Jack's blogs, I realized I could adapt to enjoy the cold by adopting a heavy seafood diet. To start, I adopted his recommended diet pretty soon after I started reading the blogs on CT and the Brain-gut series. So a few weeks ago I finally mustered the courage to spot ice. I spot iced my torso for a few days and was able to stand it for at least an hour without cold uticaria. This led me to believe I'd be safe to try the full immersions. After building an outside bath out of wood, foam padding, and plastic lining. I started full immersions up to my head immediately after the 3 days of icing my torso and had excellent results. I skipped a few steps, because I felt I had a really good o63 ratio and was a very lean tall muscular specimen. I started craving the cold water so much that I was doing 25 minutes at 50-55 to be safe every day for 7 days. I read that it was easier to handle a bit colder temps than the 70 degree range. This was so true. My chronic back pain dulled significantly towards the end of this week. I started feeling much more adept in handling stress, my pain tolerance from stubbed toes for instance had risen markedly, my cold tolerance had risen noticeably, and I felt like my brain worked much more fluidly. These are just a few of the benefits I can think of at the moment. I know there are more. I did take Inger's idea to add seaweed to my water cup for the day, and this made it very easy to get lots of sea veggies into my diet. I also have been optimizing my magnesium levels with Threonate orally and chloride transdermally with gel. I have been eating tons of Alaskan salmon, sardines, scallops, and tuna. I wish I could find fresh oysters, and I haven't tried a canned oyster I bought yet. I have no medical insurance so getting labs done is a bit difficult for me. Plus I hate needles and figure I will feel the difference.

Today will be day 3 of restarting the CT immersions after a 2.5 week break. I have ate no sugar and have stuck with eating a high fat 50% sea food diet. I have always had a problem with craving sugar, but since implementing almost everything Jack recommends, I'm finding it very easy to stop sugar entirely. I know this will help my back and the CT adaption significantly. I'm in the middle of my journey to optimal health and I feel it is right around the corner thanks to Jack. I am sooo grateful to Jack and the paleo scene for bringing all this information to light.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-17T03:12:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coldknights-journal.7929/post-92885>

Day 1 15 minutes at 54 degree water - no sugar

Day 2 18 minutes 52 degree water - no sugar

Day 3 21 minutes 50-52 degree water and no sugar today/ a little cravings because I didn't make a huge salmon and veggie meal for dinner. I had a can of sardines and a carrot instead.

Sugar cravings do stop - no question! That is one thing that worries people ....they cant imagine their life without sugar and carbs. It is so funny to look back and think that we couldn't live without them - we were so addicted. That addiction alone should scare the heck out of people.

When we know better ....we can do so much better.....

You are doing great with C/T - thanks for sharing.......

### ColdKnight — 2014-10-25T20:31:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/coldknights-journal.7929/post-145119>

I'm back baby!! Here is a little update regarding my health status. Dr. Kruse mentioned I should take vitamin d3 and drink lots of RO water about a year ago. This really helped almost stop my random disc herniations(Thanks Jack!!!!). However, I may have took that a little too far. I was taking 30,000iu daily and I began to have heart palpatations and a little chest pain near the heart. Sometimes it felt like I was going to have a heart attack. Initially, I didn't realize it was due to the unnatural form of Vitamin D I was taking. After reading Tensegrity 7 I realized the problems may be linked to the vitamin d3 supplementation. Now I'm somewhat perplexed as to how I should continue. For now, I'll be reducing my dose greatly and getting morning sun as much as possible. I've also been wondering if the low ph of Reverse Osmosis water is problematic for acid reflux. Thirdly, I previously mineralized my RO water by 2 different methods. The lack of minerals in the water makes me wonder if it can cause a problem. Recently, I've stopped mineralizing the RO water to see if I can get a better hydration and energy effect.

# Thread: The Three Legged Stool
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 31

### nonchalant — 2013-08-17T17:56:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-92927>

So you're outside, doing some yardwork. You come inside to cool off, and notice that you are hungry. You must have been too busy while you were working to realize you needed food. Right?

Wrong. While outside in the sun, you were soaking up the rays, relying on the sun to power your work. All was fine, until you came inside. Switch turned off, and your body/brain/leptin receptor says it's time for Plan B.

It's the same with magnetism. You are fine while snoozing on the Magnetico, or even playing cards on the bed. But get up just for a few minutes, and you might realize that you are now hungry. Plan B time.

It's so simple. Well, assuming everything is working right.

you are now seeing the Occam's razor of life's equation..........That is how to travel the road to optimal.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T16:55:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94048>

Looks like the sunshine works, at least for killing hunger, behind glass. I was riding in the car with DH, who does NOT like the windows rolled down while the A/C is on, when I got hungry, despite the magnet beneath me. I couldn't stick my arm out the window as usual. So I moved my solar panels while still behind the glass. Half my right thigh got some sunlight, and my lower arm as well. Sure enough, the magic happened. Hunger went away. I was a bit surprised, because I thought the lightly tinted (standard) glass blocks beneficial light, but it was enough to make me happy. Until he turned the corner. Darn it!

But there was a solution to that as well. I just put my feet on the dash. He didn't care much for that either, but my tummy overruled him. Sunlight on the tops of both feet, almost to the ankles, was enough.

I feel so .... enabled! Like I know The Secret!

### cinnamon — 2013-08-28T17:39:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94051>

nonchalant,

I have a solar powered radio. It can run on ordinary AA batteries, or on a rechargeable battery or directly from solar. When sun hits it (has to be strong enough) it can play the radio without a battery. The sun also recharges the rechargeable battery.

So, I'm wondering if the sun you got in the car was sufficient to keep you "playing" while it was shining directly on you. I wonder how much you would need so that it was enough to actually start recharging your rechargeable battery as well...

Did your hunger return when you got out of the sun? I mean, did the sun replace food? Or did it just allow you to delay eating?

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-28T18:43:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94063>

I love this thread.

I can already feel the beginnings of the seasonal change hitting me. mostly from the lack of sunlight upon waking during the work week, even though I'm stepping out side.

This morning, I grabbed my breakfast and went and sat out on the grass in my front yard to grab an little extra oomph... and felt much better, even though it was overcast and rainy.

I'm actually trying to figure out how to swing photoelectric therapy courtesy of a tanning bed and red light therapy at a tanning salon this winter....

this thread has highlighted one of the reasons I did so well when Living on Guam 12hrs of sunlight/darkness. I was at my lowest weight ever and I now know why... amazing isn't it

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T18:49:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94067>

Cinnamon, I'm guessing I would have to be outside for hours to begin to recharge anything. While I was in the car, and the sun became obscured by a cloud, I felt it immediately. Hunger soon followed. So it was 'just enough'. Plus I had a magnet below my seat, that probably helped. I usually have my window rolled down.

The sun allows me to delay eating. Usually hunger comes back right away. However, I have noticed, on occasion, that the hunger sometimes doesn't return when I leave the car and go into a house or a store. I think maybe I have had my arm out the window long enough to recharge a bit, or perhaps my body decided to burn a bit of fat instead. And there seems to be a difference in real hunger and just cravings. If it's time for dinner, it's time for dinner!

I remember long ago we had a vacation where we were sailing in a bay. One day we skipped breakfast, and then skipped lunch, and then about 4pm I figured we really aught to have a bite to eat, though neither of us were hungry one bit. We had no idea why, we figured we were just having too much fun to be hungry. We were young, and I wore long sleeves and a hat from fear of the sun. But that was a remarkable day. Spent the night in a pup tent on a sandbar. Woke up to stark terror, but that's another story.

Pretty soon I'll be sitting in the dark. Still plan on stopping because it's my morning zen time, but I need to find a sunshine alternative. Otherwise it will be lunch time before I can get back outside.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-29T03:14:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-94125>

Hi BJK! I've been using these under my car seat:

I discussed how I figured out how many in this thread: [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7853-Fun-with-Magnets/page2](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?7853-Fun-with-Magnets/page2)

Primal kitty has been really wolfing down the food, probably because she usually stays outside at night in the cold. During the day she might be sleeping in a chair and not notice that the sun is shining through the window. So I have started picking her up, walking into the south room and placing her on the sill, or taking her outside. She doesn't seem to mind my waking her up, once she sees she is getting some sunlight.

Hopefully she will slow down on the liver and fish...

### caroline — 2013-12-13T21:57:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-103346>

You have an epi-paleo cat that needs seafood???? I can barely afford seafood for myself .......lol

### nonchalant — 2014-01-06T01:41:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-104841>

It seems we humans have adaptations to maximize our exposure to the 3 legs. Our skin has reduced hair coverage to increase our exposure to sunshine. We have several adaptations to water, like the dive reflex, nostrils that allow us to be under water, bipedalism, etc. And even our feet allow maximum daytime exposure to the earth's magnetism -- we have lots of semiconductors in them, and we walk on our entire foot, not just on our toes.

### nonchalant — 2015-07-27T18:23:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-173229>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=104841)
>
> It seems we humans have adaptations to maximize our exposure to the 3 legs. Our skin has reduced hair coverage to increase our exposure to sunshine. We have several adaptations to water, like the dive reflex, nostrils that allow us to be under water, bipedalism, etc. And even our feet allow maximum daytime exposure to the earth's magnetism -- we have lots of semiconductors in them, and we walk on our entire foot, not just on our toes.

Is bipedalism an adaptation to increase our exposure to sunshine? If you are standing, almost all of your skin gets some sun exposure constantly throughout the day. Sure, being on two feet is also handy for carrying things, and walking in water, etc. But light exposure on more of the skin, including the belly, must have been really pivotal.

### nicld — 2015-07-27T19:06:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-three-legged-stool.7931/post-173236>

My tummy is nice and tanned now thanks to the nude sunbathing on the weekends. Too bad it is getting fluffier again.

# Thread: Crying over chopped veg
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crying-over-chopped-veg.7933/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nonchalant — 2013-08-17T20:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crying-over-chopped-veg.7933/post-92952>

Has anyone else noticed that chopping onions doesn't bother them any more?

It hasn't been bothering me, but I have been using very sweet onions, 1015 variety. But the other day I chopped a couple from our garden, and they didn't bother me. I didn't have to turn on the fan, walk away from the chop block a few times, or even reach for the onion chopper device. No stinging, no tears.

I personally think, that for me, CT is responsible. A year ago I found that the bull nettle didn't sting after about 5 minutes, and earlier this year I found that poison ivy doesn't break me out any more. This onion thing seems to be along the same lines.

To test, I bought a cheap yellow onion from the market, and chopped it up. Easy peasy, no blood, no sweat, no tears.

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-17T21:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/crying-over-chopped-veg.7933/post-92960>

# Thread: Speech issues
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-08-18T05:42:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93004>

Wondering if any of you knowledgeable folks can help me with this....

When I was very ill for a few years my speech became very mumbled, shallow breathing led to low volume, and it was very hard for people to understand me.

I know years ago I read the causes of this and I cant remember what it was to save my life!

I was a former opera singer and spoke with great clarity and diction. I never had trouble carrying on a conversation.

My 13 year old daughter is having this same problem, it has been going on for quite sometime and effects her socially as she is nervous about speaking up. Some times it is not happening and she is loud and clear and full of wonderful things to say. My heart breaks when I see her suffering with this.

I know its totally fixable....I fixed mine....I just don't know how i did it!! So frustrating!!

Thanks for the input.

(she has been treated for lyme, bartonella, hypothyroidism,babesia, yeast) Lots of abx....gut dysbiosis, vit A deficient......clues?

### cantweight — 2013-08-18T05:57:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93005>

I believe there is an association with parkinsons....similar mechanism

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-18T07:15:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93011>

read brain gut 5..............

### cantweight — 2013-08-18T16:58:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93029>

Read. Again. It certainly explains why she craves sardines!

She also takes a bunch of spirulina, 10k mcg B12 daily,drinks raw milk, eats a couple Brazil nuts, takes vitamin c with zinc.....not sure how else to help her.

More raw seafood seems like the answer but I am at a loss there as she didn't like oysters, and sushi which she loves is loaded with rice.

I'll just keep pushing on....thought maybe there was something I had missed.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-18T17:40:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93032>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=93004)
>
> I was a former opera singer and spoke with great clarity and diction.

Wow! Interesting folk on this forum.

I thought about asthma first.

I don't know if there is any connection, but I noticed a couple years ago that I started getting a foul mouth, to the point that I was wondering about myself. This was not in public, just in private with DH and also in my thoughts. When I improved my diet that went away. I donno, maybe I was able to think of better words?

### cantweight — 2013-08-18T18:13:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93038>

Seems very likely there is a similar mechanism at work.....she exhibits some belligerent behavior which is soooo unlike her. really short fused and bitchy. rude to her brothers. Its hard for me to decipher what is emerging teenager crap and what is occurring due to state of the body.

When the symptoms are not present she very apologetic and back to herself. She just started menstruating this spring....I am positive a lot of this is cyclical.

As if it wasn't hard enough to figure out my own cycle/hormone issues!!! ugh!

### ColdKnight — 2013-08-18T20:26:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speech-issues.7934/post-93050>

One idea, is eat scallops or oysters with a glass of water. Just 1/2 them or 1/4 them and take them like a supplement. I'm about to try this with liver

### fitness@home — 2013-08-18T14:19:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/energy-and-epigenetics-2-is-now-live.7936/post-93020>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-18T15:52:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/energy-and-epigenetics-2-is-now-live.7936/post-93022>

[http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-2-the-real-dha-story/](http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-and-epigenetics-2-the-real-dha-story/)

Commitment will sacrifice by nature because they have already laid down their all to get here. It's easier to die when you are already dead first in your mind. Just as it is easier to sacrifice when you have already sacrificed first in your mind. The greater the love, the greater will be the sacrifice. Love sacrifices with no strings attached. It never asks for a penny in return. It sacrifices initially and it sacrifices always. And that's why sacrificial love grips our very being. This is why a parent will sacrifice for their children, and how a person can fight a war they do not believe in. A soldier doesn't fight because he hates the enemy in front of him, he fights because he loves what he left behind. Committed people ask when should I sacrifice and how much? Involved people ask why should I sacrifice and how come? How much are we willing to sacrifice a part of life, our time, our comforts, or just a slice of ourselves? How considerate are we towards others who are less privileged, less informed, or who have less in life? Are we unselfish enough to share what we have for others? We give love and affection when we sacrifice something. If we have no success in our battles of life yet, it maybe because we have forgotten that we belong to each other. Today, share something with someone. A bone to the dog is not charity. Charity is the bone shared with the dog, when you are just as hungry as the dog.

I love the feeling of CT but HATE the rewarding time. Since it is summer here ans I need to get my vitamin D levels much much higher, on the weekends after a long CT session I have been going and sitting in the sun. It feels oh oh so good but today I noticed something different. My legs git all tingly like thousands of needles pricking me.

Anyone else have this happen and am I undoing all of the benefits of CT by rewarming in the sun?

# Thread: Solid Improvements
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solid-improvements.7939/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Martin — 2013-08-19T00:13:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solid-improvements.7939/post-93062>

**The Ache**
My latest successes have been around the Mitochondrial RX and how it's given me some much needed relief with this chronic fatigue and fibromyalgia that got ramped up five years ago after a severe illness. Some things have helped a little, but when I got into my third week of the RX, things shifted towards less pain, more energy.

**Useful Work**
I continued adding and subtracting the different supplements to get a sweet spot and it has really helped. I am able to do more chores and work on projects and actually have fun doing it! I take things in stride a lot better. I have done some gardening and orchard work after nearly two years of virtual inactivity. I've been able to redo some poor wiring in the house to lessen the fields.

**Focus!**
Mental clarity has improved as well. I'm not burning pots on the stove so much these days. I don't forget to feed my chickens… and my dog. And, oh yeah, my DW! I do the cooking these days, as some of you may know. Mentally, it's brought me out of a funk that was no fun at all.

**Going Exterior**
I find myself being further "out" from my body with less pain. I'm not imploding with the chronic ache in my legs and arms like before. I'm looking "outward" like I used to do, and re-starting old dreams and projects, and even some art has come up on the radar.

**Self-Absorption**
I move around the farm here, but don't exercise as such, but I've become more muscular by just eating more like a Great White. My skin staying tighter than the other guys I know that are my age. Lots of water and bone broth soups. I pass a mirror and have to stop and admire my good looks, and it's hard to get away from a full length mirror these days. That's what Big Headed people do. It's a healthy ego trip. Just wait 'til I start working out! A certain Aussie we all know and love may start paddling northward heading this way!

**Sleep like a Dummy**
I sleep like a baby, it's usually 9 to 10 hours, and while it's still tough to get up, it's a lot easier these months and years on this Paleo journey. I don't stay up late anymore, and find I look forward to sleep soon after the sun goes down. Blue blockers, of course.

**Meal Tickets**
My appetite has stabilized a lot. My in-between meal cravings and crashes have all but disappeared. I never snack… well, almost. I do sardines often. It's an inexpensive way to get my DHA. I've done oysters, salmon, other fish, shrimp, and all the paleo foods in between. Coconut oil is my best friend. Butter is my lover. Curcumin is my next conquest.

**What a Drip**
Okay, I'm going out here a bit. I didn't think I could ever admit to this, but it's huge for me and the perfect thing to share to cut-down-to-size the guy with the Big Head. Since the Mito RX, I've been able to stop wearing those g\*\*damn diapers! I was having a little trouble with the little guy drooling a bit and finally had to buy the things and figured that I'd have to for the rest of my life. Not! What a win! There, I said it. I know many of you are crushed and disheartened, perhaps put off a bit, but I can assure you, I'm back in the game!

I have a way of life now that I can do rather than try. I'm not lost in a world of latest and greatest "diet this", and "eat that", crusades that fall short of what a Human needs.

This is the place! I'm home.

And even posting more.

B. G. Head

### caroline — 2013-08-19T00:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solid-improvements.7939/post-93063>

Well Martin ...what can I say .....you already know that I am crazy about you \[and I do have standards!\] I am overwhelmed and overjoyed for you and, almost speechless....You are an amazing man and gentleman ...your DW is one lucky woman....

### caroline — 2013-08-19T00:41:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solid-improvements.7939/post-93064>

I have got to get me a big picture ..... you are having way to much fun these days.....

God knows where we will be a year from now.....we aren't getting older- we are getting way, way better

>
> **Self-Absorption**
> I move around the farm here, but don't exercise as such, but I've become more muscular by just eating more like a Great White. My skin staying tighter than the other guys I know that are my age. Lots of water and bone broth soups. I pass a mirror and have to stop and admire my good looks, and it's hard to get away from a full length mirror these days. That's what Big Headed people do. It's a healthy ego trip. Just wait 'til I start working out! A certain Aussie we all know and love may start paddling northward heading this way!
>
> **Sleep like a Dummy**
> I sleep like a baby, it's usually 9 to 10 hours, and while it's still tough to get up, it's a lot easier these months and years on this Paleo journey. I don't stay up late anymore, and find I look forward to sleep soon after the sun goes down. Blue blockers, of course.
>
> **Meal Tickets**
> My appetite has stabilized a lot. My in-between meal cravings and crashes have all but disappeared. I never snack… well, almost. I do sardines often. It's an inexpensive way to get my DHA. I've done oysters, salmon, other fish, shrimp, and all the paleo foods in between. Coconut oil is my best friend. Butter is my lover. Curcumin is my next conquest.
>
> **What a Drip**
> Okay, I'm going out here a bit. I didn't think I could ever admit to this, but it's huge for me and the perfect thing to share to cut-down-to-size the guy with the Big Head. Since the Mito RX, I've been able to stop wearing those g\*\*damn diapers! I was having a little trouble with the little guy drooling a bit and finally had to buy the things and figured that I'd have to for the rest of my life. Not! What a win! There, I said it. I know many of you are crushed and disheartened, perhaps put off a bit, but I can assure you, I'm back in the game!
>
> I have a way of life now that I can do rather than try. I'm not lost in a world of latest and greatest "diet this", and "eat that", crusades that fall short of what a Human needs.
>
> This is the place! I'm home.
>
> And even posting more.
>
> B. G. Head

It is amazing how alike our symptoms are. I have that annoying dripping problem right after I urinate. Did you used to have spine pain also?
