**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/facebook-is-bad-for-you-get-a-life.7941/post-93073>

.... They found that the most common emotion aroused by using Facebook is envy. Endlessly comparing themselves with peers who have doctored their photographs, amplified their achievements and plagiarised their bons mots can leave Facebookâ€™s users more than a little green-eyed.

[http://www.economist.com/news/scien...ork-seems-make-people-more-miserable-get-life](http://www.economist.com/news/science-and-technology/21583593-using-social-network-seems-make-people-more-miserable-get-life)

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
Question everything. Previously I have read that we are the collective sum of the 5 people that we are closest to. For some people (or maybe many people, social media such as FB could easily be one of those 5 people). Bottom line ... if a person and/or technology is bad for us, we need to greatly reduce or eliminate the negative impact.

[http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true](http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true)

### Shijin13 — 2013-08-19T17:09:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risks-of-helping-people-in-our-modern-world-ponder-the-implications.7944/post-93105>

As humans we protect what we believe is ours, when in reality it never was ours alone - it's everyone's. this is what Steve is challenging within the Status Quo. those in power are fearful of the masses waking up to realize this...

### beach bunny — 2013-08-19T17:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risks-of-helping-people-in-our-modern-world-ponder-the-implications.7944/post-93107>

>
> [http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true](http://www.menshealth.com/fitness/should-giving-diet-advice-be-against-law?fullpage=true)

So much food for thought in this article...I keep feeling like everything in this life...it's an issue of consciousness and empowerment..or lack thereof.
Even if each individual that makes up an organization has "good intentions" collectively they can still dis-empower members of a society, and inevitably enslave them to their version of the truth (even if it's killing people).
Sometimes fighting "the system" can add fuel to the fire and they just push back harder. So then the truth is once again muddled by big egos.
It is such a paradox.
Every day I pray for each of us to wake up to our own personal power...then doctors and patients become one, learning from each other...then our ideas about health radically change..
This is why I so appreciate this blog, when you're not just saying "believe this" but instead encourage us to read, learn and test everything in our own N=1.

### Hope — 2013-08-20T17:15:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-risks-of-helping-people-in-our-modern-world-ponder-the-implications.7944/post-93233>

The title of this article is actually,

Should Giving Diet Advice Be Against the Law?

I didn't have time to read the article, just the title on the website, but I know that the ADA (A. dietetics association) has been working hard on keeping non-registered dietitians from giving DIET advice to protect their people (who go to school and take exams and so on and work in hospitals etc etc). This is very very different than dispensing MEDICAL advice. Helping someone with food should not ever be illegal in my book. Doing medicine is different. No one has ever died I think from someone getting help from someone else, nutritionist or blogger even (at least not that I am aware of it) about how to change their diet. Medical advice, in person, phone (Which is what I do with my amazing MD who is out of state) is very different. I am sure everyone on here knows that the **3rd largest cause** of death at least in the US is.....consequences of medical advice. Here's a quick article about that....

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iatrogenesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iatrogenesis)

# Thread: Onions and histamine release
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/onions-and-histamine-release.7945/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 29

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-19T17:31:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/onions-and-histamine-release.7945/post-93110>

Onions: help decrease histamine release. An important health-producing ingredient in onions is quercetin. Studies have shown that baking, sauteing roasting or frying onions increases their quercetin content. Boiling is the only cooking method that reduces quercetin content.

# Thread: Office ergonomics ... good article on using how technology disrupts our bodies
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/office-ergonomics-good-article-on-using-how-technology-disrupts-our-bodies.7947/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-19T23:07:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/office-ergonomics-good-article-on-using-how-technology-disrupts-our-bodies.7947/post-93141>

**Office ergonomics ... good article on how using technology disrupts our bodies**

Below are selected excerpts from a lengthy, but interesting article.

\-----
"Over the past six or seven years we have realized the consequences of people sitting too long, and it is an astonishing list." Specifically, he lists (in no particular order) diabetes, low productivity, hypertension, apathy, clinical depression, hyperlipidemia (elevated levels of lipids in the blood), low moods or mild depression, cardiovascular diseases, obesity, back problems, deep vein thrombosis and mental sluggishness.
\-----
Tablets, for instance, might at first glance seem to free people from their desks, but in fact people tend to place them flat on their desks and read them as if they were books, explains Dr. Alan Hedge, director of the Human Factors and Ergonomics Laboratory at Cornell University in Ithaca, N.Y. With a book, people will occasionally change posture as they turn the page, but with tablets they can remain hunched for long periods, he notes.

"Leaning forward doubles the compressive forces on the vertebrae in your lower back compared to leaning back," explains Hedge. "When leaning back 20 degrees in a lounge chair you are really relaxing and halving the compression. That is why we say 'sit back and relax,' not 'hunch forward and relax.' "

One result of hunching is a syndrome called iPad Neck -- chronic soreness of the back of the neck and upper shoulders. Hedge recommends propping up the tablet or putting it on a holder so you can read it with a straight neck.
\-----
Overuse of tablets may also interfere with getting a good night's sleep, says Dr. Mariana Figueiro, director of the Light and Health Program of the Lighting Research Center at the Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute in Troy, N.Y. Exposure to bright light in the evening will suppress the body's production of the sleep-aid chemical melatonin, making it harder to go to sleep.
\-----
Laptops, meanwhile, are considered non-ergonomic by nature, since there is no way to adjust the distance between the keyboard and screen. "Your hands want to be close to your chest, but your eyes want to be focused on something two feet in front of you," making an ergonomic posture unobtainable with a laptop, Hedge explains.

Using laptops on a desk, in place of a desktop, only compounds the problem, he adds. "Desks are usually about 30 inches high, which is a good height for writing by hand on paper but dreadful for typing, as it is too high unless you are taller than 6'2". Then you are adding the height of the laptop."
\-----
As for smartphones, the reliance on thumbs for texting has led to an upsurge of a condition variously called BlackBerry Thumb, Text Thumb, Nintendo Thumb or De Quervain Syndrome.
\-----
"The signal to stand up does something to the body that tunes it, controlling the blood pressure and circulation," says Vernikos. "Every 20 to 30 minutes you need to stand up. More often is fine, but doing it 20 times at once and saying you are done is not sufficient; you must do it throughout the day."

She bases her conclusions on research she did for NASA on the bodily effects of having no gravitational stress, such as what astronauts experience in free fall. Volunteers stayed in bed for a month; some of them got up to use a treadmill and others just got up. A third group just stayed in bed. The completely bedbound volunteers lost 25% of their aerobic capacity in just four days, Vernikos recalls.
\-----

[http://www.computerworld.com/s/arti...nomics\_?source=CTWNLE\_nlt\_thisweek\_2013-08-19](http://www.computerworld.com/s/article/9241435/Surprise_Mobile_devices_don_t_help_office_ergonomics_?source=CTWNLE_nlt_thisweek_2013-08-19)

Dave asprey is saying that he got leaky gut from not eating carbs with his ketogenic diet.He ate mostly broccoli as his carbs. Supposedly, you don't make the right bile or enzymes w/out carbs/starch. I've been on and of keto for over a year, and a year ago or so I got a minor hives rash on torso only. Its gotten a little worse over the last 6 months or so. Considering you like the keto diet, I just wanted to know your thoughts on this Dr Kruse. My friend told me this today, and I didn't know what to tell him.

### yewwei.tan — 2013-08-20T05:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-from-lack-of-starchy-carbs.7949/post-93196>

Very interesting. One question that follows is how to manage the transition; From what I understand, until your cells regain their ability to tell time and access the PPP, you will not be able to make enough endogenous D-ribose to fuel the fat burning pathways, and thereby be dependent largely on the fast energy pathways and experience all the negative symptoms commonly seen in very low carb diets.

**You mention that this process of adaptation to living in the PPP can take 12-24 months, and I'd assume that the negative side effects that Asprey mentioned would persist that entire time.** You then mention in the EMF4 blog post that:

The change to optimal fat burning can be shortened when you acclimate to making enough D-ribose from your PPP.

So obviously getting the body to produce enough D-ribose endogenously is the key.

I've been on the ketogenic diet for about 2 months, since it's winter time in Australia where I'm at. Since starting, I've also experienced some additional skin breakouts (especially in the ankles), and feel that some of the tolerance toward sub-optimal foods has decreased (eg: I'm getting skin issues even after just a tiny cup of coffee).

I'm trying to think of a plan to reduce this transition time. So far, it's been the usual suspects:

\- eat Epi-Paleo + EPCOTx due to a leaky gut. Seafood is decently cheap and easy to find here in Australia.
\- get a magnetic sleep mattress and reduce EMF exposure
\- daily CT
\- lots of sleep (9+hrs a day, usualy 10pm to 7+am)
\- appropriate supplementation

**Now I'm wondering whether or not the following could be helpful in speeding up adaptation time to entering the PPP**:

\- not doing any exercise that depends heavily on the CP system
\- supplementing with D-ribose specifically when exercise is performed
\- adding some extra MCT oil in the mornings to accelerate the generation of ketones

\----

I come from an athletic background, and many times, "ketogenic diets" are implemented with the levels of protein often recommended by the athletic community. Typically, this leads to a level of 1 - 1.5g protein per pound bodyweight. **For a 22 year old 183lbs male like me,** that's 180g to 270g of protein a day. **I eat maybe 0.7 - 1.0kg of meat a day, so around 180 - 220g of protein.**

Protein ilicits an insulin response to some degree, and excess protein can be turned into glucose. **Am I right in assuming that my level of protein intake could actually hinder my adaptation to burning fat for fuel**?

I know you hate this type of question, but would you say an intake of 100-150g of protein a day for someone my size is appropriate?

\----

Speaking of skin breakouts Another misc point from EMF4.

Why are the symptoms first found in a leaky gut or in skin breakouts? The cells of both organs turn over in 2-3 days in a normal person.

Does this also mean that rate of skin recovery after a breakout is an indicator that you are doing things right again?

\----

Thanks.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-20T06:22:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-from-lack-of-starchy-carbs.7949/post-93197>

Skin.........comes up many times.......and I have said three times in the last few blogs......neuroectoderm is brain and skin......is tied to the photoelectric effect: Vitamin A cycle and Vitamin D cycle...........you are assuming it only takes 2-3 days.......in you.......it does in a person with low NFkappabeta and IL-6 in tissues derived from these.......it means what you are assuming maybe not reality......this is why I advocating testing. When your on the road back you can not trust your own beliefs......use batteries of tests and keep your self constantly curious.

# Thread: A Few Testosterone Replacement Quesitons
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-08-20T01:47:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93160>

Haven't posted in a while cause I've been trying to spend less time on the computer but I'm still pluggin away JK style as much as possible.

Went to my endo today and I was so glad that he prescribed testosterone replacement but when I got to the pharmacy I found out that my insurance won't cover the Androgel that he prescribed so it would be about $325 - Not gonna work.

So my other two options are possibly the Androderm patch or IM injections.

I will have to wait 2 weeks to find out if my insurance will cover the patch.

If not my only other option is injections.

I've read online about subcutaneous injections for testosterone instead of IM.

I asked the doctors nurse about it and she said that's not possible with testosterone (obviously it's a newer technology).

I figured if I could find a pharmacy that caries the SubQ that I might get the doc to prescribe it.

Walgreens doesn't carry it and nether does CVS - at least not the ones I called.

So i've got 2 questions:

1) What do you think of the patch vs IM injections?

2) Any ideas about finding a pharmacy that supplies SubQ

If I'm gonna do this long term I think I'd rather do SubQ injections over always wearing a patch.

From what I've read the advantages of SubQ over IM is that they are less painful and you can do them once or twice per week so you don't have the UPs and Downs that you have with the IM injections.

Any input is greatly appreciated

Thanks for reading

### Huck — 2013-08-20T03:16:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93172>

Out of curiosity, do you have a varicocele (varicose vein of the testicles)? It's sometimes described as a sack of worms! It usually occurs on the left testicle and will feel swollen, sometimes more than others. Similar to a varicose vein in legs, but this occurs in the scrotum.

The reason I ask is there is evidence that low testosterone can be a result of varicoceles causing free testosterone to back-flow into the prostate rather than into the blood stream. A varicocele is a vein with a defective valve which prevents blood from flowing through the vein. If your left testicle hangs lower than your right testicle, that is a good indication of varicocele.

I am interested in varicocele because a couple of Israeli doctors have studies and case studies that show that varicoceles cause BPH and prostate cancer. They initially treated varicoceles as a means of treating infertility (low testosterone) in men. Treating varicoceles usually results in an increase in testosterone. So if you have one or more varicocoles, treatment may restore your testosterone and also prevent BPH and prostate cancer.

ETA: Direct sun for at least 2 hours a day increased my testosterone more than testosterone cream did and with fewer side effects.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-20T03:32:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93177>

That's very interesting.

Ever since I had my vasectomy about 4 years ago I have felt something weird down there.

It's as if the tube in each sac is just moving around.

I have always wondered about that.

I wouldn't say that one hangs lower than the other though.

If I do have that what is the solution and can it be easily diagnosed?

> [Huck said:](/goto/post?id=93172)
>
> Out of curiosity, do you have a varicocele (varicose vein of the testicles)? It's sometimes described as a sack of worms! It usually occurs on the left testicle and will feel swollen, sometimes more than others. Similar to a varicose vein in legs, but this occurs in the scrotum.
>
> The reason I ask is there is evidence that low testosterone can be a result of varicoceles causing free testosterone to back-flow into the prostate rather than into the blood stream. A varicocele is a vein with a defective valve which prevents blood from flowing through the vein. If your left testicle hangs lower than your right testicle, that is a good indication of varicocele.
>
> I am interested in varicocele because a couple of Israeli doctors have studies and case studies that show that varicoceles cause BPH and prostate cancer. They initially treated varicoceles as a means of treating infertility (low testosterone) in men. Treating varicoceles usually results in an increase in testosterone. So if you have one or more varicocoles, treatment may restore your testosterone and also prevent BPH and prostate cancer.
>
> ETA: Direct sun for at least 2 hours a day increased my testosterone more than testosterone cream did and with fewer side effects.

### Huck — 2013-08-20T03:49:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-few-testosterone-replacement-quesitons.7951/post-93180>

> [fireforlunch said:](/goto/post?id=93177)
>
> That's very interesting.
>
> Ever since I had my vasectomy about 4 years ago I have felt something weird down there.
>
> It's as if the tube in each sac is just moving around.
>
> I have always wondered about that.
>
> I wouldn't say that one hangs lower than the other though.
>
> If I do have that what is the solution and can it be easily diagnosed?

It can be easily diagnosed if your doctor knows what to look for. There are 3 levels: identified by feel, identified visually, and identified by instruments. By instruments is the smallest and by sight is the largest. Many males notice a varicocele when they are in their twenties. I remember going to doctor when I was in my mid 20's thinking I might have a rupture. The doc laughed and said I had varicose veins of the left testicle. Doctors since then have said it is not a problem, but now I know better. it should have been repaired years ago. I think I read that about 70% of all males get a varicocele at some point.

I believe there is a standard treatment for varicocele for infertility and then a more complex procedure for varicocele and BPH.

Do a search on varicocele testosterone and varicocele embolization. There are also some youtube videos.

ETA: Don't forget CT. Cold helps increase testosterone.

# Thread: Confusing DHEA labs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-20T16:56:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93230>

Which is more accurate for checking DHEA levels, blood test or saliva?

I had a DHEA blood test done on 7/15 and I was taking about 25-50 mg of DHEA and I got a value of 233 ng/dl.

Just got the results back from a saliva DHEAS test and it read 32.3 ng/ml and I did not take any DHEA a day before the test or the day of the test and I had increased the supplementation to 50mg to 100mg based on my first test for the 2-3 week prior to the test.

The good range for the blood test was 102-1185 ng/dl.
The good range for the saliva test is 2-23 ng/ml based on age.

According to the blood test I am low but according to the saliva test I am too high and when I try to do the conversion to the same units is just does not make sense.

**Which one do I go with?**

Thanks

### JanSz — 2013-08-20T20:15:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93256>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=93230)
>
> Which is more accurate for checking DHEA levels, blood test or saliva?
>
> I had a DHEA blood test done on 7/15 and I was taking about 25-50 mg of DHEA and I got a value of 233 ng/dl.
>
> Just got the results back from a saliva DHEAS test and it read 32.3 ng/ml and I did not take any DHEA a day before the test or the day of the test and I had increased the supplementation to 50mg to 100mg based on my first test for the 2-3 week prior to the test.
>
> The good range for the blood test was 102-1185 ng/dl.
> The good range for the saliva test is 2-23 ng/ml based on age.
>
> According to the blood test I am low but according to the saliva test I am too high and when I try to do the conversion to the same units is just does not make sense.
>
> **Which one do I go with?**
>
> Thanks

This is pretty good question. Wish I had better answer.

when testing, DHEA=(free DHEA), DHEAs=DHEAs(storage molecule)--->Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone\_sulfate](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dehydroepiandrosterone_sulfate)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steroid\_sulfatase](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steroid_sulfatase)

> DHEA is produced from cholesterol through two cytochrome P450 enzymes. Cholesterol is converted to pregnenolone by the enzyme P450 scc (side chain cleavage); then another enzyme, CYP17A1, converts pregnenolone to 17α-hydroxypregnenolone and then to DHEA.

> Dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate is produced by the addition of a sulfate group, catalyzed by the **sulfotransferase enzymes SULT1A1 and SULT1E1**, which also produce estrone sulfate from estrone. DHEA sulfate can also be back-converted to DHEA through the action of steroid sulfatase

.

in blood we can test DHEA & DHEAs
in saliva we can test only DHEA (free DHEA)

proper balance between DHEA & DHEAs levels depends on actions of steroid sulfatase

Blood tests are commonly used.
I have seen number of answers as to applicability of saliva testing. The answer usually depends on author's inclinations.

High DHEA levels are sign of problem (DHEA is produced in the adrenal glands, the gonads, and the brain)(PCOS)
Most often after 20yo DHEAs levels are falling.

For whatever reason there is at least two groups of responses to given dose of supplemental DHEA.
Some (if not most), respond quickly to dose 25-50 mg of DHEA, (when checked by blood test)
To achieve desirable DHEAs, others need much more supplemental DHEA,
I use 300mg/day, dr Kruse was using 500mg/day during his hack.

**Until now I am using DHEA pills and monitoring DHEAs,serum.

Goal
men (350-490) μg/dL
women (275-400) μg/dL**

Goal for DHEAs levels come from lef.org
likely from dr Dzugan

.....................

DHEAs participation in properly functioning Steroid Hormone Panel is very high.
Cortisol(AM), cortisol is next.

When measuring only most notable Steroid Hormones and thaking it as 100%
DHEAs participation is 95%
Cortisol(AM) 4.25%
the 0.75% is for all other.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?6427-PG-E2-ratio&p=81508#post81508)

//////

### JanSz — 2013-08-20T20:42:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93258>

Timely post by SueC :

[http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?3941-7-Keto-Brand-matters/page5](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?3941-7-Keto-Brand-matters/page5)

> How to get the best results when monitoring DHEA therapy -
> There have been some questions lately about how to best monitor DHEA replacement therapy. And rightly so, whether using DHEA or 7 –keto DHEA, **topical or oral**, the selection of testing type is very important for accurate assessment of bioavailable hormone.

See more at: [http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals...-monitoring-dhea-therapy#sthash.zwJTD1mq.dpuf](http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals/zrt-blogs/entry/how-to-get-the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy#sthash.zwJTD1mq.dpuf)

[http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals...the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy](http://www.zrtlab.com/professionals/zrt-blogs/entry/how-to-get-the-best-results-when-monitoring-dhea-therapy)

////////

### nicld — 2013-08-20T21:11:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93260>

Very interesting but still confusing.

I am not on the 7-Keto DHEA but the test did show that my testosterone was a bit elevated and I am getting more acne. Don't know of the acne is from the DHEA or the estrogen dominance. I just might stop it for the week and see that the neuropath doc says about it when I see him and hope he can help. Oh and my cortisol levels are good too.

Thanks

### JanSz — 2013-08-21T00:29:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93286>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=93260)
>
> Very interesting but still confusing.
>
> I am not on the 7-Keto DHEA but the test did show that my testosterone was a bit elevated and I am getting more acne. Don't know of the acne is from the DHEA or the estrogen dominance. I just might stop it for the week and see that the neuropath doc says about it when I see him and hope he can help. Oh and my cortisol levels are good too.
>
> Thanks

I think that you have not made the right test, hence confusion.

> I had a DHEA blood test done on 7/15 and I was taking about 25-50 mg of DHEA and I got a value of 233 ng/dl.
>
> Just got the results back from a saliva DHEAS test and it read 32.3 ng/ml and I did not take any DHEA a day before the test or the day of the test and I had increased the supplementation to 50mg to 100mg based on my first test for the 2-3 week prior to the test.

You want to do
DHEAs, serum
test.

on LabCorp:

Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate--Test Number: 004020 CPT Code: 82627

On that test you aim at (regardless of your age)(disregard laboratory provided range):
men (350-490) μg/dL
women (275-400) μg/dL

You may get more information here:

[http://www.lef.org/protocols/index.htm](http://www.lef.org/protocols/index.htm)

DHEA Restoration Therapy
[http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic\_health/dhea\_restoration\_01.htm](http://www.lef.org/protocols/metabolic_health/dhea_restoration_01.htm)

////////////////////////

Are you men or woman?
If woman, menstruating or not?

For menstruating woman, when working on steroid hormone panel, it is easier (on the beginning) to do blood draws on day 3-4 of menstruating cycle.

Latter on, one try to add blood draw also on days on maximal progesterone levels (19-21).

//

### nicld — 2013-08-21T20:32:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/confusing-dhea-labs.7955/post-93363>

I did not have the blood one done by Labcorp but by my doctors office and it was sent to Quest Diagnostics. I looked up the test and it was the blood serum one so it should be good.

I am a woman who is still cycling. The saliva steroid panel was done at the max progesterone levels (and those are way low). The blood DHEA test was not done at the beginning of my cycle of at the max (doctor did not tell me that.....grrrrr). I wish that they would all use the same unit of measure too.

Until I see the doctor about this I have backed off of the DHEA. I just don't want to mess anything up and with getting two different readings, I need help.

When I need further testing I will be sure to check out Labcorp and see how the prices and the tests compare to the doctors office. There is a Labcorp 30 minutes from my work but if the price it the same, it would not be worth the extra gas money to go there if I can get the same test 5 minutes from work.

Thanks

I think you would need to get a good Dr. that you click with and works with you on it..... My Dr. knows what is going on in my life and what I am doing health-wise.......

My DR. explained to me that what ever natural T I have will come back up If I decide to go off. He would monitor it so the transition won't be so abrupt.... and try to do as much as we can to kick up my natural production, how ever much it is.... But it won't destroy my naturally production..... just suppresses it. That was my understanding...

Also my testies haven't shrunken up or anything like a Body builder on gear.... So I still have some natural production. I just get my T levels up to what would be, optimal upper range would be naturally if I was doing things right..... which I do anyway..... I don't take it and then not care about keeping up my natural production. I still do everything health wise, living the PPP - Jack Kruse protocol to keep health and fitness markers optimized. I'm not a slacker relying on T supplementation to get me through life..... I use it to optimize and live the best quality of life I can....

We get one chance in life..... There is no rehearsals or do overs......

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-21T02:52:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/another-testosterone-question-for-you-pros.7958/post-93311>

Mine tanked after my vesactomy.... I dodn't know it for years but one day relized my labido was down, a bit of depression set in, my energy ans sleep was not good, etc. etc.
Started thinking how before I got my "V" I was a stallion...... My energy was always high, always active, raced and won triathlons, business was great because I was a go getter...... Researched, ran across HRT..... So I went and got tested.... my range was below the lower range of T levels for my age... My free was even lower and bioavalable was even lower..... And I was fit, had muscle, lean etc. I was doing all the right things already I was just having the above symptoms. So I was pretty close to optimizing what low levels I had....

After testing and recapping my life story and chain of events with my DR. We decided that it was a great possibility it was from getting cut..... So I first started with the gels to see how things would go and that way I have a bit of control.... Well it helped. It also jacked my wife's T levels up.... lol So we decided to do the pellets. We started with a smaller dose insert at first then tested after a while.... We then kept adding until it was in a good range that would stay in a good range for about 5 months.... He said I may have been able to go to 6 but we test every 5 and insert then as needed.

### Terminator — 2013-08-20T19:57:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-93252>

Magnetico for sure. I have both. However Earthpulse is very good also especially in "Recover Mode" and after you get used to it.

### Terminator — 2013-08-26T05:29:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-93797>

I suggest you to read / research earthpulse as much as possible before you buy. I did just that. Ben Greenfield uses it also. I use it to sleep with it, mostly in "Recover" setting. This setting is by far the best. Following is what it does to me ;
1\. Sleep much beter.
2\. Recover much faster after very hard road bike workouts.
3\. Heal wounds much faster. I only experinced small cuts. My magnetico pad did not do that.

I'm now debating if i should get another earthpulse unit to use at work or to get Total shield for whole house and portable unit.

About Magnetico pad.
The reason i liked magnetico (note that it's past tense) is beacouse sometime my sleep was so good to that i would wake up after 6 hours and did not know where i was. lol This good sleep diminished in past 3 weeks. Why ? Not suere but i may have not used it properly. I now know that you cannot be around the pad during the day due to positive magnetic fields. I did that alot for 1.5 months. I only own one single Core Pad which was placed under one side of king size bed. Me and my wife would switch side to get the benefit but according to Magnetico this is bad. The side without pad is exposed to positive magnetic fields and that is bad. This may explain why i had not so great results in last few weeks. I'm upset at myself for not fully researching ang learning how to properly use it and pissed that the user manual does not state this. I currently cannot use my bedroom for just sleep so to avoid bad positive magnetic fields i removed it. I was very close to get magnetico for all members of my family but i'm holding back because same reasons.

I was not able to use both, earthpulse and pad at same time. My brain acted as if i was on caffeine.

Now i want to do more research on magnetism, maybe call magnetico to get more info from them to learn how to use it properly.

### greekgeo — 2013-09-05T14:58:11+0300

I have researched Schumann generators and the Earthpulse which I like a lot and may end up purchasing one if I can find more positive live feedback. I really like PEMF technology (a bit of a tie in to the Bob Beck protocol) and would also use the Earthpulse to treat ailments ... Tinnitus with recovery mode? Its not clear to me how the Total Shield is strong enough to protect you from extraneous EMF.

Any info from people who have used the Earthpulse and the Magnetico at the same time would be appreciated ... thanks!

The journey for sound sleep continues.

### Awainer1 — 2013-09-12T03:21:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-95667>

Hey guys. I've been wondering about this too I own an earthpulse (the less expensive one). I started with the earthpulse under my mattress and after the first few nights of great sleep I kinda got used to it and couldnt feel the difference anymore. ### Danco3636 — 2013-09-12T03:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-sleep-pad-or-earthpulse.7959/post-95670>

I had (still have it if someone wants to buy it) an earth pulse. Have me restless nights and weird dreams. I thought perhaps it was my mattress due to the coil springs. So I swapped out my mattress with a no metal coil memory foam one. Very firm and love it. Same thing with the Earthpulse. Once removed I sleep much better, very well. # Thread: Could Hypothyroid  Cause Low Testosterone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-hypothyroid-cause-low-testosterone.7960/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### fireforlunch — 2013-08-20T19:22:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-hypothyroid-cause-low-testosterone.7960/post-93247>

A little weary about taking testosterone for the rest of my life.

Not sure I really want to move forward with this.

When I saw the Endo yesterday he said I would rather not start you on 2 meds at once so since your testosterone is much worse than your thyroid lets start there then test your thyroid again in 6 weeks when we test your testosterone levels.

While reading online today I realized that The Hypothyroid could be causing the low testosterone.

See this article:

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10671947](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10671947)

Now I'm really nervous about administering this first injection today.

**If I do the testosterone for 6 weeks and my thyroid is still low could I in theory go off the testosterone and try armour and see if the thyroid fixes the low t?**

Thanks for your input

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-20T19:34:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-hypothyroid-cause-low-testosterone.7960/post-93249>

Hypothyroidism can impact many things. If I were in your shoes, I would address the thyroid first. And remember the thyroid and adrenal fatigue often go together.

Another consideration ... Dr. Kruse has said that HCG injections may be a preferable method of raising testosterone. After addressing your thyroid, you might want to research T injections vs. HCG injections.

### fireforlunch — 2013-08-20T20:05:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-hypothyroid-cause-low-testosterone.7960/post-93254>

remember the hormone cascade:

if you don't have enough Free T3 and Vitamin A you can't convert the LDL to pregnenenolone in the first hormone in the hormone cascade for sex hormones...

# Thread: Tips to help someone very new to all of this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/>
**Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-08-20T19:55:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93251>

My cousin contacted me last night....she has been suffering with hives for close to 2 years now and major leaky gut issues. She endured the loss of a child and another born with hydro-encephalitis. Multiple mis-carriages as well. She has seen stress at her young age that I can not imagine.

She is an amazing person that smiles in spite of it all, has a deeply rooted faith and wonderful zest for life. She has three young children and is the kind of mom I aspire to be.

She contacted me last night and said that her doc said she has an enlarged thyroid and is scheduled for ultrasound and she has developed allergies to peanuts, strawberries, chocolate, milk, and soy.

I immediately tell her to read The iodine Crisis, send her here and some GAPS info, explain a little leaky gut, and talk paleo. She responded that she doesnt really have any GI issues so this is not her case and she couldn't cook like that and get dinner on the table with 3 babies in tow every night. She is on the other end of the spectrum diet wise....home cooked meals but SAD ingredients. SAD for the whole family. Does herbal-life shakes for weight loss that is not happening. My heart breaks for her.

It is interesting to us both.....we are cousins and resemble each other quite a bit, have weight issues, and we have the same blue eyes with a little brown speck in one iris.

My question is....do y'all have any beginner type websites or info that will not overwhelm someone that is starting at the other end of the spectrum? Good explanations? Diet that seem more doable to someone at the very beginning of this kind of journey? I dont want her to think my info is so extreme and "out there" that she doesnt try it.

Thanks

### fitness@home — 2013-08-20T20:00:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93253>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=93251)
>
> My cousin contacted me last night....she has been suffering with hives for close to 2 years now and major leaky gut issues. She endured the loss of a child and another born with hydro-encephalitis. Multiple mis-carriages as well. She has seen stress at her young age that I can not imagine.

cantweight, the multiple miscarriages part threw a red flag up for me. Has she been tested for MTHFR through her doctors? It is a simple lab test. Worth asking for, IMHO.

Also, the 23andme test is only $99 and can give a much larger picture. Also well worth the money.

### cantweight — 2013-08-20T20:26:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93257>

I couldnt agree more! I tried to make some similar points when she was initially struggling but it is a very different way of thinking with most of my family.

They think I am a joke with all my silly diet and biotoxin illness nonsense. (I used to always say Lyme, but Martin has me thinking biotoxin illness sounds so much cooler..lol)

They all eat SAD. Their doctors know best. My stepmother is a nurse practitioner and she tries not to laugh in my face when I tell her things that we are doing.....that are working!!!

No one will listen to me....that is...until I am no longer fat. It doesnt matter that I can walk, and speak, and read again....they just see fat. Fat=failure to them.

If I mentioned these things I would over shoot and miss getting her on board...I need to ease in with things that make her feel better before doctors get at her with drugs and she gets worse.

I dont want to use the words dumb it down....she is very intelligent....but that is basically what I need to do here.

### cinnamon — 2013-08-20T22:14:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tips-to-help-someone-very-new-to-all-of-this.7961/post-93264>

cantweight,

It is the positive reinforcement of the continual successes that drives the journey for me. Maybe you just need to get your cousin started with something that seems not very risky but will be successful and then let her drive?

The first blog of Jack's that really resonated with me was [CT 7](http://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-7/). In that blog, Jack explains what goes on in the body during a 24 hour cycle. It's pretty easy for people to understand that we evolved on a planet that pulses on-off with light/dark cycles. It's not much of a stretch to think that might have an impact on some (okay probably ALL, but start by saying some) biological processes.

From there, I could easily see that it would cost me nothing to try going to bed at 10 and getting up as soon as it was light out. I could implement the BAB using bacon and eggs. So, I tried it and within a few days, I was already feeling better (not perfect, of course, but better than I had been).

Following my own experience, I started reading more and accumulated more and more changes - first paleo (but not really low carb) then CT, then epi-paleo, then high fat, low carb epi-paleo, then grounding, then non-fluoridated water, etc...

I'll send CT7 once she lets me know she is seeing improvement....maybe then the reasoning as to why will be received easier.

I hope she sees the kind of graduated success you found cinnamon...

The BAB is also antistress as with three kids on the go she nends to have strength right from the word go and not have to worry about eating and preparing for herself..It is a time saver really.

### cantweight — 2013-08-21T01:56:45+0300

# Thread: Copper & Allzheimer's  A Relationship. Do you have copper water pipes?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-allzheimers-a-relationship-do-you-have-copper-water-pipes.7962/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-08-20T21:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/copper-allzheimers-a-relationship-do-you-have-copper-water-pipes.7962/post-93262>

[http://www.latimes.com/science/sciencenow/la-sci-copper-alzheimers-disease-20130819,0,5977613.story](http://www.latimes.com/science/sciencenow/la-sci-copper-alzheimers-disease-20130819,0,5977613.story)

As I was reading this account of alzheimer's & copper, I wonder what role the field plays in opening up the BBB to copper.

"New research finds that copper in amounts readily found in our drinking water, the foods we eat and the vitamin supplements we take likely plays a key role in initiating and fueling the abnormal protein build-up and brain inflammation that are hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease.

"While the mineral is important to healthy nerve conduction, hormone secretion and the growth of bones and connective tissue, a team of researchers from the University of Rochester Medical Center suggested that too much of it may be a bad thing, and they set about to explore copper's dark side.

"What they found, said neuroscientist Rashid Deane, is "pretty scary": A steady diet of copper, even at entirely allowable levels, breaks down the barrier that keeps unwanted toxins from entering the brain, and that it fuels an increase in production of beta-amyloid but impedes the performance of proteins that clear the stuff from the brain.

"On top of that, Deane's team found that copper accumulation in the brain causes inflammation in brain tissues. At low levels and for short durations, that may be a good sign that brain tissues are responding to the danger of excess beta-amyloid proteins and are trying to expel them, Deane said. In time, however, neuro-inflammation can overwhelm the brain and begin to damage cells, he added.

"Copper is found in a wide range of the foods we eat, including red meat, shellfish, nuts and many fruits and vegetables, as well as in many vitamin supplements. It also leaches from copper pipes into the water we drink. While we take in copper from foods, it is most readily absorbed into the bloodstream in its "free" form, say researchers -- when it is suspended in water.

"The research, which lay out the case against a long-suspected culprit in Alzheimer's disease, is published Monday in the journal PNAS.

"Deane said that, in the absence of effective treatments for Alzheimer's disease, his team's findings suggest a way to prevent the memory-robbing disorder or slow it once it has taken hold. One drug candidate currently in Phase 2 trials -- an agent that binds with copper molecules and escorts them out of the body -- might well do that, said the study's lead author. But even now, Deane said, consumers could be checking their vitamin supplements for copper and researching whether their water filters are equipped to remove copper from their drinking supply.

"The key will be striking the right balance between too much and too little copper consumption," Deane said. "Right now, we cannot say what the right level will be. But diet may one day play an important role in regulating this process."

"Deane's team worked with mice and with human brain cells that play a key role in forming the blood-brain barrier to detect the mechanisms by which copper might start, drive or worsen Alzheimer's disease. They noted that in the elderly, the blood-brain barrier becomes "leaky," letting in larger toxic molecules circulating the blood. So too does the concentration of copper in the brain's small blood vessels increase with age.

"Starting in the mouse-equivalent of young adulthood, they fed mice a regular diet, but gave half of them water that contained levels of copper equal to one-tenth the maximum allowed by the Environmental Protection Agency. The other half were given double-distilled water with a very low copper content -- less than 2% of that given to the high-copper group. In a second experiment, older mice bred for their propensity to develop beta-amyloid plaques were fed the copper-tainted water or the low-copper water.

"After three months, the effects were dramatic: In the mice who took in high doses of copper, the scientists observed that copper accumulated in the small blood vessels of the brain. Compared to mice on low doses of copper, the high-copper mice showed four times fewer levels of a protein called LRP1, which transports beta-amyloid and other debris out of the brain. They also showed abnormally high levels of beta-amyloid production and of neuro-inflammation.

"As they aged, the brains of mice who were fed high doses of copper had beta-amyloid levels on par with those of mice who drank low-copper water but had been bred to develop beta-amyloid at high rates.

Copyright Â© 2013, Los Angeles Times

Cristi, copper is often elevated in folks with adrenal fatigue...it builds up and the body can't get rid of it all. Also has a lot of interplay with hormones; copper sort of acts in the body like estrogen, zinc (the other side of the see-saw) like PG4. So if you also have a E2 dominance, this is another reason...fascinating stuff. as you keep detoxing the copper should come down.

I think you are already doing a lot......eating protein is key so the Epi-paleo Rx = awesome. Vegetarians are often high in copper. It also ties in with mercury toxicity.....often we have hidden copper. There is a great book "Why am I always so tired" about copper toxicity...but the diet info isn't as good as what you know from here.....

# Thread: Extra-low-frequency magnetic fields alter cancer cells through metabolic restriction.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extra-low-frequency-magnetic-fields-alter-cancer-cells-through-metabolic-restriction.7966/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dextery — 2013-08-21T00:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extra-low-frequency-magnetic-fields-alter-cancer-cells-through-metabolic-restriction.7966/post-93284>

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23915261](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/23915261)

Have you ever wondered why cancer seems so random. Could it be the emfs and how they alter the structure of the water we drink?

Extra-low-frequency magnetic fields alter cancer cells through metabolic restriction.
Li Y, HÃ©roux P.
Source

InVitroPlus Laboratory, Department of Surgery, Royal Victoria Hospital , Montreal, QC , Canada and.
Abstract

Abstract Background: Biological effects of extra-low-frequency (ELF) magnetic fields (MFs) have lacked a credible mechanism of interaction between MFs and living material. Objectives: To examine the effect of ELF-MFs on cancer cells. Methods: Five cancer cell lines were exposed to ELF-MFs within the range of 0.025-5 ÂµT, and the cells were examined for karyotype changes after 6 d. Results: All cancer cells lines lost chromosomes from MF exposure, with a mostly flat dose-response. Constant MF exposures for three weeks allow a rising return to the baseline, unperturbed karyotypes. From this point, small MF increases or decreases are again capable of inducing karyotype contractions (KCs). Our data suggest that the KCs are caused by MF interference with mitochondria's adenosine triphosphate synthase (ATPS), compensated by the action of adenosine monophosphate-activated protein kinase (AMPK). The effects of MFs are similar to those of the ATPS inhibitor, oligomycin. They are amplified by metformin, an AMPK stimulator, and attenuated by resistin, an AMPK inhibitor. Over environmental MFs, KCs of various cancer cell lines show exceptionally wide and flat dose-responses, except for those of erythroleukemia cells, which display a progressive rise from 0.025 to 0.4 ÂµT. Conclusions: The biological effects of MFs are connected to an alteration in the structure of water that impedes the flux of protons in ATPS channels. These results may be environmentally important, in view of the central roles played in human physiology by ATPS and AMPK, particularly in their links to diabetes, cancer and longevity.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-21T04:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extra-low-frequency-magnetic-fields-alter-cancer-cells-through-metabolic-restriction.7966/post-93318>

# Thread: CT & Cycloset Effect Upon Growth Hormone Levels
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-cycloset-effect-upon-growth-hormone-levels.7968/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Trinity — 2013-08-21T03:14:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-cycloset-effect-upon-growth-hormone-levels.7968/post-93314>

Hi guys,

I am wondering about the effects of CT and cycloset upon growth hormone levels. I have been doing CT baths 4-5x per week for ~7 months and taking cycloset 2.4mg per day for ~4 months now. Although I started my CT protocol at a measly 70 degrees, after 7 months I am doing 30-45 minute daily ice baths in 42 degree water without any shivering during or after.

Although I do not have a baseline from the very beginning, over the past 4-5 months of cycloset + regular CT, my IGF-1 levels have increased by more than 100 points. In fact, I am now above the reference range at 373 ng/mL (range 53-331). From what I've researched, this is quite high for someone my age (34 years old). My endocrinologist said that he does not see levels that high very often in his patients and was quite interested in my CT protocol. Should I be concerned? Is this an expected side effect of CT? Cycloset is also classified as a mild growth hormone releasing agent, but 100+ points is not a "mild" increase so I am wondering if there could be some synergistic effect between the cycloset and CT protocols. Hopefully, elevated growth hormone is not a problem because the effects upon my damaged metabolism have been nothing less than remarkable!

I am morbidly obese and have been unable to loose weight for nearly 10 years despite heroic efforts at diet and exercise which did more to undermine my overall health than improve it... BUT over the past few months something really seems to be changing... I am noticing that I have become quite resistant to weight gain for the first time ever! In fact, I just bought pants 1 size smaller because my old pants were starting to look a little sloppy. I have only lost 4-5 pounds so I am guessing that there must be some muscle gain occurring in addition to fat loss. The truly amazing thing is that this has happened without any calorie restriction or increase in exercise! And on top of that I will admit that my diet is still a work in progress. I eat a lowish carb (~75-100g carbs per day), mostly paleo diet (some grass fed dairy), but no where near ketogenic or Epi-Paleo and my adherence to the Leptin Rx has been less than steller lately (started having mid morning coffee with cream again  ...BUT even in spite of a not perfectly optimal diet I seem to still be loosing fat and gaining muscle. And with that success, I am now finding myself more motivated to work on getting back on the Leptin Rx horse and adopting a more Epi-Paleo diet by increasing my seafood intake.

Trinity

### Trinity — 2013-08-22T01:10:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-cycloset-effect-upon-growth-hormone-levels.7968/post-93376>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=93316)
>
> as long as you are minding your light levels and you are eating ketogenic........you will do great......This post is CT 6 testimony right here. I had the exact same result.....102 point increase when I began to my 6 month test......but I was meticulous about the light and diet.......

Hi Dr K,

Thanks for clarifying that the increase in IGF-1 is an expected result of sustained CT. That is really interesting that you also saw a 100 point increase in IGF-1. I just finished reading CT-6 in its entirety and I greatly appreciate your efforts to make this information available. Being in a research oriented field (not medicine), I have spent a significant amount of time and energy reading up on the metabolic and obesity research in an effort to uncover a fix for my own problems, however, it was not until I came across your website that I started to see the "big picture".

I am in this CT game for the long haul. As a morbidly obese person, I fully understand the absolute necessity of CT and the need to induce fat cell apoptosis. Without permanently destroying a significant number of my fat cells, long term fat loss will be physiologically impossible to sustain. I know this from first had experience because it was a substantial weight loss earlier in my life that severely damaged my endocrine system. I kept the weight off for several years through sheer force of will combined with unhealthy dieting and aggressive exercise... But in the end my self discipline backfired on me and hormonal havoc ensued. Despite my best efforts, I gained all of the weight back and then some along with a host of new problems including adrenal dysfunction, low DHEA, massively elevated reverse t3, trashed progesterone, pre-diabetes, and a destroyed circadian rhythm. I have spent what were supposed to be the best and most productive years of my life trying to recover my health with little assistance and a great deal of bitterness towards the conventional medical establishment.

In addition to the massive increase in IGF-1 I mentioned, I have also seen other improvements from CT that support everything you discuss in CT-6:

1) My t3 levels have improved over the past few months \*without\* increasing my cytomel dose

2) I have managed to get off of Cortef after almost 2 years on it. My cortisol is still not optimal, but its getting better. Sleep still isn't perfect either but my chronic insomnia has noticeably improved in response to CT, rigorously minding the light cycles, and now a magnetico pad.

Conventional wisdom's admonishment to restrict calories and exercise myself to death is simply not part of my plan to conquer my obesity anymore... That road led me away from health rather than towards it... And as they say, stupidity is defined as doing the same thing over and over expecting different results

My new strategy is 3-fold:

1) Use CT to work towards long term fat cell apoptosis: I actually invested in a commercial grade water chiller that I have hooked up to a submersible pump sitting in my bath tub. It is on a timer and starts chilling my water about an hour and a half before I get home from work. So far it has worked really great because it has allowed me to control and very gradually reduce the temperature as I have acclimated. It will cool down to 37 degrees and that is where I'm heading over the next few months (currently at 42 degrees).

2) Reduce Inflammation and Improve Mitochondrial Function: Although CT helps with this, I am also supplementing with Tumeric (Meriva), poly-resveratrol, CoQ10, Magnesium, D-Ribose, and am working on improving my dietary omega-3 ratio. I also recently purchased a Magnetico core pad. I have become aware that morbid obesity is a highly inflammatory state and CT-6 really confirmed my need to reduce inflammation when it talked about the inflammatory factors blocking leptin from binding to the receptor.

3) Work on improving circadian rhythm: To this end, I make a point to consistently get light exposure first thing in the AM and wear my blue blockers after 7pm every night. I am also consistently in bed by 10PM and sleep in complete darkness thanks to heavy duty light blocking curtains and a sleep mask. On the weekends, I make an effort to spend a few hours outside in the sun during the morning or early afternoon.

After reading CT-6, it appears that my results might be even better if I adopt a ketogenic diet. I am currently eating paleo and plan to gradually move in the ketogenic direction as the fall and winter approach. I have taken the carb reduction a bit slow because past efforts to go fully ketogenic have really backfired in terms of the effect upon my mood and energy. I now understand that this is because I have not been able to burn fat very efficiently. With my broken metabolism I have no idea how long converting to an efficient fat burner might take. I am currently planning to use D-ribose to temporarily fill in the gap as I attempt to make the adjustment. It is interesting that I have still gotten some of the benefits from the cold pathway without being fully ketogenic. The benefits I've seen so far are definitely motivating me to keep moving more towards optimal

Trinity

CT 6 is really, really massive ... my eyes must have been glazed over the whole first time I read it. I suppose now that we know more we can appreciate it so much more. I now seem to find that the amount of information in there is overwhelming and I forgot to re read the comments yesterday - todays gift of knowledge ...Thanks Dan for reminding me!

Dan ...do you run movnat classes? I am obsessed with the idea that a lot of the young men here would benefit so much from doing a movnat with Erwan. Sort of strip away everything, clear their head and get down to basics and re focus....

I had been investigating all the requirements of making a DIY version - ie the type, strength and the positioning of magnets, plus the distance needed between it and your sleeping body to be sure to be exposed to the correct gauss! ### BJK77 — 2013-09-04T14:11:34+0300

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=94780)

However, that being said, DH's back has been bothering him all week (until this morning) since we put our pads in the kids' beds. I'm hoping getting the super sleep system with the 20 gauss will make a noticeable difference for us.

> [freesia said:](/goto/post?id=94790)

> Yes the field extends upwards, and 20 gauss (or 10 - whatever you purchase) would likely be taking into account the thickness of an average mattress.

Yes, you are correct. Magnetico's website says the pads measure 10 or 20 gauss at the surface of an 8" mattress. Anything thicker and you'll lose some of the pull. I think I read before you lose 1 gauss per inch and my mattress is 12" thick, so I was probably only getting a measurement of about 6 gauss.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetico-mattress-15-offer-for-members-still-active.7970/post-94886>

> [marcoc said:](/goto/post?id=94847)
>
> My bed is aligned head side toward north and feet south. I confirmend this with a compas. When i place the pad under the mattress magnetic field reverses 180 degrees. Is this normal ?

If you use a ball/globe compass, you should see that North doesn't just reverse, it points down toward the bed, whichever side of the bed you are at. If you stand on the bed, and hold the compass above your head, it will point straight down.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-dim-make-you-dim.7974/post-93370>

I added DIM to my arsenal for the past week and I have noticed a very foggy feeling. I keep leaving the stove and oven on....I never do this! I also feel kind of disconnected and my libido is crashing and burning. I dont feel depressed or sad, just flat.

Has anyone had similar issues? Do you know if I should discontinue?

I never even got my hormone panel results....my doc closed up shop and left the building (yeah, thats the kind of luck I have..lol) Supposedly they will re-open in a new location and I can get them next week. I just started the DIM under the assumption it would help.

# Thread: Best DHA source?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### psionic — 2013-08-22T15:30:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/post-93404>

Hi! I am new here and sorry if something related has been already posted.

I dont have to point out that Dr. Kruse stresses DHA as a most important factor for brain development and shellfish and crustaceans as a main and best source.

I want to ask if one can use other sources of omega-3, I mean (not intoxicated) fish oil caps, hemp seed (omega6mega3 is 3:1) or flax seed with almost identical results as seafood? The answer of this question is targeted mainly for people located in inlands where the access to shellfish and crustaceans is limited.

Thanks for suggestions

### Lahelada — 2013-08-22T15:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/post-93405>

Welcome . Fish oil is generally not recommended as it is difficult to source it fresh enough.The same goes for seeds. While hemp has a good O6 O3 relation you will need to make sure as well that the seeds have not been stored for a long time. The way to tell if seeds have gone to seed is if you grind them they release an odour of fish.
If you decide on using seeds you might be better of using flaxseed with a ratio of 4.5 to 1 as it is used in many diets so the turnover of it must be higher.

I am not a fish lover so I can't do strong tasting fish. While I do eat fresh fish the most effective fish for inlanders seems to be mackerel as it has a ratio of 11:1.
Have a look here.It is wikipedia but the values seem to be correct judging from two checks I made .

Furthermore before you supplement I would read this if you have not already done so.

[http://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/](http://www.jackkruse.com/is-fish-oil-good-or-bad/)

### cjhf — 2013-08-22T18:12:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-dha-source.7975/post-93408>

If this will help anyone else I'll share info from my misguided path.
Question everything I am learning.

After many years of taking pharm. grade fish oil, having joined MLM firm to market product, attending conferences promoting its use, reading numerous references to the benefits I have
discontinued my dogma and stopped taking it.

Brain Gut 5 was recommended to me but I have many years invested in fish oil and still didn't get it.

More evidence with research articles and recordings by an unbiased engineer have convinced me to change my ways.
[http://www.brianpeskin.com/](http://www.brianpeskin.com/) In addition to the fish oil attack on his site I found other logical info useful and it has broadened my perspective.

I have not taken a different DHA supplement product at this point.
So far it is sardines, oysters, herring and salmon.

I've heard if you try eatting something 10 times you become tolerant. I am working up to some other recommended DHA sources.

I get canned sardines locally or online. There are many different spice combinations and added vegs to make them taste quite good.

I hope this will keep someone else from making the same mistake as me. Continuing to learn...the body is very adaptive and forgiving.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

> Will Thornhill said:
>
> Quantum theory must look for a cause. We cannot have a theory which relies on probabilistic ideas.
> God does not play dice with universe.
> Probability is not involved. There must be a direct cause and effect, which is related to the electrical structure of matter.

I have to admit that I am a _standard Quantum Mechanics_ skeptic. As much as it is used to explain experimental phenomena once they have being produced (just like in economy), I am only aware of the Casimir effect as the only one being **predicted**, and it seems that [there is a classical (including Einstein) explanation too](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Casimir_effect#Relativistic_van_der_Waals_force). I would like that a theory like [that proposed by Dr. Randell L. Mills](http://www.blacklightpower.com/theory-2/theory/) be correct instead. Actually it seems that he predicted the accelerated expansion of the Universe before it being measured. I certainly like the beauty of his equations getting rid of something like 20 different constants needed by _standard Quantum Mechanics_: take a look at Page 29 of [this presentation](http://www.blacklightpower.com/wp-content/uploads/theory/TheoryPresentationPt3.pdf), for example. I don't know if his neutrinos (they are special photons in his theory) may explain the variation in rate of decay at hand though.

I thought all of you would really like to see that science is laying foundations for the April 2016 webinar and some of the things in this thread. If solar output via neutrinos can alter radioactive decay rates what else might it affect on Earth? Leaves? Nocturnal clock mechanism? Might it also connect to the mitochondrial matrix? April 2016 webinar made many predictions about what I expect and this paper makes me smile.

[http://news.stanford.edu/news/2010/august/sun-082310.html](http://news.stanford.edu/news/2010/august/sun-082310.html)

During daylight hours the sun builds a massive battery in cell water and water stores that potential energy for a long period of time as Gerald Pollack has showed. As that charge is built up during day this allows to a decoherence as the cell physical properties allows for decoherence with the environment under the power of the suns waves. They to understand is that in warm environment coherence can be maintained in transient fashion at night. During the absence of sun at night, while using the massive charges stored in cell water called the exclusion zone, cells become able to transiently able to become quantum coherent. This allows for regeneration and the creation of the living state. This implies that sleep was likely the original state that life began and living things evolved wakefulness during daylight hours. How would nocturnal animals have powered their metabolisms and mitochondria? Could it be the varying release of solar neutrino's at night? We know nocturnal animals reversed the diurnal process that uses sunrise and sunset as the major oscillator. Could they also use other formation of cosmic radiation from the Van Allen belts at night? John Ott thought about this possibility. We know IR light is present day and night to affect ATP creation and help yoke their circadian systems with other frequencies of light. What about the other frequencies of light found in the spectrum of light we do not observe. Could they be a source of energy that nocturnal animals use? No matter how improbable this appears to physicists nature laws do not forbid quantum coherence by any know law or math proof, therefore, given enough time nature will find a way. We've got to follow the data. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/27499509](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpubmed%2F27499509&h=ATN2gfTAfnVbrWlXkQ1VUgpY17J6GqajpqwKE5nS3egm8P5YBgGfP_w2AvBOn7Dm2pIVCp-GX8E7P3G_j9725Jqxa_h2A-0APUAPH1OhDcZGqMcjjPzIV330-WkPmkq3fO5B1pE857cP9Rgk439apreDpZlUtMA)

Proteins are hydrated biomolecules that are designed to absorb specific solar radiation frequencies that drive cell signaling. Once absorbed by the protein (light antenna) the light can be transformed to other sources of energy. The color of the transformed light tells us there has been a shift in light frequency. These frequencies must not be reflective, but must be absorbed within the cells and tissues to work. Once absorbed by the atoms in light the light is changed in its frequency and in its color. That light is then re-emitted by cells for more down steam effects and signaling (Fermat's law). When the incident light is during daytime the incident rays are between 250-780 nm. That light re-emitted from the visible spectrum is in the extreme low UV range. Proteins have been selected for and built by nature to pay attention to these specific frequencies of light in the visible spectrum during day time. This is why certain amino acids are coded for in DNA and why all proteins are made from these amino acids by life (20). The resulting color a cell re-emits is the photonic signal at the detector proteins in adjacent cells and structures. Light waves can also be turned into electricity, solitons, phonons, and many other wave forms. Color determine frequency in life. But what about nocturnal life that does not see full spectrum solar light? Is there another form of energy from the sun we do not see that might control their growth and metabolism pathways in ways we do not expect? What if the light from the sun has no color we can see? Could something other than a protein absorb it? Could it be atoms or particles we cannot see in mitochondrial matrix? Life really is color coded, and it the code likely extends way past colors we can see.......................

Good question, hopefully I'll find the direct answer to this, I believe I saw it in the comments of the CT series I'm currently digging through, but as far as I know this has to do entirely with your tissue fatty acids - you need to have slightly more o6 than o3 and you won't lay down fat around the abdomens. This fat is beneficial to species living in the oceans because they need the extra 'warmth' as well as fluidity (in their cells) to operate under the immense pressure and lack of heat. We don't necessarily need that. So yes, CT will lead to that IF your tissue fatty acid ratios are off. This is why Dr. Kruse doesn't necessarily demonize o6 fats.

### cinnamon — 2013-08-23T21:35:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fat-around-the-mid-section-from-ct.7978/post-93537>

I started doing CT last summer. I would stand in a cold shower hoping my skin would turn pink. I did this every 2 or 3 days, probably for at most about 10 minutes each time. I found that if I started with either the top of my head or my face, I could push the water colder and colder. I would keep going, letting the water hit my face until it was cold enough that it felt like a blizzard wind - numbing and tingling and taking my breath away. I would focus the water on my belly, because I had developed a pot belly in the previous couple of years that simply wouldn't go away. I would also focus on my bottom, hoping it might help the sagging cellulite that plagued me there.

I knew that this was no where near as extreme as those on the forum who reported lying for 30 to 60 minutes at a time in a tub with ice floating in the water. I figured it would be a good first step, and not too hard to implement. I was stunned and pleased to see that my pot belly was shrinking and my cellulite was disappearing. I was getting muscles in my arms and legs while doing no excercise except walking. My skin looked great!

I am sure that eating good fats (allowing absorption of fat-soluble nutrients that my body needed) increasing protein, and paying attention to circadian rhythm all helped with the improvement. But I am a firm believer that any CT is good!

After the start of the new year, when I decided to increase my seafood intake (which for many years had been zero). I noticed that CT was easier and I could let the water get quite cold (straight from the cold tap is 42 to 44 degrees F). I also noticed that my skin was turning pink while CTing and then retaining a wonderful rosy hue all the time. This summer I have been CTing almost everyday. Still almost always in the shower, but for probably 20 minutes or so at a time.

I should also mention that I think grounding has an impact on body comp. It was still deep winter here when Jack started blogging about grounding, so I started spending 30 minutes or so each day barefoot on the concrete floor in my basement. Literally within 2 days, I could sense a change in the shape of my butt! It felt somehow rounder? firmer? not sure how to describe it. When I do a lot of grounding I notice more muscle.

I am attaching a picture I took this morning. Unfortunately, I don't have a "before" shot. You'll just have to trust me that I would not have been wearing a bikini! LOL
