### caroline — 2013-08-30T01:03:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94251>

Hang in there Annie! .... In a year's time you won't believe the changes in every conceivable part of your body. It won't happen over nite .... but it will absolutely and definitely happen

Have you read the iodine thread? better yet - get this book .... The Iodine Crisis - by Lynne Farrow .....available at Amazon

Have you read the post yesterday in the journal section by ...Mr. Pinkies ....she is awe inspiring...

I can't imagine living with a husband who is so unsupportive ..... but it is what it is....you must take care of you!

It is your life, your journey and you won't get a do over. You need to be really strong and 100% committed and engaged.

Show him and others what is possible .... come here for support .... we are all on this journey of a lifetime together.

Read other's journals for inspiration ....so many here are doing outstanding things for themselves. Start a journal ...it is really helpful to keep on top of things and we will help.

Have you listened to Jack's Podcasts yet?

I can't suggest strongly enough to become a silver or gold member - if you can manage it. I think it ups the commitment level. The webinars and live Q&A's that follow have so much awesome information and you can ask questions. I think there is a special running right now.

Are you reading Jack's blogs? especially the ones that pertain to you to start with. Sorry if I have asked you this before - I may be losing my mind!

### AnnieW — 2013-08-30T05:33:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94308>

Nope you're not losing your mind. I'm working on the brain gut series right now and getting started on EMF. I listened to a podcast he linked to the other day, it was really good.

I'm working through the iodine thread over on MDA and I've finished the one here. I've also been in touch with Grizz and he has helped me with what I need to do in terms of supplementing.

I don't expect my husband to be 100% supportive because his head is full of CW and in his mind "eat less, exercise more" is what I need to do. Sometimes it's like talking to a brick wall. That's ok though because I intend to be an example that this stuff works.

I will definitely check out the journals and I've been wanting to get a membership since I found out about this site.

I just want to say thanks for your support and for cheering me on. I really love when you pop in with your words of encouragement!

### caroline — 2013-08-30T06:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94309>

You have got great spitit Annie! You are absolutely right about your DH. ....his brain isn't optimal ...but he will see a huge change in you ..so you are helping him too.

Noone can believe if you eat fat and don't exercise you will lose and your body comp will change hugely ....I can't believe
How many sizes I have gone down. I can't afford to keep up with this ever changing body of mine. I now want to wear pretty dresses! At least you have an advantage - he will see what you are eating. My friends don't believe me when I tell them that I eat high fat, medium protein and low carb!

Please take measurements and do you have a picture? Honestly you will be blown away at the changes in a years time.

I had my first consult with Dr. k. A year ago today. I remember Vividly that I had gone up 2 K. But I was down 2 sizes and I hadn't noticed. My clothes were hanging off me and didn't realize until a young woman in a shop asked me why I bought my clothes so big!!

Please start a journal today and then we can chat. - everyone is very supportive .... Just start simply. When I started I was only going to keep track of CT etc. but it has turned into so much more - I don't know if I ever kept track of CT.

### prAna303 — 2013-08-31T09:36:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94449>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=94432)
>
> We all arrived here for different reasons ..... and look what we wound up with! Losing the weight etc. etc. is so secondary to everything else we are achieving. I hope one day we can make Dr. K. proud .... it must be so hard dragging us all kicking and screaming to a new reality ....which is really an old reality .... the laws of nature.

Yes, we all have different stories. I joined this place because it matched my own journey of optimal, not in the way of being sick or ill, just because many things Jack tells us has been natural to me my whole life. I have always been a outsider, weirdo and on my own trail. Obsessed i have been called but never ever gave up on my own inner voice, the one that i was born with. Looking thru so many places many of them touches things just a little bit, like making ripples on a lake but this place i feel that change is happening in a way that i feel is right. Perhaps this is the reason why so many come and go here? It is scary when your bubble is out in the open and you get advices that tears down every little thing you thought was right. I do not take it personal because i lived like this my whole life, i know it can be really tough to stand alone but i love it, times with others are around the corner the whole time. Time after time i stand alone but i KNOW soon company is here again, it is in my blueprint to find them. This is what i mean with resonation, it is not how a girl look, it is the complete human that walks past me that crashes in to me. I can see a model looking girl that everyone around me is drooling about and feel nothing, just because the shell and brains is out of sync. Take a beautiful mind and match it with a body of purpose, i see a reason to live, the body is the tool to do your thoughts, I AM IN LOVE.

This is the reason why my prefered taste in company is the ones that DO it and not ACTING it. In the kind of tribes i live with a common word is "posers", never trust the guy with shining gears...

So bottom line: For me looks tells a story, please never ever remove scars and other marks. If you get some of them, tell the reason why, it says that you LIVED. The only thing i feel matters is the sync between mind and body. Live with a purpose and you shine.

### caroline — 2013-08-31T10:08:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-feel-about-your-body.8018/post-94452>

Take a beautiful mind and match it with a body of purpose...... that is what I am feeling more and more and could not articulate it until recently. I get so bored when I am around people with no purpose.

I love when someone has a passion for something, anything --- then they have me at "hello"

Passion vibrates thru your whole body ..... I think you can see someone's soul ....it is beyond exciting...

It changes everything - it lights up the space you are in .... not a word needs to be spoken.

My DH was a very quiet man ...but he was magic.......

# Thread: Psychedelic Drugs Linked to Lower Risk of Mental Illness?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2013-08-25T12:52:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-93685>

An epidemiological study questions old dogma:

[http://www.healthline.com/health-news/mental-psychedelics-not-linked-to-mental-health-problems-082213](http://www.healthline.com/health-news/mental-psychedelics-not-linked-to-mental-health-problems-082213)

> Examining data on more than 130,000 Americans in the National Survey on Drug Use and Health, researchers from the Norwegian University of Science and Technology's Department of Neuroscience found that the use of psychedelic drugs not only doesnâ€™t contribute to mental illness, but also may actually have a protective effect against it.
>
> They found that people who used psilocybin or mescaline throughout their lives, as well as people who used LSD in the past year, had lower rates of serious psychological distress, outpatient mental health treatment, and prescriptions for psychiatric medications.
>
> â€œOther studies have found no evidence of health or social problems among people who had used psychedelics hundreds of times in legally protected religious ceremonies,â€￾ clinical psychologist PÃ¥l-Ã˜rjan Johansen said.

### Antonis — 2018-05-12T21:18:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241087>

I want to know about this. I heard people saying that those drugs helped people when they tried them with a clear mind. They were talking about "the 3rd eye" and helping with stress.

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T13:58:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241154>

One of the functional med Doctors on the Quantum Facebook Group insists that he has seen heavy Psilocybin usage cause damage to people. I would approach with caution - even including the microdosing. MAPS has plenty of research available and yet we are to be convinced. I would do my homework on this.

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T15:39:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241164>

There is a shitload pro and anti psychedelics available - I have listened to many a podcast from experts from the pro-research groups etc. Quantum Biology #11 touches on reality iro quantum physics and LSD. I don't you will get Dr Kruse to comment beyond QED on this one. Perhaps spend some time keywording it and Jack Kruse on Google.

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T16:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241166>

I can understand why it may help for dysthymia and creativity. I have no experience with microdosing multiple times. As a member of various Facebook Forums around Psychedelics there often are people who report major problems as a result of heavy use of LSD, mushrooms, Peyote etc. These forums are worth exploring if you can get past the rubbish:like what movies and music to listen to whilst tripping etc. Reports on Erowid can be helpful. So too, their research etc.

### Antonis — 2018-05-14T16:35:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241169>

> [Jason Coates said:](/goto/post?id=241166)
>
> I can understand why it may help for dysthymia and creativity. I have no experience with microdosing multiple times. As a member of various Facebook Forums around Psychedelics there often are people who report major problems as a result of heavy use of LSD, mushrooms, Peyote etc. These forums are worth exploring if you can get past the rubbish:like what movies and music to listen to whilst tripping etc. Reports on Erowid can be helpful. So too, their research etc.

I understand mate. I was just curious if somebody did already a research on these drugs and knows. I don't trust online bullshit. I really have an issue with my stress but I would never go to drungs than be in the sun and change my environment

### Jason Coates — 2018-05-14T17:18:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241171>

By "research" do you mean personal experience? There are plenty of people here who have.
There was a TV documentary I saw where a man was taking epic doses of mushrooms for his migraines. He hated the trips, but it was the only thing that ever helped him. Consider that if you are going to level 4 or 5 once a month what it can do:
**Level 4**
Strong hallucinations, i.e. objects morphing into other objects. Destruction or multiple splitting of the ego. (Things start talking to ou, or you find that you are feeling contradictory things simultaneously). Some loss of reality. Time becomes meaningless. Out of body experiences and e.s.p. type phenomena. Blending of the senses.
**Level 5**
Total loss of visual connection with reality. The senses cease to function in the normal way. Total loss of ego. Merging with space, other objects, or the universe. The loss of reality becomes so severe that it defies explanation. The earlier levels are relatively easy to explain in terms of measurable changes in perception and thought patterns. This level is different in that the actual universe within which things are normally perceived, ceases to exist! Satori enlightenment (and other such labels). [https://www.shroomery.org/9067/What-do-the-different-trip-levels-Lvl-1-5-mean](https://www.shroomery.org/9067/What-do-the-different-trip-levels-Lvl-1-5-mean)

Most people don't go past level 3 and then only do it a few times in their lives. If they hit a 4 or a 5 it usually scares them off it. Microdosing is a relatively new phenomenon.

### Antonis — 2018-05-14T17:25:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/psychedelic-drugs-linked-to-lower-risk-of-mental-illness.8021/post-241172>

# Thread: Trying the coldest CT to date
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2013-08-25T17:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93704>

It is warm here and going to get HOT today so I am going to try my coldest CT to date. I have been making ice chunks for the past week by filling up ice cream buckets about half full (too full and the buckets break) and I have two milk jugs frozen too. I am gong to try for an hour but at least 45 minutes. I will probably do another warmer shorter one tonight like I did last night. If I am too hot going at bed time, I just don't sleep.

Wish me luck.

### nicld — 2013-08-25T19:33:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93714>

Thanks I managed to stay in for an hour and when I got done most of my ice was melted and the skin was just about 50Â°. The skin was pretty pink except where the tummy roll and the low tatas covered the flesh. I stayed indoors and did the dishes to try and keep cold for a while. Now I outside in the 90Â° heat rewarming for a while.

Definitely will try to do this in the mornings on the weekends when I have plenty of time to rewarm. Just don't think I am adapted enough to do it at night and rewarm enough to get to sleep.

### Optimalbound — 2013-08-25T21:59:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93732>

A full hour is very impressive. Well done!

### nicld — 2013-08-25T22:24:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93737>

Its not bad when I am in it, just the rewarming in brutal right now. I know it may not be optimal but I watch Super skinny versus Super fat on my kindle. Makes the time just fly. The lower legs are still tingling so I am hoping it did something.

### freesia — 2013-08-26T00:28:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93755>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=93737)
>
> Its not bad when I am in it, just the rewarming in brutal right now. I know it may not be optimal but I watch Super skinny versus Super fat on my kindle. Makes the time just fly. The lower legs are still tingling so I am hoping it did something.

Re-warming will become way less brutal. (Brutal is the perfect description by the way. LOL.)
Enjoy soaking up some sun afterwards while you still have it, I don't think it hinders progress.
You'll be sailing through CT **and** warm-up by the time Winter comes.
(You are doing so well!!)

### nicld — 2013-08-26T01:36:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/trying-the-coldest-ct-to-date.8027/post-93764>

Thanks Freesia, I hope it gets better by winter, cause in the colder, longer CT's is brutal. I don't think I could have started this in the winter without having a heating blanket on the bed.

I will try to do a couple of colder ones this week since I can step outside when I am done and rewarm since the temps are supposed to be in the 90's and humid all week. I HATE the heat.

# Thread: Cashews?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-25T19:25:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-93713>

I love raw cashews... I usually eat a few raw almonds and raw cashews as a night snack (I know snacks aren't Epi-Paleo but I need to gain 20lbs so they are for me).... anyway... are raw cashews OK?

### Jonathin — 2013-08-28T06:49:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94016>

There are a number of iffy things about cashews. Seeds of all sorts have problems. Coconut manna or coconut based bark might be a better source of calories. Raw grassfed Cream is an option if milk is not a problem.

Food you love can get you into trouble.

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-08-28T16:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94037>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=93713)
>
> I love raw cashews... I usually eat a few raw almonds and raw cashews as a night snack (I know snacks aren't Epi-Paleo but I need to gain 20lbs so they are for me).... anyway... are raw cashews OK?

Google "cashews toxins" for some interesting reading...

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T16:35:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94042>

Yeah, I was so sad when Jack said "no" to cashews.

### cantweight — 2013-08-28T17:26:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94049>

We stopped eating raw cashews after reading that the shells contain the same poison as poison ivy. That is why they are never shelled. Roasting is believed to kill the urushiol. But roasting oxidizes the fats. So basically there is no good way to eat cashews...bummer.

How about brazil nuts?

### Hope — 2013-08-28T23:50:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94107>

Have you tried hazelnuts? Super expensive, but boy, amazing. Mixed with a little raw organic cocoa = my only treat. Super yummy.

### Grantmeliberty — 2013-09-02T09:27:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94666>

The concern about eating "raw" cashews was something I encountered about 34 years ago, but didn't come to a conclusion until a few years ago. The "raw" cashews from the health food store are already heated enough to deactivate the toxins. Actually roasting them is unnecessary.

### Albert83BCN — 2013-09-02T10:15:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cashews.8029/post-94667>

Every single food has some issues if you look deep into it, just saying...

Hi,

In about 1 hour after a heavy workout on the gym I get sleepy and have to take a nap (about 1 hour long). And by 'sleepy' I do not mean eghausted, dizzy or having brain fog. I feel good and relaxed. The question is: why is it so? Is it because I've started doing workout about 1-2 p.m. as Jack suggests? (before I used to do sports in the evening). Anybody feels the same?

# Thread: Fixing an ectopic heartbeat?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-an-ectopic-heartbeat.8047/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arthurian Legend — 2013-08-26T22:26:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-an-ectopic-heartbeat.8047/post-93835>

I've been on a roller coaster ride of discovery for the past 18 months, following on from a decision that when I hit 40, I would get to the root of some nagging health problems, and even change career from a desk bound graphic designer to 'something' in the health and fitness industry.

The biggest issue was the worry of heart palpitations when performing HIIT training, mainly sprint intervals (tabatas) on a treadmill. After getting checked out (ECGs, echocardiograms, stress tests, HRV monitoring etc) the consultant determined that my heart was in good condition, but that I should not train to such intensity! This was a relief in some respects, but frustrating at the same time! I also realised that I was hyperventilating, and research into Butekyo breathing sorted that out. It also improved the palpitations (ectopic beats) when exercising, but didn't eliminate them. Several months later I discovered Jack Kruse and the importance of EMFs. It occurred to me that the electrical signals of my heart might be affected by exposure to unnatural EMFs, so I purchased some detectors and went hunting. No surprise that my treadmill emits a shocking (pun unintended!) amount of volts, and could have contributed. Then I took my Trifield 100XE to work and tested where I sat. I was horrified. The magnetic needle was off the scale right at my desk, where I had been sitting for almost 2 years - a few months before noticing the heart issues. I had been immersed in a 20ft EMF bubble that dropped off considerably on the other side of the office. Turns out that there's a ton of wiring and a generator in a room underneath, that feeds machines in the factory. I soon moved desk, much to the bewilderment of my colleagues.

I've now been out of that field for about 6 weeks, with the belief that my heart will rewire itself and beat properly when pushed close to MHR. So far, so good! I've been gradually ramping up my intensity (I do the HIIT training once a week) and the last one was on Friday. I won't wear a heart rate monitor again (wireless can't be good that close to the heart!) but I'm checking my pulse the old school way! No skipped beats. I'm so excited at the prospect of fixing this, **but could this be a coincidence?** How long could it take?

I've been eating a standard paleo diet for about 18 months, and I'm currently in the progress of upgrading to epi-Paleo after dodging scary shellfish all my life. I ground whenever I can, and am currently weighing up the financial pros and cons of getting a Magnetico pad vs the Earthpulse. I've also just started the face dunking stage of the CT protocol.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-26T23:53:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-an-ectopic-heartbeat.8047/post-93844>

Hi Sir Arthur, welcome!! Wow, I guess that Trifield paid for itself! Glad you were able to mitigate that problem at work.
You need to make sure you are drinking unfluoridated water (always), and you might want to take some D-Ribose for your heart. Do you get sunshine? How's your sleep?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-27T04:01:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-an-ectopic-heartbeat.8047/post-93866>

The learning process is a never-ending pursuit of knowledge to be used to provide the most appropriate, most beneficial, and safest care for our patients. Knowledge is not static. It evolves and gradually changes past practices. Ultimately new knowledge comes from existing knowledge mingled with uncertainty, curiosity, discovery, assessment of evidence and experience, both personal and vicarious.
Standardization, or the use of set protocols, can in many ways diminish the desire to search for new knowledge thus solidifying old habits and procedures at the expense of scientific advancement.

### Arthurian Legend — 2013-08-27T17:33:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-an-ectopic-heartbeat.8047/post-93909>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=93844)
>
> Hi Sir Arthur, welcome!! Wow, I guess that Trifield paid for itself! Glad you were able to mitigate that problem at work.
> You need to make sure you are drinking unfluoridated water (always), and you might want to take some D-Ribose for your heart. Do you get sunshine? How's your sleep?

Hi Nonchalant! First time I've joined a forum in several years, so unfortunately I overlooked the meet and greet threads!

I've not looked into D-Ribose yet (I'm about to try out Ubiquinol) but I'll add it to my epic to-do list thanks. I live in the middle of the UK, so sunshine is at a premium even at this time of year. I get exposure whenever I can though, and my sunburn tolerance has gone from appalling (I once ended up in hospital in Thailand!) to acceptable - which I definitely put down to my massively improved Omega fats ratio. Vit D supplementation is resorted to when I can't get my own biofactory to produce it.

Sleep is great, but there's always room for improvement. It's a lot better since I upgraded my blueblocking glasses to a better brand. I can now wake up like clockwork depending on what time of the evening I put them on. I've lent some out to family and friends and they're all giving good feedback, apart from complaining that the TV is just too orange...

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=93866)
>
> The learning process is a never-ending pursuit of knowledge to be used to provide the most appropriate, most beneficial, and safest care for our patients. Knowledge is not static. It evolves and gradually changes past practices. Ultimately new knowledge comes from existing knowledge mingled with uncertainty, curiosity, discovery, assessment of evidence and experience, both personal and vicarious.
> Standardization, or the use of set protocols, can in many ways diminish the desire to search for new knowledge thus solidifying old habits and procedures at the expense of scientific advancement.

Thank you for the advice Dr Kruse - and not just from the quote above. All this is quite literally changing my life, and it's hard to contain my excitement to pass on the message. I'm intending to go back to the doctor's to request another ECG early next year to see if the heart block has fizzled out. If so, I would like to contact the specialist to offer my n=1 hypothesis, and point him to articles on this website.

### nonchalant — 2013-08-27T19:17:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fixing-an-ectopic-heartbeat.8047/post-93922>

Actually, I have been having second thoughts on the D-ribose for you. It's good for anyone with a mitochondria, but since your heart was experiencing an electrical issue and not a structural/functional issue, your heart in particular may not be in critical need for d-ribose.

The blue blockers are wonderful, aren't they.
So glad you started speaking up on the forum.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Please help with my new lab results
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-new-lab-results.8048/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Flora — 2013-08-26T22:28:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-with-my-new-lab-results.8048/post-93836>

Have just received my lab results and am pretty worried - particularly by the thyroid tests.

The antibody tests have come back high (but I don't know how high as I haven't yet managed to find out what constitutes high) and I guess this means a Hashimotos diagnosis, despite normal TSH and free T4 and T3? (Reverse T3 has been done but will take a month for result, as it has to be done in Paris!)
The results came back directly to me and I haven't yet heard from my doctor - so no feedback yet.
Anyone know if there is any other reason for high antibodies?

A bit of background - I'm 58 and have followed JK for the past year, adopting epi-paleo (I do eat raw milk - mostly sheep or goats) and gradually incorporating as much as possible as I've followed the blog posts.
I did low-carb 6 months before that and lost 20 lbs but then weightloss stopped and fatigue worsened - not terrible all the time but definitely not right and lots of unexplained aches and pains too and sleep went downhill. Also put on 10 lbs very easily during spring when excessively stressed. But no other thyroid malfunction symptoms such as hairloss, cold, constipation - in fact hair is thick and virtually no grey and I'm usually hot rather than cold (especially at night).
Sleep has improved this summer since taking 50mg DHEA - also take Mg malate, niacin, occasional kelp if I've not eaten fish for a couple of days.
I think we are in a low EMF environment (but Tri-meter is on its way for testing) and I aim to do some grounding each day and observe light cycles (used blueblockers, candles and firelight last winter)

The thing that worries me most is that my Mum died of thyroid cancer 20 years ago - so I've had thyroid checked before but this is the first time antibodies have been done.
I live in very rural France (but am English), which adds to difficulties with doctor!

I last had some labs 9 months ago but (different) doctor wouldn't to Thyroid antibodies then - comparison levels of those available included below.

I maybe misguided - or not have enough info - but the rest of my labs seem to be ok - Vit D, cholesterol panel, DHEA, HS CRP, which are the basic ones Jack recommends for the Optimal reset.

I think my BUN/creatinine ratio may be shot, though - as it appears to be 33 ??? (and was at the last test too - but doc didn't comment. I only picked this up from reading this forum recently). Maybe I haven't worked it out correctly (lab doesn't do it) but it's rather worrying, isn't it?

Anyway - I'd be really grateful for any help / comments anyone can give.
I'm afraid the results are in French, but it's fairly easy to work out what's what!
Many thanks - Flora

We will be moving the entire site to a new server in the next 24-48 hours in efforts to improve overall speed and accessibility. This process will hopefully only take a few hours. # Thread: Newbie here, suffering for years, requesting help
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-suffering-for-years-requesting-help.8052/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### codered5 — 2013-08-27T02:49:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-suffering-for-years-requesting-help.8052/post-93859>

Hi all, I am a 27 year old male who has been suffering with some health issues for appx 7 years. In response to my issues, I went paleo beginning Feb 2012, have had good success with it, have lost 25 lbs, improved sleep, etc. But I haven't been 100% strict and haven't been able to clean up my health issues. I have been doing a lot of reading on Dr. Kruse's site and am thinking I may dive into the leptin reset here in the next week (although I am actually in pretty good shape, probably about 10 lbs overweight). Basically I am just desperately searching for answers - for the first 4 years of my issues, I was basically told that I was a hypochondriac by many doctors and refused their band-aid approaches to treating me simply because I prefer to stay away from drugs...I will also add that in the last year or so I have made very good progress - I still have all of the issues and symptoms, but the "volume" has been turned down on my discomfort if that makes sense. I should also add that this all started for me in the middle of my college baseball career, where I was training a lot and heavily using one of those preworkout drink mixes with a lot of caffeine.

Here are the symptoms I have been constantly suffering from -
\- I usually am very conscious of my heartbeat, often feels like my body is running on pure adrenaline. I used to often wake up in the morning and it felt like I was in the middle of a marathon, heart pounding, tons of unnatural feeling energy, I would just shoot out of bed like a cannon (this has calmed A TON).
\- The adrenaline like feeling gives me tightness across my face and neck, I would often suffer from hot flashes and my face often feels like I am embarrassed, an extremely uncomfortable feeling. This drives me more crazy than anything else I experience.
\- Caffeine makes me feel absolutely terrible and I mean terrible - I am so sensitive it kind of worries me (which is why I have quit caffeine)
\- Sometimes have hangovers even if I only have 1-2 alcoholic drinks - if I make sure to eat something after drinking it doesn't appear to bother me so much
\- Any type of stress put on my system sent me into a nervous worry
\- My eyes are almost always dry and semi-red, had an eye exam just a couple of months ago, my eyes are structurally fine and healthy
\- Where I feel this the most unfortunately is sexually - my libido SUCKS and even if it is there, often times I am so uncomfortable because of my heartbeat/adrenaline feeling that I can't enjoy it - I usually am able to perform, but it feels more like I just get through it. I haven't truly enjoyed sex in 7 years. Some of the time the discomfort causes me to lose erections. The few times when I don't feel the adrenaline feeling in my system, the first thing that comes back is my libido, followed by my energy. It's literally like two different people within me.
\- Every once in a while I will get very anxious doing something that I was perfectly comfortable doing the day before - this could be work related, sexual, etc. Like one day I will be confident in a social setting, and the next day it could be the exact same social setting and then just randomly I am extremely anxious - My mind doesn't feel too anxious, just my body.

These are the major things, probably some more small ones somewhere. I should add that I have had an ASI done and I have a relatively flat cortisol curve - my cortisol is at the lower part, but within range in the morning, but at night it's actually a little bit above the range. It's just so frustrating as an outward healthy looking, athletically built 27 year old to have these issues. I have given up caffeine, gluten, extremely limited sugar and dairy, given up pornography, limited my alcohol consumptions, limited my exercise and I still suffer with these issues. If you can, someone please help. I'm on the verge of giving up and just letting the doctors work their chemistry on me.

Thank you.

### caroline — 2013-08-27T03:16:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-suffering-for-years-requesting-help.8052/post-93861>

Welcome codered5 ....That is quite a bucket list that you have there! Please read the easy start guides for the LeptinRX and for C/T. You need to start reading Dr. K's blogs ....and have you read his book? It will give you an overview of all of this.
There is a ton of reading - so keep at it and ask questions.

It would be great if you would start a journal - it helps to keep you committed and involved in this community. We can follow your progress and offer suggestions. It is a good idea to follow other journals and see what they are struggling with too. Have a look at Nathan's and Hemming 's and Sean's journals and connect with them.

The basic approach is ...... do the leptin reset - extremely important to reset your clock. Eat epi-paleo
pay attention to light cycles and limit your EMF exposure - hugely important.

start C/T and make sure you are outside grounding as much as possible. Do you live near an ocean?

Drink spring water

I am guessing that the most important thing for you is limiting your EMF exposure - please read Dr. K's EMF blogs

also read CT #4 and CT # 6

Also listen to Dr. K's podcasts - especially the last one with Bev Meyer - I think there is a new one now too.
The podcasts offer a ton of information so you can connect the dots a little quicker.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-27T03:50:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newbie-here-suffering-for-years-requesting-help.8052/post-93865>

Read the EMF Rx FAQ's [http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-rx-the-top-ten-emf-faqs/](http://www.jackkruse.com/emf-rx-the-top-ten-emf-faqs/)

# Thread: Fitbit? Jawbone?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MeghanK — 2013-08-27T03:05:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93860>

I wanted to purchase one of these devices to track sleep and activity etc.... but was worried about the EMFs... would something like this emit EMF?
[Fitbit](http://www.fitbit.com/one?gclid=CJ7P3uCpnLkCFfE-Mgod7wsAjQ)

[Jawbone](https://jawbone.com/up)

Anyone use either? Likes? Dislikes?
Worth the $100 price?

Thanks!

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-27T03:39:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93863>

Blue tooth transmition. I keep mine off.

### MeghanK — 2013-08-27T17:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93907>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=93863)
>
> Blue tooth transmition. I keep mine off.

What? I'm confused.....

### MeghanK — 2013-08-27T17:29:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93908>

> [Danco3636 said:](/goto/post?id=93863)
>
> Blue tooth transmition. I keep mine off.

What? I'm confused.....

### JeffMacDougall — 2013-08-27T17:49:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93910>

> [MeghanK said:](/goto/post?id=93908)
>
> What? I'm confused.....

These devices connect wirelessly to your phone via Bluetooth, a wireless technology standard for exchanging data over short distances (using short-wavelength radio transmissions in the ISM band from 2400–2480 MHz) from fixed and mobile devices. In other words... EMF. EMF in the vicinity of your brain. Might not be a good idea. Hence, Dan keeps his off, as do I.

### caroline — 2013-08-28T02:43:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-93978>

Is a Holter monitor \[to check heart rate\] a bad thing as well? My Doc wants me to wear one for a couple of days - just a precaution.... I keep putting it off...

### Nrelax11 — 2019-09-01T02:35:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-276934>

My parents bought my girlfriend one recently. I wish they would have told me they were going to, so I could have told them not to. When we visited them in June, she wore it around them because we felt bad lol. Was th only time she wore it.

### malc0088 — 2019-09-04T20:40:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fitbit-jawbone.8053/post-277147>

Trash for sure! I don't own one but see alot of people who wear them, and another concern with them is the color of light that is used to gather the biometric data via the wrist. A Lot of the ones i see are using a green light, though i have not measured the spectrum, looks like it might fall within the blue/green range that we should be mitigating. I measure HRV in the morning when i wake up and I use an old fashioned strap and my phone via bluetooth for 3 minutes.

# Thread: Is anyone up for a challenge???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2013-08-27T05:17:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/post-93876>

I would like to propose a challenge for all of us....

I think we all should do something silly and outrageous and out of character......something that we would have never dreamt of doing before we started this journey to wellness for our mind, body and spirit.
Something that was out of our realm of possibility before we got our head in the game......

What would be really outrageous and off the charts for you? What is something, for you, that would never have been a possibility before?

I think that we should really push ourselves to be fully engaged ...... something so outrageous that we can't believe it.

If you show me yours .... I will show you mine.....

I suppose that some here \[not naming names - but you know who you are\] "live large" anyway - so they would have nowhere to go with this ...but the rest of us need to catch up....

### caroline — 2013-08-28T02:33:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/post-93976>

I was taking to a guy not long ago that is friends with the guy that owns this parachute jumping thing. He says, on a good day, it is like raining money!

You have given me an idea tho - their is a man not far from here that owns an old small plane and he will take you up and do tricks ..... that doesn't sound quite right........

### fitness@home — 2013-08-28T03:01:11+0300

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T03:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/post-93994>

Sounds like Ivan has gotten into the medicinal sherry again...

### caroline — 2013-08-28T03:58:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/post-94000>

I just want to push my boundaries - live life to the fullest - not be worried about what others think .....make myself accountable for my life ...... no regrets....

I once had a dear friend who was in a bad car crash and taken to emergency - we all heard about it because he was a local lawyer and "bon vivant"

He showed up an hour later \[with an entourage in tow\] at our place for lunch - we had so much fun that afternoon. You just couldn't keep him down. He always lived life to the absolute fullest - he was always in motion - living his dream.

He once told me that he didn't want to be on his death bed and have regrets ...and he didn't. Sadly, he died at 55.
He gave himself the most amazing farewell party - there were hundreds of people there and lots and lots of beer.......

### nonchalant — 2013-08-28T04:07:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/post-94007>

I'm hip deep already in the challenge of leaving the Big City... Having to keep reassuring DH who is balking about all the unknowns.
I don't have all the answers either, but I'm confident that we can solve any problem we encounter.

### fitness@home — 2013-08-28T04:09:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/post-94009>

> [IvanDrago said:](/goto/post?id=93989)
>
> I have an even better idea. I think I have outdone myself this time.
>
> I am going to dress up as a leprechaun and hang out in the cereal aisle at the grocery store. If anybody tries to buy Lucky Charms I will verbally rip them to shreds under the guise of protecting my birth right. If they get real pushy with me, I'll drop the act and lay down some basic biochemistry regarding the obesity epidemic and diabetes. If it's a kid, I will ask them if they're really ready to embrace a future involving diabetes and broken dreams. Once you realize there is no pot of gold at the end of his rainbow, everything gets better. His cartoon cheerfulness is a nothing more than a cheap facade. A facade about as convincing as a carnival worker telling you that the beer bottle smash is a winnable game or the USDA food plate.
>
> If the staff tries to escort me out, I'll insist that General Mills sent me here as part of a quasi-promotional/public relations exercise because they felt bad for systematically ruining the health of our formerly healthy population. I mean, if the costume is believable enough and I can forge some decent documentation I am sure they will believe me. Worst case Ontario, I can just dish out free advice in a public place. Who wouldn't take a leprechaun seriously?!

Oh, my father would be so offended! As a former 30 year employee of General Mills, he always asks if I am supporting his retirement by purchasing their products. Has also been know to go snooping in my pantry for a look-see.

I haven't been supporting his retirement for a long time...

### SeaHorse — 2013-08-28T16:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-anyone-up-for-a-challenge.8054/post-94045>

Hey Caroline....I'm learning how to haul a horse trailer for the first time in a lifetime of owning horses. I want to be able to travel and take my horse to different workshops and clinics. Plus my dream is to do a long distance ride someday....a month or two on horseback in the wilderness somewhere. I want to learn to haul a trailer, be my own trucker and get over my history of bad experiences with horse trailering.

I just bought a trailer yesterday.....!

My other challenge is going to be a cold water swim in Newfoundland where we used to live. We were there for a holiday and I read in the paper about a former Newfoundlander who came back to the island and did a swim from St. Pierre (a French island off the coast) to Newfoundland. He is going to try and make an annual event and I want to do it!!

So by land and by sea I'm shaking up any chance I had of complacency....and it's a good thing too.

However, I do love editing and if you think it might be useful, I am very happy to take it up again. I think the whole EMF series is worth a compilation of its own.

# Thread: Tired after breakfast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2013-08-27T17:51:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93912>

This morning around 6:45 I had my BAB which was two grass-fed beef patties, three eggs fried in coconut oil, several pieces of bacon, and a few spoonfuls of coconut manna. I'm not hungry at all and my body feels good, I just feel really tired. Yesterday I had the same thing happen but it was in the afternoon and I was nearly dozing off at my desk. It almost hits me out of the blue, like I just ate a huge sugar-laden meal. Any ideas?

### cantweight — 2013-08-27T18:10:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93915>

It took my body a couple weeks to get use to low carb/high fat BABs. I believe it was my body lacking the ability to efficiently convert fat and protein to energy. I was a sugar burner, but denying the sugar gave my body no choice and it eventually caught on. Perhaps that is what you are seeing?

### RGP — 2013-08-27T20:10:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93925>

> [AnnieW said:](/goto/post?id=93912)
>
> This morning around 6:45 I had my BAB which was two grass-fed beef patties, three eggs fried in coconut oil, several pieces of bacon, and a few spoonfuls of coconut manna. I'm not hungry at all and my body feels good, I just feel really tired. Yesterday I had the same thing happen but it was in the afternoon and I was nearly dozing off at my desk. It almost hits me out of the blue, like I just ate a huge sugar-laden meal. Any ideas?

Your breakfast is extremely acidic and so your pH level may be low. Check your pH levels and make sure that your drinking water is slightly alkaline to help buffer the acidic meals. Worked wonders for me.

Could be you have a CBS SNP and your sulfur levels are too high. I have multiple CBS SNP's and I can't do eggs.

Just a couple of thoughts.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-27T22:44:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93944>

Thank you both so much for your replies. It wouldn't surprise me if it's my body just looking for sugar. I used to eat four breakfast biscuits and a large Coke from Chic-fil-a. I drink spring water so I'm not sure what the pH is.

RGP, what is CBS SNP? What can you do to raise the body's pH?

Thanks again!

### caroline — 2013-08-28T02:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-93980>

Hi Annie....I used to be really tired after BAB too. I upped the fat and lowered the protein. That really helped a lot.

### RGP — 2013-08-28T15:32:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-94026>

AnnieW:
CBS is the enzyme which catalyses the reaction which converts homocysteine into cystathionine (that's a mouthful). When one has a CBS SNP (single nucleotide polymorphism) or gene defect, the SNP can increase the enzyme activity (known as upregulation) of the CBS gene . If the gene is expressing or turned on (just because one has the defect doesn't mean its turned on), excess sulfur may increase CBS enzyme activity. A few causes may be high vitamin B6 supplementation, excess cortisol, excess sulfur in the diet. For more info about this, Google Dr. Amy Yasko's paper called "Methionine and Methylation: Chicken or the Egg".

You can obtain your gene information by doing a saliva test through 23andme.com ($99), then input your data for free at geneticgenie.com or for a small fee, go to mthfrsupport.com and receive a much more detailed report.

I follow the Epipaleo diet and I have active CBS SNP's. Therefore, I have to consume less meat/fish protein than I would like and watch the amount of sulfur obtained from food (meat protein, eggs, onions, garlic, etc).

I found that my spring water's pH level was around 5. So to raise my body's pH level, I raised the pH level of my drinking water to 7.5 - 8.0. I buffer my drinking water with magnesium bicarbonate (just Google to find recipe using seltzer water and milk of magnesia) or by mixing 2 parts sodium bicarbonate (baking soda) to 1 part potassium bicarbonate and measuring the pH level. I always measure the final pH because I don't want it too high. FYI: adding tea or coffee to your water will lower the pH, so the water one uses for these drinks will need to have a higher pH.

By only increasing the pH of my drinking water, my urine and saliva pH levels are in the ranges I want.

For more info on pH, check out the forum thread "The Iodine Thread". A poster named Grizz has provided alot of great info.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-28T18:41:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tired-after-breakfast.8059/post-94062>

This is very interesting. I have been in contact with Grizz as he is sharing some iodine info with me.

Thanks!

I've been following JK and the blog for almost a year and have started to see some improvements lately in sleep (50mg DHEA supplementation?) - but still no weightloss, nor much improved energy.
I live in rural France with very little EMFs in surroundings (edge of forest, end of line for power supply, nearest neighbour at 50m with no computer, leave alone wi-fi!). # Thread: Optimal Breathing?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-breathing.8063/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 37
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradTheBreather — 2013-08-27T19:32:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-breathing.8063/post-93923>

I'm posting this here because there isn't a breathing section of the forum (though I think it might deserve one).

I'm wondering if Jack has ever mentioned the importance of proper breathing not just during exercise but at all times, especially during sleep. I think its common in a spiritual/meditation context to see people saying to "pay attention to your breath" which I think is great, but we as westerners tend to breathe in a way that is not getting the most out of our lungs for our everyday lives.

From _The Science of Breath_ by Yogi Ramacharaka:

"The quality of the blood depends largely upon its proper oxygenation in the lungs, and if it is under-oxygenated it becomes poor in quality and laden with all sorts of impurities, and the system suffers from lack of nourishment, and often becomes actually poisoned by the waste products remaining uneliminated in the blood. As the entire body, every organ and every part, is dependent upon the blood for nourishment, impure blood must have a serious effect upon the entire system. The remedy is plain-practice the Yogi Complete Breath (44)"

"During inhalation, the diaphragm contracts and exerts a gentle pressure upon the liver, stomach and other organs, which in connection with the rhythm of the lungs, acts as a gentle massage of these organs and stimulates their action (46)"

Anyone who has spent some time in the cold tub knows how important it is to remember to breathe. Breathing correctly can help generate your internal heat and make the bath both more comfortable and effective.

Here is a brief summary how to breathe the Yogi Complete Breath from _The Science of Breath_
\-Always inhale and exhale through the nose
\-for the first 2/3 of the in-breath, the air should be filling the lower lungs, expanding the belly
\-for the last 1/3 of the in-breath, expand the chest, completely filling the lungs
\-for the out breath, smoothly release the air from your lungs, starting from the belly then the lungs
\-repeat

It sounds simple and that's because it is, yet very few westerners breathe from the belly with any regularity. I think its fair to guess that breathing in more air with every breath would lead to a healthier body (assuming the air is relatively clean). I am very new to the quantum biological perspective but I would love to hear the quantum effects of increased oxygen consumption or of the internal massage affect that comes from full breaths.

Whenever I evangelize about the importance of breath I always draw attention to our survival: We can go weeks without food, days without water, but only a few minutes without breath. Obviously its pretty important for our biology, and when you consider that just about everyone (in the west) at all times is breathing at less-than-optimal capacity, might breathing techniques deserve more attention from people who are concerned about their health?

### fitness@home — 2013-08-27T20:26:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-breathing.8063/post-93927>

Good points Brad. Will be interesting to see some feedback from others. If I am feeling stressed, I make it a point to take slow, deep breaths. It helps...

### Wurst23 — 2013-08-27T20:42:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-breathing.8063/post-93928>

You should have a look at the buteyko method - very interesting! loads of data as well

### BigPapaChakra777 — 2013-08-27T22:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-breathing.8063/post-93942>

I have to vouch for the Buteyko Method as well, it works! I've only been doing it for a couple weeks at most, and I haven't been doing all the 'exercises', but here's what I've been doing the last few days:

(1) Spending 40-60mins on the BreathSlim device (similar to the Frolov device) to enhance body oxygenation and increase CO2 - I immediately feel more calm and centered, slightly better focus, mood, motivation, etc. It becomes very easy to do diaphragmatic breathing after using this. I spread the sessions out over the course of the day, normally 20 minute sessions
(2) Only breathe through my nose, and I remind myself to (1) breathe from the belly, and (2) do reduced breathing - not trying to completely expand and fill my lungs, and I occasionally throw in a 'breath hold' after exhalations
(3) Do deep nasal breathing while exercising - it drastically increases the intensity of exercise and I feel 'better' after it, almost as if I get a much stronger endorphin rush

I've recently been using the BreathSlim to enhance meditation via the effects of CO2 on the body (you instantly become more centered and relaxed, and the device makes it effortless to both breath from the belly and be mindful of your breathing rhythm). I'm doing little experiments/bio-hacks to enhance my well-being using the device: meditate while using BreathSlim; Meditate while using BreathSlim AND binaural audio and visual entrainment via SquareEater; BreathSlim OR Elevation Training Mask while Buteyko Breathing in an epsom salt+lavender+sodium bicarbonate bath to greatly increase CO2. My CP raises significantly when I do this, so I know it's working, let alone the nice feeling I get after.

Somewhere on here I posted that I think air or breathing should be added to Dr. Kruse' 3 things for life (photoelectric effect, water, magnetism), since we are aerobic organisms after all. It's crazy that I've been an athlete since elementary school, done martial arts, etc. and that I didn't really know how to truly breathe. I highly recommend at least experimenting with Buteyko, and maybe getting the BreathSlim/Frolov device. You can make a DIY Frolov device as well, but I'm not too knowledgeable on that.

### Jack Kruse — 2023-05-03T05:33:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-breathing.8063/post-320792>

Climate scientists are just marketers who are looking for dollars from governments. Marketing is nothing more than applied psychology. It’s about connecting with people, persuading them, and, if good marketing helping them. Much of marketing is done to take advantage of people by separating them from their dollar by selling and incomplete story of the truth. This is why many people think marketing is a nasty or immoral thing. It is not when you have the proper context down. It is also something to keep in mind when evaluating someone' pitch to you. Marketing entrepreneurs are dopamine junkies that have found their calling. Just realize what is good for them may not be optimized for your biology.

### Alan Hall — 2023-05-04T18:13:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-breathing.8063/post-320859>

> [JoeBranca said:](/goto/post?id=320663)
>
> What is your feedback and results from Erwan's course? I'm putting it on my to do list for this year, but first I have to go get tortured in and survive the Taos mountains with some Movnat folks this coming week.

Hi Joe,
It was an excellent course. Erwan is so chilled and made time to ensure everyone understood the details.
We were all given work to do in our own time in between sessions and of course the effort determines the result.
Everyone at least doubled their breath hold time and some tripled theirs.

The live test on the last day was a great supportive atmosphere even though it was online.
I have found my breathing rate has somewhat reduced. I can very easily consciously reduce to two breaths per minute. My normal at rest rate is four breaths per minute.
I’d wholeheartedly recommend the course.
The experience of communicating with Erwan and getting his input is priceless.

I hope this helps.
I’m sure you’ll enjoy it.
Alan

# Thread: All the Labs You'd Ever Want to Do
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-the-labs-youd-ever-want-to-do.8064/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2013-08-27T21:04:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-the-labs-youd-ever-want-to-do.8064/post-93933>

Hi All,

Jack encouraged me to post my labs here and so that's what I'm doing. Because of my own N=1, I have gone the extra mile and done more testing than many might normally do. But I have a lot going on, so I thought the more the merrier.

You can see them as a PDF here: [http://sdrv.ms/14B2Jn9](http://sdrv.ms/14B2Jn9)

It's hard to format labs here on the forum, so I posted them to the cloud instead. I've highlighed in red the labs that are out of range, so hopefully that is clear. These may give you an idea of some of the corners in which you can shine your flashlights when trying to get ahold of your situations. In my labs you will see: multiple markers of inflammation, multiple infectious disease and parasite scans, hormone testing, full liver detox function testing, mitochondrial peformance, toxin exposures, neurotransmitter levels, comprehensive allergy scans, antibody production, immune system function, intracellular micronutrients, antioxidant levels, cancer scans, gut permeability, etc. etc. etc.

They are listed by date with the most recent first. So the 2 most recent sets are probably most relevant.

I'd be happy to field questions about the testing i've done and what I'm doing with all the results. I'd also love any feedback folks have, since I myself am a bit overwhelmed by the sheer number of things going wrong all at once. Sometimes the more you know, the harder it becomes to take the first step.

Some interesting findings you will see:
1\. My 23andMe profile showed that I have the SNP that makes me a slow metabolizer of caffeine. However, during the caffeine detox challenge on 6/28/2013, I was found to have increased NAT2 (N-Acetyltransferase) activity. This confounds me.
2\. I have salivary cortisol testing from both this summer and the summer of 2012. I went from making very little cortisol in 2012, to what seems to be an upside down pattern in 2013. Hmm.
3\. Jack talks about NFkB and IL-6 being labs that generally track together when they get worse. Not the case for me. My NFkB sucks for sure, but my IL-6 was normal. Although I don't know if normal equals optimal in this case.
4\. I showed no reactivity to wheat and gluten; and that was a HUGE surprise. However, I am reactive to diary apparently, which now explains how a small amount of liquid yogurt sent me into a GERD tailspin and nearly immediate inflammatory crisis with swollen lymph nodes and pain all over.
5\. Depsite poor liver detox function on 6/28/2013, prior testing on 4/12/2013 showed good glutathione levels. Would have expected glutathione to be worse.
6\. I have good CoQ10 levels, which I did not expect considering my problems.
7\. I was deficient in Vitamin C despite taking 1.5g daily prior to the test on 4/12/2013.
8\. Calcium and potassium were low.
9\. My ACTH stimulation test seems to prove Jack's point that adrenal fatigue is NOT a disease of the adrenals. My adrenals responded just fine, despite the fact that I was, at that very same time, making very little cortisol whatsoever.

There's tons in there. Enjoy looking through them if you like. All the best.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-28T01:17:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-the-labs-youd-ever-want-to-do.8064/post-93963>

The clue was the Calcium and Potassium........calcium efflux out of cells is what happens due to EMF......I think the NF kappa beta and IL-6 incongruency makes some sense......you are doing a lot to help your self but your environment is not allowing for a flip........The ACTH test also supports a leaky BBB and stress response thru the brainstem. C is low cause iodine (thyroid is still not optimal) I think BCM 7 and DPPIV maybe your version of celiac........my next blog is made for you.

### TheKid — 2013-08-28T06:27:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-the-labs-youd-ever-want-to-do.8064/post-94015>

One thing that gets talked up a lot amongst alt med practitioners is toxins. My testing shows I've got a lot of Formaldehyde and this Phthalate: [http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dioctyl\_phthalate](http://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dioctyl_phthalate).

That Phthalate by itself can cause insulin resistance, heart arrhythmia, and endocrine disruption, all of which I have problems with.

We don't talk as much about toxins here as other stuff. And I'm wondering if it might also be the Phthlates. Although I have no idea how to chelate a Phthalate. I suppose I might have to just get healthy and let my liver do the work.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-28T16:13:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-the-labs-youd-ever-want-to-do.8064/post-94035>

I am no fan of chelation........big fan of improving liver detox pathways.....but that is directly tied to your field and the PPP.......go back and read what the PPP three main functions are........you might re remember something tied to your issue.

My very skeptical husband believes that EMFs donâ€™t have any effect on us. He is a technology geek and loves all things electronic, wifi, bluetooth, etcâ€¦

I have the perfect place for this and it wonâ€™t get noticed at all with some strategic camouflaging. So I am going to observe both he and my stepson (another techno-geek) and will report back in a few weeks with any comments they may make regarding sleep, energy level, how they feel, etc. This is a _very_ unscientific experiment.

Think I am going to bring the unit into work with me during the day. It canâ€™t hurtâ€¦and it may help.

"This popular multipurpose unit neutralizes electromagnetic fields and geopathic stress in your home or office. For EMF protection, the Total Shield produces a 7.83 Hz field (the Earthâ€™s natural field, also called the Schumann Resonance), which blankets a 20,000-square-foot area (or more, depending on the model) and overcomes negative EMF fields. It protects you from all electrical and electronic devices, including computers, TVs, microwaves, even power lines outside your house. For geopathic stress, the unit detects and then amplifies the waves, reversing them by 180 degrees and retransmitting them. This counteracts the harmful waves, similar to how noise cancellation technology uses a low hum to cancel sounds.

You can use the Total Shield in one of three ways: EMF protection only, geopathic stress only or a combination of both. Colored lights blink at the Schumann resonance and can be used for stress relief or to help with biofeedback. Operates on AC adapter (included). The units are available in four coil strengths, with each additional coil adding 50% more intensity. Stronger units are appropriate for people who are very sensitive to EMFs; who have a large number of electronic devices in their home or office; or who live very close to power lines or stations. ### fitness@home — 2013-09-11T16:39:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-experiment-commences.8065/post-95613>

Total Shield (Unscientific Review)

It’s been a couple weeks and I have had a few PMs about my experiment with the Total Shield (TS). Going to post the Questions/Answers below.

I feel good about doing something to mitigate the EMF exposure. Placebo effect? I don’t know… My DH has not mentioned anything of note in our conversations (if you recall, he doesn’t yet know I have the unit). We are both sleeping well. He’s been falling asleep in his La-Z-Boy more frequently after I turn in for the night.

Question:
Fitness, I'm seriously considering the TotalShield for my parents. I know you were going to wait a couple weeks to see how your unit was working with your family, and it's probably too early for a favorable review... But is there anything you don't like about it yet? With Danco's experience, I'm ready to buy! But I'll probably just get a single coil. If they like it, they can buy one, or get a 2-coil one, and then I'll move the first unit to another relative's house. Sort of a try-before-you-buy thing.

_Answer__:_
_I have been taking the unit back and forth between home and work. No one realizes it is there because I hide it.

Here is what I noticed at home (and not sure that I like this result!). My 24-yr old stepson lives with us. He has been pretty much MIA since July. Not working, running around, only comes home most the time when we are not there...you get the picture. Irritates the heck out of me. We do not operate the zone for AC upstairs where his room is because I am not doubling our electric bill for someone that doesn't contribute to the household.

Well, the first week I used it at home my stepson was home every night except this past Friday and Saturday (holiday weekend). Slept downstairs on the sofa where it is cooler. Like I said...not sure I like this trend . The unit is about 30 ft from the sofa.

My sleep has been pretty good (still want a Magnetico though). My husband has not mentioned anything yet that gives me an indication of whether it is doing anything for him or not. I have the unit placed centrally in the house in our dining room. We have an old church bench in there that has some empty boxes on it. I placed an empty box over top of it. Sneaky, aren't I?_

_At work, I feel good knowing that I am doing something to counter the EMFs I am exposed to. My cube is in the middle between two other executive admins. The one on my right is VERY high strung. This week will be very busy because next week is the Quarterly Board meeting and she supports the Head Honcho of Finance. Her stress level always peaks the week before...she gets snappy, unhappy, and obviously stressed. I am going to pay attention to how she behaves this week and see if there is a difference.

BTW, I am going to purchase another unit in the future (single coil) to keep at work. If I ever change jobs it will move with me. We have wifi in this building and they just installed an antenna on the roof so employees would get better cell phone reception! At least it's not a high-rise like I used to work in...it is only four stories.

I hope this helps. When the unit is on my desk and hidden behind the file organizer, I place a glass container filled with water next to it. I am hoping it helps structure it...what do you think?_

### fitness@home — 2013-09-11T16:40:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-experiment-commences.8065/post-95614>

Question:
So, Dawn, was your stressful admin's week any better?

Answer:
_Funny you should ask... The last thing I said to her before leaving work was that I noticed things went much smoother for her this time. There were no meltdowns . I had a breeze of a week, as the person I support was on vacation last week. Let me rephrase that...I had a loooong week. Don't like not being busy.

Now, was it due to the TS? Who really knows. It may have been many other factors. This is such an unscientific "experiment". It is more like casual observation.

My hiding place for it at home was almost discovered! DH wanted to put some things on the church bench and if he had moved the box the TS would have been found._

Response:
No meltdowns sounds like a relief. At the very least, however, you know that it didn't agitate anyone. And there is a good possibility that it did help. I'm going to take the plunge and order one. ### nonchalant — 2013-09-23T17:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-experiment-commences.8065/post-95823>

Actually, I'm going to return my Total Shield. I put it in the center of my house, but it didn't help DH sleep. I tried various settings with no improvement. So I moved it to the bathroom next to our bedroom, and just left it on the Schumann frequency. He still didn't sleep well (waking up at 3-4 am). My sleep during this time was also disrupted a bit. Then my next ice bath, in that same bathroom, was unusually brief, because I got cold in only 20 minutes. It actually took me a few hours to connect the problem with the TS. So then I turned the unit off during the day, and used it only at night. That next morning, when I went into the bathroom, I ... how do I say this... I "resonated" with the resonator. I felt like my entire body was quivering in time to that red blinking light. It was interesting. Not a positive or a negative feeling. I unplugged the unit and I continued to vibrate for a minute or so.

My next ice bath was back to normal, and I could stay in the entire hour. My sleep is better now, with just a brief wakening toward the morning. DH's sleep still isn't great, unless I manage to sneak some boron into his supper.

I suspect that the TS might be great for others, especially if they do not ground themselves, have a Magnetico, drink good water, or respect the light cycles. I planned to test this unit a while and then take it over to my parents, but without a great response in myself, I hesitate to ask others to try it. I don't want to lose what credibility I've managed to build this last year. I still would like to try a blind test on others, because I think it could really help people insulated from the environment.

# Thread: Look where AHS and paleo are headed.......surprise, surprise.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-where-ahs-and-paleo-are-headed-surprise-surprise.8075/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-28T05:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/look-where-ahs-and-paleo-are-headed-surprise-surprise.8075/post-94014>

A quote from an AHS 2013 review: "Jeff Rothschild gave a nice summary of the research on time-restricted feeding, body weight and health in animal models and humans. Research in this area is expanding and the results are pretty interesting, suggesting that when you restrict a rodent's feeding window to the time of day when it would naturally consume food (rather than giving constant access during both day and night), it becomes more resistant to obesity even when exposed to a fattening diet. Rothschild tied this concept together with circadian regulation in a compelling way. Since food is one of the stimuli that sets the circadian clock, Rothschild proposes to eat when the sun is up, and not when it's down, synchronizing eating behavior with the natural seasonal light rhythm. I think it's a great idea, although it wouldn't be practical for me to implement it currently. Maybe someday if I have a more flexible schedule. Rothschild is about to publish a review paper on this topic as part of his master's degree training, so keep your eyes peeled."

Where there is faith in the future there is hope in the present. Hope enables us to endure the hardships of life with courage and contentment. The secret of fulfilling our potential is in acknowledging my limitations and believing that time is a gift, not a threat. Hope is the key that unlocks the door of discouragement. The beauty of hope lies in knowing that our life will improve when we share our light with others. The journey becomes richer, the understanding becomes truth, the dreams become realities and today can be altered forever. Make this Aug 28th your day to dream. Become a leader who deals in hope for your clan.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-29T16:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-crazy.8077/post-94164>

Take it from someone who screwed around. I lost four pounds in three days and then started sneaking little things here and there. Then I ended up going back to Chic-fil-a for my 2000 calorie carb and sugar breakfast. It's a slippery slope! I've been 100% dedicated this week and I feel amazing. You really have to concentrate on having your huge breakfast in the morning. I have always been a big breakfast person so this wasn't difficult for me. Eat fat if you are trying to slay the sugar dragon. I haven't had any sugar cravings at all this week which is incredible. And I've been faced with bad stuff all this week. I have a bag of toffee chips at home that I haven't touched. Trust me, for someone who used to eat sugar non-stop during the day and drink several Dr. Peppers, this is a big deal. Fat is crucial. Breakfast is crucial. If you have a big enough breakfast, your sugar cravings will go away and you won't feel the need to snack. I've never been much for night eating but having a good dinner that's high in protein really helps. I ate two pounds of scallops last night for dinner and totally forgot to eat the vegetables I made with them.

# Thread: Betaine HCL, no burning what so ever
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### seanb4 — 2013-08-28T18:51:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-94071>

Hi guys,

So i've had stomach problems for a while and have tried various things to fix them amongst which was betaine HCL. It is supposed to increase acid levels in your stomach.
You are meant to take an extra pill each meal and stop when you feel a burning sensation as this means you have gone too far.

Now when I was doing my experiment with this I took up to 4x the maximum recommended dose and felt no burning whatsoever, all it did was cause the food to go back up my oesophagus as if I was going to be sick, but I never was.

I think figuring out why I never experience burning with HCL when everyone else seems to could be really helpful in figuring out what is wrong with my stomach.

Is is that all the acid is being neutralised in my stomach?
Is my stomach acid really high all the time so my stomach wall produces excess protective mucus?

Also why do I often get reflux (food coming back up throat) but no burning whatsoever?

Thanks, Sean.

### seanb4 — 2013-08-28T19:08:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-94078>

Also could there be bacteria in my stomach that are maybe neutralising my stomach acid or even ones that can survive there (h pylori came back negative).

Would any of this stuff explain bloating after drinking just water? Like maybe bacteria are drinking the water and somehow this makes me bloat?

### seanb4 — 2013-09-08T08:52:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-95205>

Bump. Anyone got any idea's?

### caroline — 2013-09-08T11:38:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-95216>

Sorry - I don't....but it seems like some sound reasoning tho.
On Dave Asprey's site ....he is offering a coconut Charcoal - have you seen that?

### cantweight — 2013-09-08T16:43:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-95228>

The things that come to my mind are candida, parasites, and SIBO. My youngest has issues with bloating. Water on an empty stomach makes him feel sick. When treated with nystatin he is much better. Have not yet been able to control this crap w/o meds yet......a catch 22 for sure.

There are natural parasite cleanses. Turmeric and wormwood are helpful. If using be sure to take a binder. I like Welchol but it is a prescription. Activated charcoal, chlorella, betonite clay are other options.

Add raw milk and fermented foods to repopulate good bacteria.

That all I can think of....
