### seanb4 — 2013-09-08T20:18:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-95249>

Maybe my stomach acid is constantly high so my body is desensitised to it?

It is weird because every time I get reflux, which is quite regular, it doesn't burn whatsoever. This seems to suggest that the liquefied contents of my stomach are not acidic.

But if this was true I assume I would have gastroparesis which would have shown up on my barrium swallow test. Maybe it did't show up because my barium meal was just carbs and these don't need stomach acid to digest. But if all this was true, why do I still bloat from water and carbs? Also the betaine would have helped somewhat surely and it didn't.

Yeah I'm very confused...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-09-08T20:43:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-95251>

gave you the answer in the last few blogs......when you are inundated with EMF your IMLCC inactivates your esophageal plexus because it opens up you calcium voltage channels in these nerves and in your vagus nerve.......your vagus nerve innervates the glands that help make intrinsic factor that binds B12. It also helps release pepsin and acid in the stomach......this is the cause of GERD and LE reflex and Barrett's esophagus. Game set match.

You might be interested to here people in surgery are starting to realize that the new obsity surgery that uses a magnet to close off the stomach inside of suturing it off actually helps GERD big time.......I wonder how long it is going to take them to understand how electro magnetism can help as well as hurt biology.

### seanb4 — 2013-09-10T21:10:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/betaine-hcl-no-burning-what-so-ever.8078/post-95472>

I also agree with what you just said, I think Jack is a great guy with real noble goals, he does the best he can with what little information we give him and replies to almost every thread. He does this for free as he wants to change the world for a better place using the in-depth knowledge he has gained over the years and I fully respect this.

However in this instance I don't think he is hitting the nail on its head. This is because he is going only on the small amount of information I have gave him, with no labs to back it up.

I say this because in dissecting the post it appears some things are incorrect.

"when you are inundated with EMF your IMLCC inactivates your esophageal plexus because it opens up you calcium voltage channels in these nerves and in your vagus nerve"
This suggests EMF is to blame for my stomach issues/bad health however all my problems came about after I got a stomach ulcer. In this time no noticable changes in EMF occured. Plus it wasn't a slow gradual decay of my health. I was really healthy then suddenly got stomach problems. EMF would surely have a more gradual effect.

"your vagus nerve innervates the glands that help make intrinsic factor that binds B12. It also helps release pepsin and acid in the stomach"
Okay so calcium voltage channels are open and causing me to release more pepsin and acid in the stomach. If this is true then why did various anti-acid medication not make a difference? Surely they would have neutralized the excess and my bloating etc would have been reduced?

"this is the cause of GERD and LE reflex and Barrett's esophagus"
I don't have many of the main symptoms of these diseases. I have no heartburn, and no burning reflux.
If my oesophagus was inactive letting up all that extra acid and pepsin then why do I feel no burning?

The information he has put out there is probably spot on for people with those problems however I don't think it tackles my health problems.

### caroline — 2013-09-11T00:38:29+0300

### Mally — 2013-08-28T21:17:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cycloset-administered-vaginally.8080/post-94090>

I've been taking Cycloset for about a month now at .8mg every morning. The side effects have been pretty hard - increased fatigue, headaches and dizziness and my already low diastolic BP has dropped even more.

My prolactin level started at 37 and when I retested recently, it had only dropped to 34. Clearly I need to up the Cycloset dose but I am nervous considering how bad .8 mg makes me feel.

Someone pointed out this article indicating that regular bromocriptine 2.5 mg can be administered vaginally with similar effects and fewer sides. Does anyone know if this might work for Cycloset as well?

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/1502929](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/1502929)

Thanks!

Ever since Jack first began sharing his insight with us about EMF I have believed it, but I donâ€™t think I realized how much of an impact it has on ME.

I now absolutely believe that it is a major player, if not the root cause, in all of my issues: food intolerances, celiac disease, Raynaudâ€™s disease, ME/CFS, etc. etc. Itâ€™s enlightening and frightening at the same time to realize that something I cannot taste, touch, see, hear, smell or feel can have such a profound effect on my health. Itâ€™s frustrating to know that I need to read this forum and Jackâ€™s blogs to further my understanding and knowledge of how to heal myself and protect my family, but doing so also causes my health to deteriorate. I have become much more sensitive to EMF now to the point where my hand tingles and gets ice cold after typing for only a few minutes.

I took two vacations this summer where I was hardly exposed to any wifi or other major forms of EMF and I felt fantastic. One vacation was at the beach where I spent hours every day in the ocean (even in the rain!) and the second vacation was in the middle of Olympic National Park in WA â€“ nothing but gorgeous mountains and a beautiful rainforest.

Iâ€™m trying to formulate a plan for how I can make this happen permanently, but I always come back to the question: how does one function in this world without technology?! Seems almost impossible! We are cutting wifi at home and have some other plans in the works, but Iâ€™d love to figure out a way to completely do without it all!

Anyway, just wanted to pop back in and say â€œhi!â€ and hopefully nudge those of you who arenâ€™t convinced yet of the EMF connection to really do some personal experimentation. If you canâ€™t heal and you feel like youâ€™re doing everything right (like I did), the answer has to lie in your surroundings. This is now plain as day to me!
Iâ€™m going to try to become a bit more active on the forum again as Iâ€™m finding I need the community and some help answering questions I come up with as I continue to heal, but my time will definitely be very limited.

### cantweight — 2013-08-28T22:46:56+0300

I had a very similar experience this summer. Cut way back on my computer time in June/July and when I got back on my fingertips burned when I typed...Its been a few weeks now that I have had to be back online often for my college courses and I have noticed the burning and tingling is gone. Not a good sign. I want that reminder and my guess is the damage is irritating something else I dont feel.

I am trying to solve the same problem...how to find an acceptable balance. I am trying to print out as much as I can and no longer order ebooks for my classes. I'd rather spend an extra 100 bucks on a hard copy I can sit in the sun and read with my feet in the grass.

Love to hear what solutions you come up with and so glad to hear that you are healing

### Abdrx — 2013-08-31T23:45:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/using-what-i%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2ve-learned-to-finally-heal.8081/post-94513>

Hi All, I am new to posting on the forum and to reading much of it. I just listened to Jack's podcast on how diet and exercise alone will not fix us. I've been following him for couple of years but realize I've made a few mistakes along the way. BJK77, you expressed exactly what I was thinking about as my first post on the forum. Thanks for doing such a great job. The mistake I feel I've made is getting sidetracked on FB and devoting too much time to certain groups, which do relate to me. However, I realize my time might have been better spent just staying up with Jack's blogs and this forum. I absolutely love his podcasts, especially now, I think it's b/c hearing the "almost" final thoughts will help me to go back and start at the beginning of some of the series, understanding it all much better.

I am also challenged to limit my computer exposure AND to get all this info, which has to come by way of technology. I really don't want to create more paper in my life either and was sitting outside, grounding, while reading on the computer via my in-house wifi. I'm not sure if I'm accomplishing anything at all doing it that way? So now I think I will copy the different series into Word and at least unplug the wifi and either read inside or outside with my computer for now.

I've debated whether I should buy a grounding mat while on the computer and also wondered about the Magnetico Pad. My husband wanted to buy me a rather expensive purse, in the price range of the Magnetico, and I thought why in the world spend money on that when I could get the pad instead! I haven't done any checking of outlets inside and we don't live in an urban setting, only have 1 other wifi option showing up near our home, am surrounded by much land and a golf course behind our house, so only 3 homes to our left.

Does anyone know if there is some precaution to do first before purchasing a Magnetico pad? Anyone have one and can testify to it's benefit?

I am 62, in pretty good shape but know there's room for improvement, almost near end of working on my MTHFR issues and feeling better than I have in about 8 years, since Hurricane Katrina, where we lost it all (long story, family being relocated all over). Life is beautiful, Jack has helped me realize just how lucky I am to be living where I am today, and learning to enjoy nature, grounding, CT (cold baths, adding ice, about 30 minutes, 2-3x/week), good diet, epi-paleo but need to learn a bit more there, long time practioner of mind/body/spirit. I guess that pretty much covers me, retired now from medical technology of 40 years so all Jack says is of immense interest to me and makes so much sense. Thank God for people who are bright, think outside the box, and willing to risk criticism yet share it to make people's lives better. Those who want to do the work, that is! Thanks for reading my post and in advance for any help!

[http://12160.info/m/discussion?id=2649739:Topic:1536735](http://12160.info/m/discussion?id=2649739%3ATopic%3A1536735)

Because the Amish are living in in a mostly 1915 environment, in the article I just posted ^^^, I don't think the 'lack of vaccines' are why the Amish are benefitting. From my understanding, it's a person with a low Redox will be aggrivated by the vaccine. My hypothesizing that vaccines in low-redox individuals results in a great potential for calcium efflux. As a potential, preventative measure for a low-redox person that desires vaccination, what if that person increases the dietary taurine (like a lot of egg, fish, meats) a week before and on the day of vaccine to help block the NMDA receptor of direct-injected glutamate into the blood system? Ibuprofin is also a glutamate-blocker.

(As a side-note, injecting vaccines into babies right out of the womb would also be problematic too, because the liver doesn't produce taurine until about 6-9 months alter from what I've read... thinking here that glutamate's impact on the neurological system is going to be much stronger as there is less buffering. Just like in cats that never produce taurine and rely on the diet for it, babies would rely on taurine in mother's milk for it).

I suggest this because taurine will compete with glutamate and be more present to bind to the NMDA receptor sites (thereby preventing the chain reaction that leads to calcium efflux). The reasoning why I'm suggesting this is that gelatin is the base of nearly all vaccines and the vaccines that appear to be the most problematic have MSG added to them directly as the adjuvant. So it's pointing to a quick, unbuffered glutamate-overload into the cardiovascular system as a potential culprit. Making this basis off of the Alzheimer drugs manufactured a few years back were all glutamate-blockers.

This all makes me curious about the redox of the typical Amish person. The Amish are living in the morning light, are grounded, not getting blue-light in the evening and without exposure to magnetic and electrical EMF. However, I think we're bleeding over into their communities with RF-EMF now.

Hi all. I am a 45 yr old male, 6' tall, 265 lbs, with an office job. Just got my first set of labs done and reviewed with my ND. ND recommends Androgel for low T, and a Leptin reset diet. I don't understand most of this.

I appreciate any help in understanding this.

Sodium 140 mmol/L (reference range 135-145)
Potassium 3.9 mmol/L (3.5 - 5.0)
Chloride 105 mmol/L (99-109)
CO2 26 mmol/L (22-31)

BUN/CRE Ratio 19 (7-24)

T Bilirubin 0.7 mg/dL (.1-1.5)
sgot-ast 18 Unit/L (10-45)
Alk Phos 45 IU/L (35 - 115)

Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy 57 ng/mL (20-50)

Total LDL 138 (<130)

Real LDL Size Pattern "B" (marked as abnormal?) (ref range "a")
Remnant Lipo 32 (IDL+VLDL3) (0)
HDL 2 12 (>10)
HDL 3 41 (>30)
VLDL 3 15 (<10)
LDL 4 19.6
LDL 3 61.9
LDL 2 15
LDL 1 18.8
Total APO B100 119 (<109)
APO A1 159 (>118)
APO B100-A1 Ratio 0.75 (<.92)

T Uptake 43.5 (27.5 - 42.8)
T4 (Thyroxine) 6.9 mcg/dL (4.7 - 11.3)

TSH 3.192 uIU/mL (.450 - 5.100)

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-08-29T00:08:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-time-labs-what-do-you-think.8083/post-94108>

Jim, welcome to the forum. Your cholesterol numbers are fairly concerning. Triglycerides @ 227 is very high, and pattern "A" lipids are unhealthy. This combined w/ your weight indicates your diet really needs to be cleaned up ... leptin reset and eating epipaleo would be a big help.

Not sure of your lab ranges, but your testosterone appears very low. Read the "Hormones 101" and "Hormones 102" blogs for more info. All hormones start w/ cholesterol ... and right now your lipids are not healthy. There are many things you can do BEFORE considering going on T therapy.

### Ken23 — 2013-08-29T07:56:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/26-with-bipolar-disorder.8088/post-94138>

Hi, Im new to the forum. Im 26 years old and have been suffering from the affects of bipolar disorder for many years. I have done much searching on the internet for a solution to my problem without using prescription medications, as I have tried them over and over with not much success. I came here hoping I could possibly receive some good advice/information on the steps I should take to get my life back in order because it has really disrupted things for a long time. Other than just eating the epi paleo diet, are there certain labs or a specific protocal I should begin with? Any advice from someone who has experience with this would be greatly appreciated. Thank you

### caroline — 2013-08-29T10:45:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/26-with-bipolar-disorder.8088/post-94139>

Welcome Ken to this forum! Please go to the home page and read the easy start guides. Also, go thru Dr. Kruse's blog index and start by reading what pertains to you. There is a section on labs too.

Also, please listen to Dr. K's podcasts - they help alot to get information quickly. Have you read Dr. K's book? That will give you a great overview of all of this and he also talks about what labs you need. It is available on Amazon in paper or kindle. His book is a great place to start actually. THE EPI -PALEO RX ....the prescription for Disease reversal and Optimal Health.

There is a ton of stuff to get thru here .....just keep reading and re reading and asking questions .... and, don't forget, when you are reading a blog .... make sure to read all the comments - there is gold in the comments!

You are definitely in the right place ...this is a journey of a lifetime to optimal health .....you do need to be 100% committed and you can change your life. Lots and Lots of us here have done exactly that.

Please start a journal so we can follow along and help. It helps if you read other's journals as well. You never know when some one else says something that rings a bell for you .....and also you can help others...

Please have a look at Hemming's journal and Nathan's journal and Sean's journal and there are lots of others that will help as well. Have a look at the journal of Mr. Pinkies ..... she has an awesome post today of her and her family's successes this past year.

I don't know much about bi-polar disorder but someone else will have some ideas I am sure.

The webinars and Q&As are really, really important if you can manage that. Today's live Q&A was over 90 minutes with an unbelievable amount of information and you can ask questions......

### cantweight — 2013-08-29T14:03:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/26-with-bipolar-disorder.8088/post-94150>

I was diagnosed with bi-polar disorder in my 20's and was heavily medicated for years with little to no relief. Prescriptions kept getting stronger and symptoms kept getting worse.

I went off my medications all 3 times I was pregnant and found myself feeling better than ever....big clue.

I would strongly suggest you read about LABS. Have blood work done, see what is going on in your body. Check for active infections and get a good baseline on your hormones as both of these can be massively disruptive.

Read all the brain/gut blogs, EMF series, and start an epi-paleo diet. Incorporate CT as well and you are on your way. Be mindful of detox symptoms as they can be rough to navigate and will be better or worse depending on what kind of diet you are coming from. But they are also short lived and well worth pushing through.

I have had no bi-polar issues for 4 years now after healing my body and cleaning my diet. It is shocking and awesome and bullshit that nobody tells you its this simple. There are psychiatrists using these methods now with great success, but they are few and far between and completely unappreciated.

I wish you the best...this will work

### fitness@home — 2013-08-30T14:41:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/26-with-bipolar-disorder.8088/post-94335>

Thanks for starting this thread. My 30 year old son was diagnosed bipolar 2 last year. The cycles started in his early 20s and I knew far before he finally went for help what it was. They started when he was in the Air Force (Networking & Communications).

He only stayed on meds for three months. I keep waiting for another phone call from him in a downward cycle.

How do you get an adult child to listen to you?! He is not gluten free, worked in IT for years, and is a night owl. Cannot hold a job longer than a year before a cycle comes along and throws him off track.

My son is so intelligent (IQ) but still cannot see that it is his own choices that shape him.

Breaks my heart...

### Ken23 — 2013-09-07T23:09:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/26-with-bipolar-disorder.8088/post-95160>

I thought I'd post an update as a few people messaged me saying they were going to follow the thread.

I am now a little over a week into eating the epi paleo diet, doing the Leptin reset and one ct session. I've noticed a lot of positives.

My mood is much better. More focused and upbeat, less anxiety.
Sleep has improved. I wake up pretty easily, feeling well rested.
Only feel the need to eat breakfast and dinner.
Lost about 5lbs

Skin has been more oily, not sure if this is typical or will eventually go away
Perspiration has radically switched, which is quite strange because I used to sweat a lot during workouts. I went for a few sprints the other day in 80 degree weather and barely broke a sweat. I was always under the impression that sweating was healthy, supposed to cool your body. I do feel my body is overheating at times even though I'm drinking a ton of water. Any thoughts?

Also wondering if I should add any supplements from Dr. K's list to help with the oily skin and just for overrall purposes. Maybe some K2 coq10

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-06T03:56:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/26-with-bipolar-disorder.8088/post-96981>

Mitochondrial superoxide dismutase is the bigger target for fat laden diets. This is the place where a ketogenic diet acts upon histone deactelyases that alter epigenetic signaling in DNA and RNA expression. People do not understand how food and the electromagnetic field impacts the epigenetic expression of RNA and DNA. It does it by adding or extracting energy around the double helix structure of the protein to alter its structure. If the structure is more open then gene expression occurs. If it is more closed down by the binding of methyl groups of histones the gene wont be expressed. This is how epigenetics acts in all life. Anything that acts an HDAC inhibitor in our cells, blocks mTOR signaling by increasing the process of autophagy. Sleep has the exact same effect. Autophagy occurs maximally in humans during sleep when we are regenerating. Ketosis blocks mTOR signaling and that makes it the key target in human mitochondria.

When you target cytochrome one you are going to get metabolic syndrome because the heme proteins in this cytochrome can no longer tunnel electrons in ECT. This is why constant carbs suck for modern humans. They help in certain environmental conditions but these are not conditions modern humans face any longer because of the technology we embrace like fools. You lose hair from carbs and from higher EMF fields.

### Josh — 2014-06-01T05:53:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/26-with-bipolar-disorder.8088/post-129135>

[http://brain.oxfordjournals.org/content/126/3/685.full](http://brain.oxfordjournals.org/content/126/3/685.full)

**Summary**
We previously have described a group of patients with gluten sensitivity presenting with ataxia (gluten ataxia) and suggested that this disease entity may account for a large number of patients with sporadic idiopathic ataxia. We have therefore investigated the prevalence of gluten sensitivity amongst a large cohort of patients with sporadic and familial ataxia and looked at possible genetic predisposition to gluten sensitivity amongst these groups. Two hundred and twenty‐four patients with various causes of ataxia from North Trent (59 familial and/or positive testing for spinocerebellar ataxias 1, 2, 3, 6 and 7, and Friedreich’s ataxia, 132 sporadic idiopathic and 33 clinically probable cerebellar variant of multiple system atrophy MSA‐C) and 44 patients with sporadic idiopathic ataxia from The Institute of Neurology, London, were screened for the presence of antigliadin antibodies. A total of 1200 volunteers were screened as normal controls. The prevalence of antigliadin antibodies in the familial group was eight out of 59 (14%), 54 out of 132 (41%) in the sporadic idiopathic group, five out of 33 (15%) in the MSA‐C group and 149 out of 1200 (12%) in the normal controls. The prevalence in the sporadic idiopathic group from London was 14 out of 44 (32%). The difference in prevalence between the idiopathic sporadic groups and the other groups was highly significant (_P_ < 0.0001 and _P_ < 0.003, respectively). The clinical characteristics of 68 patients with gluten ataxia were as follows: the mean age at onset of the ataxia was 48 years (range 14–81 years) with a mean duration of the ataxia of 9.7 years (range 1–40 years). Ocular signs were observed in 84% and dysarthria in 66%. Upper limb ataxia was evident in 75%, lower limb ataxia in 90% and gait ataxia in 100% of patients. Gastrointestinal symptoms were present in only 13%. MRI revealed atrophy of the cerebellum in 79% and white matter hyperintensities in 19%. Forty‐five percent of patients had neurophysiological evidence of a sensorimotor axonal neuropathy. **_Gluten‐sensitive enteropathy was found in 24%. HLA DQ2 was present in 72% of patients. Gluten ataxia is therefore the single most common cause of sporadic idiopathic ataxia. Antigliadin antibody testing is essential at first presentation of patients with sporadic ataxia._**

Ken,

Well long story short I have never had psychiatric problems my whole life- been a very stable happy dude. I was given ativan for 2 weeks to counter some thyroid meds and the ativan completely ruined my life. I'm still recovering from it now, 2 years later. I had over 70 symptoms, 60 of them psychiatric. Things such as 20 hour long panic attacks, raging anxiety and depression, depersonalization, cognitive loss etc.. I almost took my life a few times because the anxiety was so high and relentless for over a year straight.
I then got encephalitis and since then have had tons more psych problems- psychosis, dementia, emotional instability, depersonalization, severe memory loss, unable to feel time.... crazy stuff like that.
I know it's all my brain though, not my mind. My mind is in tact- IM still there, just trapped inside this broken, malfunctioning brain. I have physical problems as well but the mental stuff is way worse. All my psychiatric problems are of physical nature though. So heal my brain and I know I'll be me again. I have no doubt you will be the same.

# Thread: Dealing with unsupportive family members
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AnnieW — 2013-08-29T16:31:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94159>

So last night I tried to talk to my husband about the dangers of EMF. I had a feeling that he wasn't ready to hear that information but I tried anyway. He got pissed at me and said I was going nuts. He said he talked to a friend at work about my diet and she told him that it was "very unhealthy" and that everyone she knew who did "this kind of diet" got grumpy and "went crazy." I'm assuming she was talking about the Atkin's diet. She also told him that he needed to start running, so I guess now he is taking all of his health advice from her.

He is very involved in IT and wants to turn a room in our home into a lab for all of these switches and computers. I simply asked him if he could turn it off at night and he ripped my head off. He said I was "going crazy" just like his friend at work said.

I'd like to give CT a try but I can't imagine hauling bags of ice into my house and hearing his reaction to that. For those who have gone through this, how did you deal?

### nonchalant — 2013-08-29T16:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94162>

Hi Annie. My sympathies.
I would ask him to at least go wireless. If he is running some compilations or whatever at night, he won't want to turn the computer or network off, but wireless shouldn't be a problem.

My husband was disturbed by the ice baths also, until he noticed the favorable affect on my hormones. He became VERY supportive of my CT, even asking me if I had remembered to do it. He doesn't understand it, and could really care less. Just Do It!!! Lol.

I've found the less I say about EMF the better. I just mitigate as I can, and work for the day we leave town.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-08-29T17:25:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94170>

I've found the less I say about EMF the better. I just mitigate as I can, and work for the day we leave town. This is the best policy with your situation............but I would have the electrician install a kill switch for night time for you.....he does not need to know and you might consider the magnetico because this can give you both protection and he will not even know its on the bed.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-29T17:55:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94185>

We live in a town house right now and the room where he has all this equipment is across the hall from our bedroom. We do have a wireless router and there isn't any PC equipment in the bedroom. I am still considering a magnetico and trying to sneak it in when he's not home.

Thanks guys for the support and great suggestions.

### johnnyb — 2013-08-29T18:34:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94197>

I can get her to the beach, "but the water is too cold" etc. I can keep house lights off around me off at night, but only I will wear blue blockers. We did reposition furniture to avoid an unfortunate magnetic field in the back yard. We plan to get a sleep pad in October, but there are so many things only a person can do for someone else.

### Terminator — 2013-08-29T20:11:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94218>

I used to get very bad verbal tretment from my brother. Well, not anymore. Smart meter got installed on his home and now his kids feel the effects of emf with headaches. Now he is asking me to buy him a cover to reduce signal strength.

I have been subject of jockes at work many times, well tables are turning sloooowly.

I suggest to sparingly talk about symptoms caused by EMF's. This is my stategy. Almost everyone has syptoms especially from wireless crap. In time they will realize that these are not myths.

### vkiernan — 2013-08-29T20:34:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94222>

All in good time they will know you are not crazy!!!

Btw, haul the ice in if that's what you need. He will get over it....

### Danco3636 — 2013-08-29T20:45:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dealing-with-unsupportive-family-members.8090/post-94225>

Do what you do for you! We have one chance in this life, make it yours..... My wife isn't "into" what I do but she is ok with me doing what I do.... And I can tell you she is slowly embracing it as well as she see's the benefits..... She wants glowing skin like me.... She wants bountiful energy like me.... She wants to sleep like a rock star like me..... It goes on.... They understand but changing their paradigm will hit resistance..... How strong can you be? Embrace this change with love and caring though..... Compassion in your eyes and caring in your heart.
I don't talk about what I do.... I kind of keep it under wraps (except on my FB page, lol) but when asked I will explain. Those that want to hear will listen. Those that don't will go away.... perhaps grumble or smirk a bit but I really don't care. I let my results speak for me....
Live by example..... Do what "you" need to do.

Wishing you great health!

### nicld — 2013-08-29T21:39:05+0300

My hubby thinks I am a bit crazy too but after 24 years together he just accepts it. He thinks it is a bit nuts about the CT but I have been dealing with health/weight issues for so long, nothing surprises him anymore. Do what you need to do to make yourself healthy. I always joke with my hubby that when we get old and he needs taken care of becasue he did not take care of himself, he's going in a home.....hahaha.

### AnnieW — 2013-08-29T21:40:46+0300

I was really irritated that his work friend tried to critique what I was doing when she is severely underweight and is struggling with conceiving her second child. She told me the other day (before I was on the reset) that humans eat way too much meat and it's bad for the environment and she's considering being a vegetarian. I want to be optimal for me. What really irritates me is the fact that my husband is very unhappy with my health because I am so overweight, and he wants to have a baby, but he's giving me crap for doing what I KNOW is the best thing.
